title: AI & the Brain: As We Embrace AI, Let’s Not Forget Our Minds
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount I-II
description: Across homes, offices, and classrooms, AI is becoming ubiquitous—mapping our routes, writing our code, even dreaming up art in seconds. What once sounded like science fiction is now silently transforming our daily existence. And yet, amid this great digital leap forward, a quieter, more fragile system is at stake: the human brain.We are perched at a precipice in history. As AI systems grow in power and presence, we must ask a question too few dare to voice: What is this doing to us - our capacity to explore, to learn, to reason, and to create? The machines are learning. But are we forgetting?
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: AI’s Rise, Talent’s Fall: Fixing the Skills Deficit
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom B
description: Everyone’s talking about AI disruption — but not enough are talking about the human talent disruption it demands. This candid session challenges the myth that AI alone will future-proof workforces. It exposes blind spots in today’s upskilling agendas — from overlooked durable skills to the illusion of instant AI fluency. Backed by global insights and fresh data, this session offers a bold roadmap to turn AI anxiety into human potential — where curiosity, creativity, and adaptability matter as much as code.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Building a New, Future-Proof University in 2026
description: What happens when an academic's vision for transforming higher education meets building an actual university? This session explores the journey from Alternative Universities' theoretical framework to building a new university in 2026 that is debt-free and apprenticeship-based. We examine the "ROTC for private sector" funding approach that shifts costs from students to employers, polymathic curriculum combining three non-adjacent majors, and navigating 2026's unique obstacles like accreditation and funding while leveraging opportunities from starting without entrenched bureaucracy.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Designing the Partnership Ecosystem: Five Systems Missions
description: What if partnerships worked like ecosystems—adaptive, intentional, and built to lift entire systems? In this immersive workshop, participants become boundary-spanning “agents,” completing five design missions: The Connector, The Broker, The Buffer, The Coordinator, and The Innovator. Each mission unlocks a new way to build systems that connect education, industry, and community around shared purpose. Blending research, design thinking, and play, this session turns collaboration into strategy. Attendees leave with a blueprint for designing ecosystems that sustain innovation and measurable impact.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

description: Two extraordinary engineers-turned-entrepreneurs share how early exposure to entrepreneurship can change the trajectory of young women’s lives. This fireside chat explores why entrepreneurial mindsets should be taught as early as elementary school, how engineering thinking fuels problem-solving, and how giving back can spark a movement. Through personal stories and practical strategies, this session highlights how nurturing girls’ confidence and creativity prepares them to launch ventures, shape industries, and transform the future of entrepreneurship.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Moonshots that Move the Needle

description: Advanced tech is quickly expanding the ways we can support teachers and students both in and out of the classroom. But how do we design and fund programs that turn potential into impact? This panel explores how setting bold, time-bound learning goals can accelerate and level-up innovation while catalyzing impactful financial support. From coordinated research programs to targeted funding strategies, this session will explore how moonshot-centered models can rally diverse innovators around a shared outcome, encourage rigorous validation, and accelerate breakthroughs for teaching and learning.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: From TikTok to Toolbelt: Preparing Gen Z for Work
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Dubbed the ‘toolbelt generation,’ over half of Gen Z respondents in a recent survey say they are considering a skilled trade career—up 12% from last year. Schools are now working to modernize career preparation to meet the moment, including by tapping immersive technology to expose students to in-demand skilled trades. This panel brings together tech, career prep and school leaders to discuss how we can harness tech to engage students in learning while preparing them to successfully meet workforce demands.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Game On: Reimagining Career Exploration Through Play
description: Manufacturing is high-tech, high-paying, and full of opportunity—yet this vital industry still ranks last among teens’ career choices. By blending game-based learning, Dimensions of Success, and authentic career experiences shaped by SMEs and teachers, a futuristic learning pod is reshaping how students view advanced manufacturing. After one semester, students are 75% more likely to consider careers in the field, and 63% want further learning. Discover how a global industry leader, a nonprofit, and educators are sparking new enthusiasm and transforming perceptions of technical careers.Reservations are not available for this event
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Hsu Untied: Beyond Numbers: Decision-Making in the Math Classroom
description: Join a live podcast recording that explores the dynamic relationship between math and decision-making. Our guests will examine how math instruction can nurture decision-making skills such as probabilistic thinking and numeracy, and how applying decision-making contexts can lead to new math structures that feel more meaningful and relevant. Through practical examples, the discussion will show how integrating these approaches helps students apply mathematical reasoning to real-world choices and develop lasting habits of thoughtful, evidence-based analysis.

Through real examples and direct engagement with the founders, participants will uncover actionable strategies for building cross-regional partnerships, designing adaptable learning solutions, and identifying what truly resonates with today’s learners and educators.

title: Media That Matters: Nuclear Weapons & the Next Generation
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: How can we prepare a generation to understand the renewed global nuclear threats when so many students know little about nuclear history or policymaking? This session features two nuclear policy experts–supporters of education on these issues–and spotlights public media’s role in creating compelling video, primary sources, and inquiry-based learning to bring nuclear history and global security policy into the classroom. We’ll explore how media-based tools can help teachers address topics like deterrence, disarmament, and democratic accountability in a complex world.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: SESSION CANCELED: CTRL + SHIFT: Reimagining School Engagement Through Esports
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount III
description: School esports can increase attendance, raise engagement, and foster belonging. Learn why hundreds of Texas school districts (and thousands around the US) are investing in esports programs, both in the classroom and afterschool. This session breaks down the myths and shows how campus and district esports programs connect students to school and to each other.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Technology Convergence: Winning the Exponential Upswing
description: Every industrial revolution started with mashups. Today, advanced digital, biological, and physical technologies are creating their S-Curves together, unleashing a burst of convergence that generates value none could release alone, a dynamic mapped by the World Economic Forum's Technology Convergence Report. In this hands-on session, executives and founders will work through a diagnostic framework to map the intricacies of today's technology landscape, stress-test where their next major bet sits within the convergence wave, and walk away with a practical lens they can apply immediately.

title: Turning Up the Heat: Climate Content That Clicks
description: From YouTube science shows to animated series and hit podcasts, climate content is getting a creative reboot. And today, climate-smart content isn't optional; it's foundational.This session brings together leaders from media, education, and policy to explore how we prepare the next generation to adapt and lead in a changing world. Learn how storytelling, humor, and accessibility are helping families make sense of climate change and act on it together.

title: Who’s Funding the Future—and Who’s Getting Left Out
description: While billions flow into tech, too little reaches the people building for equity, justice, and long-term public benefit. This session brings together leaders rethinking who gets to fund emerging tech and why—across both venture capital and philanthropy. We'll explore how to back founders closest to the problems, build alternatives to extractive tech, and redirect capital toward innovation that actually serves communities. It's time to expand who defines innovation—and who profits from it.

title: American Exchange Project: Experiential Civics in Action
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount III
description: Picture this: Students from 8 different towns across the U.S. are watching the sun set in L.A. Among them is a line-dancing photographer from Texas, an aspiring chef from south L.A., and an avid reader becoming a real-life main character. Through the American Exchange Project, a free domestic exchange program, students get a greater understanding of themselves, their new friends, and the country we all share. In this solo talk, David McCullough III explores how domestic exchanges are the interactive civics lesson students need at this crucial time in American history.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Afraid of AI? In Cleveland, It’s Enabling Economic Mobility
description: A tale as old as time: employers need talent but can’t see who’s job-ready, and job seekers need quality jobs but have limited visibility into what’s available and pathways to get there. We’re changing that in Cleveland by coming together around a shared vision of what works and what will drive better outcomes for everyone—especially those who’ve been systemically excluded. By leveraging AI (without sacrificing any existing jobs!), we’re transforming how we connect people to opportunity and advancing economic opportunity and mobility through a smarter, more equitable workforce infrastructure.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Climate Paradox of AI Education
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: As AI transforms climate education with personalized learning and interactive simulations, we face an uncomfortable truth: the data centers powering these solutions are driving massive energy consumption that threatens to undermine our climate goals. With data center electricity use projected to double by 2030, how do we justify using energy-intensive AI to teach climate solutions? We'll examine real trade-offs, share concrete solutions, and propose a path toward AI-powered climate education that helps rather than harms our environmental future.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Code Switch: Defending Black EdTech amid AI Crackdowns
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount I-II
description: As AI takes center stage and political resistance to equity accelerates, Black-centered EdTech stands at a crossroads. This panel spotlights philanthropic, nonprofit, product, and platform leaders working to protect the cultural, pedagogical, and digital infrastructure of Black EdTech. One year into new AI regulations and increasing DEI rollbacks, we’ll explore how to build for resilience – embedding protections into platforms, pedagogy, and policy. What does it take not just to survive, but to lead and thrive in the face of erasure?
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Cracking Higher Ed: Why Startups Miss the Mark
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom B
description: Why do early-stage EdTech startups stall in higher ed? They build for assumptions, not real institutional needs. This talk introduces the Jobs to Be Done (JTBD) framework to help founders validate demand early, avoid false positives in pilots, and align products with urgent 2026 campus priorities. Fast. Focused. Brutally useful.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Deeply Human in an AI World
description: In an AI-driven world, this meet-up invites on-the-ground educators to share what’s working, to keep teaching deeply human. Human relationships are at the heart of learning. Warmth and presence matter more than ever. Let’s talk connection, creativity, and the skills students can’t get from machines. Bring your heart, your joy, and your wisdom. Leave with new tools, real strategies, and inspiration. Participants will also have the chance to share their insights with the wider educator community.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Early Days Again: What AI Makes Possible for New Schools
description: AI tools are improving quickly, and educators are already finding meaningful uses for them. What hasn’t been defined yet is the school model that makes AI work for students and teachers in a coherent, reliable way at scale.This session examines what early adopters of AI in public education are learning in real classrooms and school systems, the concrete design choices and conditions behind early success, and the operational, instructional, and governance questions that must be resolved over the next five years for AI to strengthen public education rather than fragment it.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Growing Intelligence: Youth, AI, and the Future of Ag
description: AI is revolutionizing the world around us, from how we grow food and educate our children, to how we deliver care – and it’s happening fast. So how do we ensure the next generation is empowered to thrive in an unknown future? Since 2017, 4-H and Google have equipped nearly 1.5M youth with the skills to succeed in an AI-powered world. Join this session to explore why equitable access to AI education and support for youth-led innovation are essential to building prosperity and resilience across communities and industries.

title: Hospitality as Pedagogy
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount III
description: What if classrooms were designed like hospitality spaces—hotels, restaurants, places of welcome, rhythm, and care? In this talk, designer-researcher Shruthi Manjula Balakrishna explores how hospitality frameworks such as arrival sequence, wayfinding, sensory design, and emotional arc can reshape the rituals and environments of learning. Drawing from design psychology and service strategy, this session offers a fresh lens for fostering trust, comfort, and connection in education.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Innovating in Science & Tech When Budgets are Tight
description: When public funding becomes unpredictable, how can organizations boost efficiency without stifling innovation? This fireside chat explores how the Space Telescope Science Institute, home of the Hubble and Webb telescopes, revolutionized access to space data. The lessons, born before today’s budget pressures, offer a powerful blueprint for any organization facing resource constraints. Whether you're advancing science, education, or social impact, the message is clear: Transformation is possible, even in lean times.

title: Leading the AI Future: How to Think Exponentially in a Chaotic Decade
description: AI is accelerating markets, work, and entire industries faster than most leaders can process. In this Core Conversation, Kian Gohar (former Singularity University Executive Director) joins Jeff Duncan, CEO of Ingenuity Live, to explore how leaders must shift from linear thinking to exponential thinking. Drawing on experience with NASA, XPRIZE, and Fortune 500 companies, Kian shares mental models for navigating uncertainty, reframing risk and opportunity, and building a “future operating system” to lead confidently in an AI-driven decade.

title: The Learning Geeks: How to Think When Machines Think Faster
description: AI helps us work faster than ever—but are we sacrificing how we develop? In this live podcast, we discuss why metacognition—how we think, feel, and learn—is the human advantage when partnering with AI in our work. We’ll unpack how intentional thinking helps people decide when to offload to AI, when to lean in, and how to grow through the process. Expect practical insights, geeky discussions, and evidence-based strategies you can immediately apply to monitor, evaluate, and optimize your learning and working as AI increasingly influences our lives.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

description: How can gaming unlock access to STEAM careers for underrepresented youth? This panel explores the story of STEAM in Alexandria, VA, and a proven model that blends culturally responsive teaching, bilingual support, SEL, and industry exposure to create belonging. Participants will learn our scalable framework for building culturally responsive STEAM ecosystems through partnerships with higher ed, industry, and gaming partners. The session will also explore research-based strategies using gaming, project-based learning, and cultural content to build belonging in historically excluded students.

title: Rethinking Higher Education to Build an AI-First Workforce

description: AI's rapid integration risks leaving entire groups—and vast talent pools—behind. In this panel, explore the awareness gaps impacting low-income communities and actionable strategies to cultivate an inclusive, future-ready workforce. Learn how collaborations between today’s leading companies, nonprofits, and colleges/universities will prepare students for AI-driven careers. Ask important questions like, “should degrees even be required?,” hear real-world examples of employer-backed training to equip students with applied skills, and discover how initiatives like these can be replicated.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Thrive or Survive: Why Creativity is the Key to an AI-Future
description: AI may make life easier, but at what cost to our imagination? Like a bike we stop riding, our creativity weakens when we overrely on AI. Still, while adults may feel rusty, we can get back on. For children, the risk is even greater: if they never build that muscle in the first place—never learn to ride at all—there’s nothing to return to. This all comes just as jobs are being disrupted, problems grow more complex, and we need more imagination, not less. This panel explores how we can grow creatively with AI (versus be diminished by it) and what we must build now to make that future possible.

title: Workforce Innovation in Production & the Creative Economy

Participants will share strategies, examine gaps, and discuss how policy levers like film tax credits, emerging technology, and cross-sector collaboration can create scalable, equitable pathways from education to sustainable careers.

badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

Leave with real-world examples, ready-to-use ideas, and strategies to reduce burnout, improve efficiency, and reignite your creativity. Learn how AI can help you save time on low-level tasks so you can focus on the high-level tasks that bring maximum impact to your job.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: From Insight to Impact: Superintendents Share What Works
description: Want to understand how superintendents make purchasing decisions? IEI superintendents will share candid insights and firsthand experiences to help founders and executives design solutions that truly support student success. The conversation will be guided by superintendents/district leaders and a 20-year industry veteran who has helped hundreds of ed tech companies grow and scale effectively.

title: The Big Blur: Rethinking the Transition from School to Work
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: What if high school, college, and career weren’t separate systems—but one seamless experience designed to help today’s learners succeed in a fast-changing world of work? This panel explores “The Big Blur,” a bold vision to redesign how we educate 16–20-year-olds by erasing the boundaries between K–12, higher education, and work. Panelists will share how they’re building learning models that integrate academics, career-connected learning, and real credentials—starting in high school. Hear how these efforts are creating real opportunity for students and what it will take to scale nationwide.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Charmageddon: Robots That Steal Hearts and Jobs
description: Robots are no longer confined to factories. Today, they work in offices, live in homes, and support people in hospitals. But even the most advanced technical performance doesn’t ensure success. What often makes the difference? Likeability.In this talk, we explore how product strategy, design and character development help robots go from impressive machines to trusted companions. Through real-world case studies, we’ll show how emotional connection can be just as important as capability.

title: The Education Founders Meet Up at SXSW EDU
description: Calling all education founders!If you’re building a company that serves schools, districts, or higher ed — especially in this fast-moving era of AI — this room is for you. Join us for an energizing hour of real connection with fellow education founders who understand the unique challenges of building, selling , and scaling in this market.We’ll kick off with a fast-paced session and create space for conversations about growth, funding, AI integration, and long sales cycles.Come find your next collaborator, mentor, or strategic partner — and leave with clarity, momentum, and community.

description: AI is accelerating markets, work, and entire industries faster than most leaders can process. In this Core Conversation, Kian Gohar (former Singularity University Executive Director) joins Jeff Duncan, CEO of Ingenuity Live, to explore how leaders must shift from linear thinking to exponential thinking. Drawing on experience with NASA, XPRIZE, and Fortune 500 companies, Kian shares mental models for navigating uncertainty, reframing risk and opportunity, and building a “future operating system” to lead confidently in an AI-driven decade.

title: Featured Session: How to Support Resilient Youth in an AI World
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how young people learn, connect, and understand themselves. From AI tutors and creative tools to always-on digital companions, these technologies promise new opportunities, but also raise urgent questions about children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. Rather than focusing on whether AI should be used in education, how do we ensure AI strengthens—rather than undermines—young people’s capacity to think, relate, and thrive?Drawing from the recent Brookings report "A New Direction for Students in an AI World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect,” with data across 50 countries, this panel will include a rich mix of perspectives and explore what it truly means to support resilient youth in an AI-saturated world.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Glowing Brains & Robosuits: Terminating the AI Cliché
description: The metaphors we use shape the futures we create. While the whole world becomes human-AI hybrid, we still talk about AI like a god, ghost or omnipotent spreadsheet.Corporate ‘cyborg-centaur’ sci-fi clichés trap organisations in macho, mechanistic tropes. We urgently need new myths to think with. Bring your sledgehammer as we deconstruct the lazy visual language of AI. Whether you’re designing tech, making art or building teams, the models unveiled in this session will give you the tools you need to take control and make AI an effective, equitable partner in the redesign of the new workplace.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Mall Rats Reborn: How Hot Topic is Using Entertainment to Revitalise Retail
description: Malls have long been a place for people to meet, gather and hang, and Hot Topic is celebrating that cultural legacy. As Gen Z flocks back to IRL hangouts and “third spaces," the brand is transforming shopping centres into entertainment stages. In a widely successful, experimental pilot, Hot Topic has launched two social-first sitcoms shot entirely in malls, casting creators from the emo, gamer and cosplay scenes that form the brand's DNA. It’s part of a wider shift toward entertainment-first marketing, echoing moves by brands like Dick’s Sporting Goods, which have built in-house studios to power their growth. Hear from Hot Topic and their agency partners Small W•rld and Tracksuit about how to pull this strategy off successfully.

title: Pedagogy, Politics, & Place: Video PD for a Polarized Age
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount I-II
description: With the 250th anniversary of the United States nearing, the need for accurate, engaging, and factual instruction has never been more urgent. Educators need professional development that is both flexible and trustworthy. Museums and nonprofits - longstanding partners in education - are responding to this moment by reimagining professional learning through video-based formats that preserve the power of place and story. This panel explores how these organizations are using video to support educators in navigating today’s polarized educational landscape with clarity, confidence and integrity.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Unleashing Students' Tech Genius to Ensure Mental Wellness
description: Panelists will discuss emerging tech-driven mental health solutions and the importance of involving end users – in this case, college students – in their development. Centered on the student-led creation of a first-of-its-kind AI tool to support mental health, this discussion with the project’s researchers and students will emphasize the need to let research guide how we integrate AI into healthcare to harness its benefits without creating new harms, all while empowering students to become ethical stewards of AI.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Reducing the Cognitive Cost of Change to Drive Innovation
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount III
description: Change keeps coming fast. Teams, educators, and leaders are being asked to adopt new tools, behaviors, and priorities at a relentless pace. But many change efforts fail to stick. People feel overwhelmed, disconnected, or left behind. Change does not fail because people resist it. It fails because of how we design and deliver it.This session rethinks change through the lens of learning science. Drawing on research around metacognition, motivation, and cognitive load, we’ll explore why transformation stalls when people can’t see the relevance, can’t experiment safely, or simply can’t keep up.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Startup Lessons from the Baby-Sitters Club
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom B
description: The Baby-Sitters Club was a female-founded startup that thrived in the care economy, which included early-childhood education. This session highlights how the startup founders responded to local demand, built trust, and scaled their service with limited resources. Their fee-for-service business model provided affordable childcare to families. The Baby-Sitters Club incorporated empathy, social and emotional learning, creativity, and collaborative leadership into their core values. These business practices and values are transferable to startups in the real and modern world.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: AI with Aloha: Stories from Hawai‘i’s Classrooms
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount III
description: While most schools banned AI when it first emerged, one school in Hawai‘i saw the wave coming and started paddling early. As the emerging technology teacher at ‘Iolani School, my goal was to make every mistake as quickly as possible so that we can learn valuable lessons together. We knew from day one that AI was here to stay, so while others watched from shore, my students and I were wiping out, experimenting, and figuring it out together. This session shares the stories from those chaotic early days and how they led to the AI literacy programs we’ve built in Hawai‘i today.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Augmented Reality & Place-Based Learning: Layers of Place
location: Courtyard Marriott / Rio Grande Ballroom AB
description: This interactive workshop explores how augmented reality (AR) can be used as a pedagogical tool to deepen learners’ relationships to place, memory, and community. Drawing from Layers of Place: Austin—a collection of six location-based AR artworks—participants will engage with artists’ approaches to place-based storytelling, public history, and experiential learning. Through discussion, short activities, and case studies, the session examines how AR can function as a method for cultivating attention, care, and critical engagement with the spaces we inhabit.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Beyond Voting: Defending Truth in Education: AI, Equity, & Power
description: Join Beyond Voting for a live conversation exposing how AI fuels authoritarianism in education—erasing marginalized scholarship, surveilling students, and exploiting hidden labor. We’ll examine threats to academic freedom, student privacy, and the educator workforce, and highlight how educators and students—especially those from marginalized communities—can fight for equity, protect dissent, and defend truth in learning and research.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Building AI Pathways for HBCU Talent & Community Impact
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: As AI rapidly reshapes education and work, HBCUs sit at a critical intersection of talent development, cultural stewardship, and community accountability. This panel brings together higher education, industry, and ecosystem leaders to examine how ethical AI pathways can be built with HBCUs—not around them. Panelists will explore practical models for aligning curriculum, workforce pipelines, industry partnerships, and community impact so AI innovation leads to access, ownership, and long-term opportunity for Black talent and communities.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Future is Low-Tech: Lessons from the Early 2000s
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom B
description: Let's rewind back to the early 2000s – slower interfaces, finite gameplay, loading bars, shared family computers and digital experiences that rewarded patience, curiosity and real-world interaction.In this talk, we'll unpack the overlooked design features of the era and explore how they can be applied today.This session is for designers, educators, technologists and decision-makers who want to take part in building a low-tech future!
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Future Tense: A Vibecoding Experience for Educators
description: After 3+ years of AI panels and predictions, most people still haven't experienced the technology in a way that makes the stakes visceral. This hands-on workshop changes that. Participants will "vibe code"—building functional software through conversation with AI, no technical background required. You'll create your first learning tool in 10 minutes, iterate with peers, and leave with a working prototype. We'll close by exploring what this means for the future of learning and the future of work: What happens when anyone can build software? What skills matter when routine tasks get automated? Less philosophizing, more co-creating.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Human Futures: Learning in a Tech-Shaped World
description: Through open dialogue, participants will examine how emerging tools reshape learning environments and expand access to knowledge. The meetup invites attendees to interrogate the ways tech products and services are shaping education, challenge assumptions, and imagine new models of collaboration. Through our reporting, we cover how technology sparks more inclusive, creative, culturally relevant experiences outside the West.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: It Takes a Region: Cybersecurity Pathways in Rural Alabama
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: In rural eastern Alabama, a cybersecurity talent pipeline is taking root—powered by bold collaboration between K–12 schools, postsecondary institutions, and local industry. This session shares how a regional alliance is designing hands-on learning experiences, embedding aligned credentials, and unlocking high-wage, high-demand careers for students. Discover how a rural community is transforming opportunity through workforce-aligned education and community-wide vision.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Shaping Education in a Culture x Tech World
description: As culture and technology evolve rapidly, education struggles to keep up. The CultTech Educational Program, grounded in European experience, explores how to teach without fixed curricula or best practices, focusing on real, unsolved challenges. In a world where voices can be borrowed, critical issues unfold through games, and digital tools defy the physical limits of traditional creation, this Meet Up invites diverse communities to share ideas and test how they resonate with an American audience. Join us to ask tough questions and imagine future-ready learning together.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Shortchanged: Gen Z, the Economy, and Brands
description: As economic opportunity slips further out of reach for young adults, brands face a new reality: a generation facing high living costs, stagnant wages, and declining opportunity is redefining how, why, and where they spend. This session explores how financial precarity is reshaping consumer behavior and what it means for marketers. Learn how to build relevance and win over a generation that’s not just broke, but breaking the mold.

title: Skills You’ll Need To Succeed in the Post-AI Age
description: We’re living and working in the AI age.But you already knew that.The question is: What can you do to prepare, manage, and make the most of AI-inspired change?This session is here to give you answers. It’s based on the Skills Horizon, a dynamic guide to everything you need to know next. And it features practical insights and wisdom drawn from conversations about tech, mindset, creativity, trust, productivity, and other big topics with dozens of leaders from around the world.In just one hour, you’ll walk away ready to succeed and lead in these AI-centric times.

title: The Trust Paradox: AI, Creativity, & Credibility
location: The Westin Austin Downtown / Paramount I-II
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming how stories are created, distributed, and trusted across the Internet. As generative tools expand creative possibility, they also strain credibility. How can public media, creator platforms, and cultural institutions navigate this trust paradox? This session explores how leaders across media, the creator economy, and the arts are responding. Attendees will gain practical strategies for evaluating and employing AI generated content, designing transparent workflows, and strengthening audience trust.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: The B Word: How to Get Creative with Marketing Budget
description: Sometimes the biggest constraints come from inside the house — executive leadership or clients who only see spreadsheets instead of brand-building impact. This session explores strategies for persuasion, covering the skills needed to sell your big idea and get others to buy into your vision. This candid conversation will dive into: the need to spend big for big results, how to get executives to buy in, and ways to execute bold marketing strategies, even in harsh economic times.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Creative Economy is Thriving: Why Founders Keep Building for Creators
description: As the creator economy accelerates, a new wave of founders is redefining how artists, musicians, and creators build, collaborate, and earn. This panel gathers startup founders who are shaping the next generation of creative tools and platforms to unpack why this space continues to attract such intense entrepreneurial momentum. Expect a candid conversation about what it really takes to design technology for creators— the opportunities waiting to be seized, the missteps to avoid, and the lessons learned from what works, what doesn’t, and why.

title: Discovery Engine: Inside the AI-Powered Scientific Revolution
description: AI is reshaping how discovery happens across science, healthcare, and the creative industries by dramatically accelerating research and experimentation. What once required years of trial and error can now be modeled and iterated at computational speed. Join innovators and industry leaders for an engaging discussion on the AI infrastructure enabling this shift, and why access to scalable compute is becoming foundational to the next generation of breakthroughs. Join us for an interactive session. Attendees will have a chance to win prize packs.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Fandom Force: How to Create a Fan-First Social Approach
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Fandoms are powerful, passionate groups that drive culture and conversion. New Deloitte Digital research found that 75% of surveyed U.S. adults consider themselves part of a fandom, proving there’s ample opportunity for brands to connect with truly devoted fans. In this session, Kenny Gold, Deloitte Digital’s Head of Social, Content and Influencer will be joined by Colie Edison, SVP & Chief Growth Officer of the WNBA, to explore how social-first brands can engage online fandoms, capitalize on their loyalty and build lasting relationships that go beyond the algorithm.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Multiplayer Futures: Co-Creating a Vision of SXSW 2030 (live!)
description: What if the future isn't predicted by one speaker, but imagined by thousands? In this radically inclusive and participatory session, you won't just hear a keynote – you'll co-create one.Sketch your vision of SXSW 2030, and watch your idea become part of a map of the room's collective imagination and intelligence. Then we’ll explore its signals, themes and provocations, and leave you with a clearer lens to navigate the rest of the week.All live. All together.While this will be one of the most interactive talks you’ll experience all week, it will also give you a visceral experience of what an AI-first innovation culture – where everyone has the capacity, agency and confidence to contribute – really feels like.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Forget Moments Marketing: Welcome to Worldbuilding
description: As our industry faces the death of monoculture and an increasingly fragmented landscape, it’s time for marketers to move beyond single moments or campaigns and build larger, expansive Worlds. This fireside chat will explore how brands and marketers can leverage different communities (and what it means to be a community insider), drive greater ongoing consumer engagement and loyalty, and explore rich insights when we look at Worlds through a local lens. As part of that, we’ll share Worldbuilding examples of brands steeped in culture, including the award-winning Vaseline Verified campaign.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: From Who to What to You: The Weird AI-Infused Future of the Creator Economy
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: First they laughed at YouTubers. Now they're panicking over AI-generated videos with wombats and dead celebrities. Welcome to the next chapter. This talk unpacks the hysteria around AI “slop” and reveals what’s actually changing in the creator economy. Spoiler: the future is even weirder, more customized, and deeply personal, and creators who embrace the chaos will win. And remember: it's not slop if someone loves it.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Future-Proof Your Strategy: An AI Test and Learn Lab
description: Forget fuzzy predictions. It’s time to get real. What if AI doesn’t unfold the way we expect? In this hands-on, high-energy workshop, you’ll test your strategy against four radically different futures: Growth, Constraint, Transform, and Collapse. You’ll game out scenarios, stress-test decisions, and build a future-ready playbook to lead—not just react—through AI’s uncertainty. Walk away with fresh insights, a sharper strategic lens, and tools to navigate the complexity and opportunity ahead.

title: Pitching to Angels 101: Think Like an Investor
description: Step into the investor’s shoes in a hands-on workshop, hosted by the Central Texas Angel Network, one of the nation’s leading angel groups. In this interactive session, founders will reverse roles, evaluating pitches from companies CTAN has funded—as well as those we’ve passed on, using CTAN’s processes. You’ll vote on which companies should receive investment and compare your decisions with CTAN’s actual investment choices. By dissecting these examples from an investor's lens, you’ll gain a clear sense of the qualities that drive investments and boost your chances of securing an investment.

title: The Price of Influence: Why Undefined Value Kills Trust
description: Influencer marketing is booming, but no one can answer the most basic question: What is this actually worth? Creators are guessing. Agencies rely on gut checks. Brands spend millions with no clear sense of ROI. The result? A pricing vacuum that erodes trust across the entire ecosystem. This session explores why the creator economy still lacks a standard for value, and what that's doing to credibility and performance. We'll look at how AI distorts signals and what it will take to rebuild a system where trust is the real currency. This is about the very soul of the economy we’re building.

title: Resurrecting the Future: The Viral Rise of De-Extinction
description: One word rarely used to describe synthetic biology is excitement.  While the world’s leading scientists have worked tirelessly to combat biodiversity loss, public engagement has lagged behind the urgency of the crisis. Until now that is. Colossal Biosciences is disrupting the scientific landscape by capturing mass attention and inspiring children and young people around the world, using de-extinction and advanced genetic technologies to bring unprecedented attention—and tangible solutions—to the biodiversity crisis. As traditional conservation struggles to keep pace with accelerating species loss, Colossal is redefining what’s possible —including the Colossal BioVault, a genetic repository designed to safeguard biodiversity at unprecedented scale. Protecting more than 10,000 species and preserving over one million biological samples, the BioVault represents a modern-day Noah's Ark and a bold new safeguard against extinction. This isn’t science fiction. It’s the next frontier.

title: The Rise of the Creator CEO: The Future of Creator Companies
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Charlie Chang has built far more than a 1.4M subscriber YouTube channel. He runs a multi-platform media business spanning video, podcasts, courses, websites, and localized content across languages and markets. In this fireside chat, Charlie shares how creators can evolve from solo operators into founder-led companies, design durable revenue models, and build brands that outlast algorithms. Expect practical lessons on scaling teams, owning audiences, and turning your personal brand into a real business.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: SXSW Pitch-Healthcare, AssistiveTech & BioTech
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Friday, March 13, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Healthcare, Assistive Tech and BioTech echnologies focused on human-centric applications that advance medicine and wellness and connect individuals with health systems and facilities, medical technologies and devices, care teams, providers, health insurance, pharmaceuticals, corporate and personal wellness services, and public health and social services. These startups may include applications in aging and geriatrics, vaccines, mRNA technology, general wellness, assistive technology, diagnosis, treatment, reproductive health, synthetic biology, digital therapeutics, women’s health, genetics, sports and exercise, home fitness, plant-based therapeutics, molecular diagnosis, lab-on-a-chip, child monitoring systems, sleep, and medical wearable technologies.Winner of the Healthcare, Assistive Tech and BioTech Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Through the Noise: The State of Science Storytelling
description: Science is a human endeavor, built on curiosity and beauty. So science stories are, at heart, human stories. But as we grapple with paradigm-shifting technologies, changing political landscapes and more, what is the future of science storytelling? What kinds of science stories can break through all the noise, and why? Leaders from documentary film, journalism, and public radio will explore today’s science media landscape - the challenges ahead, as well as the opportunities that might emerge.

title: Two Futurists and an AI: Seeing Trouble Ahead
description: Join celebrity futurist Faith Popcorn, techno-futurist Sarah DaVanzo, and Delph.Ai, an AI Synthetic Human trained on the minds of female futurists in a boundary-pushing, three-way debate on the future of leadership in the age of intelligent machines. With Faith’s dystopian view, Sarah’s utopian counterpoint, and Delph.Ai’s unexpected input, this session explores the trouble coming as AI challenges the very definition of human agency, influence, and power.

title: XR Immersive Filmmaking & The Future of Entertainment
description: XR is not just a new format—it’s a new language of interactive cinematic storytelling. Join director Doug Liman’s award-winning team 30 Ninjas as they explore how working with Google XR teams they are combining emerging formats like volumetric capture, spatial narrative, 3D engines and real-time Google LLM driven characters are reshaping entertainment from the inside out. Learn how cinematic craft, game mechanics, XR and AI are merging at the highest level to create the future of audience experience and participation—where the viewer isn’t just watching the story, they’re inside it.

title: You Are STILL the Brand: What AI Can’t Do for You
description: AI can write your next caption, but it can’t write your legacy. In this empowering and practical session, we are reminded that real personal brands aren’t built on trends or tools. Rather, they’re built on voice, values, and visibility. Learn what parts of your brand should never be outsourced and how to stay unmistakably you in a world of sameness.

title: 10 Truths About Technology That Are Shaping Your Life
description: Every day, invisible systems shape your decisions, desires, attention, and identity, often without your awareness. This session uncovers 10 hidden truths about how algorithms, AI models, and digital platforms subtly reprogram human behavior. Through real-world examples and sharp analysis, this talk explores how technology influences us psychologically and culturally, and what we can do to remain conscious, critical, and truly in control.

title: Activate Your Art House
description: Art House Convergence - a coalition that amplifies & connects community and mission-driven exhibitors  -  is working to provide filmmakers with best practices and resources for connecting with independent cinemas,  with  - or without - a traditional distributor.

title: Brand Worlds are the Next Marketing Frontier
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: What if we're missing AI's most transformative potential?There is a radical paradigm shift: AI as immersive brand worlds where marketers can experience thousands of futures before committing a single dollar to market. Drawing inspiration from Surrealism, where imagination and reality merge into a unified experience, we'll explore how leading brands like Unilever are creating neuro-synthetic environments where strategy, experimentation, and decision-making happen inside intelligent systems.Join us to make the jump from AI-as-a-tool to "AI-as-Worlds" and reshape the future of marketing.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: CTRL + ALT + CREATE: How AI Is Reshaping the Creative Process
description: A tech titan and a touring musician dig into how AI is making us reconsider creativity—fueling ideas, speeding production, and widening access, while also raising concerns about ownership, authenticity, and sameness. This candid conversation explores where AI elevates human imagination, where it threatens it, and how creators can keep art innovative and human.

title: Designing the Future of Human Performance
description: What happens when elite athleticism meets cutting-edge technology? This session explores how human performance, innovation, and storytelling converge to push the boundaries of what’s possible in sport and beyond.A conversation with Paralympian Hunter Woodhall, Olympic Gold medalist Tara Davis-Woodhall, Deloitte’s US Growth Markets, CMO, Nelson Kunkel, and Narrative principal Justin Moore-Lewy.

title: The Energy Evolution of Data Centers
description: The rapid pace of data center development is testing the ability of utilities to keep pace while providing the steady 24/7 power to businesses and consumers. What if AI data centers could evolve beyond passive electricity users and provide power back to the grid, bolstering grid reliability, resilience, and affordability for all consumers? An EPRI-led effort aims to demonstrate the technologies, policies, and tools to make data center flexibility a reality in different markets. Learn more about data center flexibility in a fireside chat with EPRI and Google.

title: Excess is out. Intention is in. How can brands navigate shifting consumer appetites toward moderation?
description: In an era of overstimulation and abundance, how can brands position themselves between pleasure and responsibility? This panel brings together leaders from beverages and food to discuss how moderation can become more than a corporate message — a cultural movement. From alcohol to sugar, the conversation explores how industries are reshaping narratives of enjoyment without excess.

title: Featured Session: A Conversation With Spotify's New Co-CEO About The Past, Present And Future Of Delivering Creativity To The World
description: Twenty years ago, the music industry faced an existential crisis. Piracy was reshaping consumption, the old model was unraveling, and no clear path forward existed. Spotify was born from a simple but radical belief: that technology could reconnect artists and fans in a way that worked for both. Join Gustav for a look at the future of Spotify’s technological transformation across music, podcasts, and audiobooks, exploring how user control, creator innovation, and thoughtful technology are shaping the next chapter of entertainment. He'll then be joined by country superstar Lainey Wilson and podcast host David Friedberg for a conversation on building for the long run. Two decades of music, podcasts, technology, and creative evolution; one session on what the future holds for music and podcasts.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: Breaking Barriers, Building Solutions: Meet the Changemakers Transforming Health Innovation
description: A new generation of women and underrepresented founders is reshaping the future of health with community-rooted solutions. Presented by Reckitt Catalyst and Entrepreneur-in-Residence Serena Williams, this session spotlights entrepreneurs who are turning lived experience into breakthrough ideas, exploring the barriers they’ve overcome, the models that enable scale, and what it really takes to build lasting, real-world change from the ground up.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: From Data to Decisions: Lives, Livelihoods, and the Planet
description: Decisions about land, conservation, food and energy systems, and climate are increasingly shaped by data and AI. But how does that information actually enter a decision? How does a farmer decide when to plant, or a policymaker weigh risk and uncertainty? This fireside chat explores how data-driven insights move from analysis to real-world decision-making across very different contexts, and what it takes for data and models to be reliable enough to act on when the stakes are high.

title: From Field to Fan: The Future of Sports Storytelling
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Sports brands have more tools than ever to tell powerful, emotionally resonant stories. By bringing fans closer to the action, they are immersed in the atmosphere, and given deeper access to athlete narratives. New opportunities from emerging technologies – from real-time data and spatial experiences to AI-enhanced content and personalised touchpoints – are reshaping how stories are crafted and delivered. This panel brings together top sports storytellers and technologists to explore the future of fan engagement and narrative innovation.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: From Podcasts to Radio: Unlocking Scaled Audio with Modern Attribution
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom F
description: Radio ad measurement has advanced far beyond traditional metrics, following the path podcasting paved first. As pixel-based attribution reshaped marketer expectations, offline audio is getting a fresh look. This panel explores how radio measurement is becoming smarter, more connected, and more accountable. Leaders from Podscribe, Right Side Up, Gusto, and ESPN will share how context, attention, and cross-platform signals are redefining success—and what marketers need to know to navigate today’s audio landscape.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: From Viral to Viable: Building IP as a Creator
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: How do you turn a quick video into a globally recognized brand? Four standout creators share how they used platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube to launch lasting IP. They’ll break down their creative processes, growing loyal communities, launching physical products like books and games, and keeping creative control from viral moment to full-fledged brand.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How We Could Lose Control: Avoiding the Paths to Runaway AI
description: When experts talk about AI risks, they often talk about the loss of human control – but what does that actually mean? And how could it happen? In this fireside chat, physicist Anthony Aguirre and tech ethicist Tristan Harris go beyond surface-level alarm to map out the real mechanisms that could lead humanity to be sidelined by its own creation. From opaque incentives and design choices to systemic misalignment and power concentration, they’ll unpack how humans could lose control over AI and what can still be done to prevent it.

title: The Inmates are Running the ASYLM: Ron Perlman’s New Blueprint for Creative-Led Films
description: The traditional Hollywood studio model is a top-down relic. Asylm Studios is flipping the script by handing the keys of the institution back to the people who actually make the magic. From A-list stars to the drivers and cleaners, Asylm is built on a ‘equity-for-all' ecosystem where every contributor is a literal stakeholder.Join founders Ron Perlman and Willie Morris for a candid look at how radical transparency, unshakable trust, and shared equity aren't just moral choices—they are the blueprints for a more efficient, resilient, and joyful way to create films and TV shows.

title: Launch with 0 Investors: A Playbook for Crowdfunding in 2026
description: VC money isn't flowing like it used to, but great products still deserve to launch. Crowdfunding is one of the most powerful tools a founder can use in 2026, but most get it totally wrong. This fireside chat brings together top Kickstarter consultancy LaunchBoom's CEO Mark Pecota and Austin-based hardware brand Xebec's CEO Alex Levine to show you how to crowdfund the right way. Learn why just hitting "launch" won't cut it and tactical tips on how to build momentum before day one and the operational strategies brands like Xebec use to win every launch.

title: The Lucky 10%: CPGs Flipping Off the Startup Failure Rate
description: 90% of CPG startups flame out within two years. This panel is for the scrappy founders and CEOs determined not to be a statistic. Hear from leaders who clawed their way from pre-revenue chaos to nine figures, and who know the secret sauce to sustaining viral and culturally relevant high-growth brands. No BS. No fairy tales. Just hard-won lessons from the trenches on what it really takes to scale in one of the most ruthless industries.

title: Prune or Perish: Cut Toxic Leadership for a Thriving Culture
description: Toxic leaders can look successful on the surface while quietly poisoning teams and cultures. This research-backed session reveals how to spot early warning signs of toxic leadership, recognize eight common toxic personas, and take courageous action to prevent damage. Learn how to build a healthier, more human-centered workplace where trust and engagement thrive—and where toxic behaviors are addressed before they take root.

title: Scientists Break Down the Infrastructure Behind AI
description: What does it look like when AI delivers real scientific and industry impact? In this session, scientists, founders, and executives share how they’ve moved AI from experimentation into production environments where performance and reliability matter. Join a focused and interactive discussion on the AI infrastructure and tech stacks enabling large‑scale experimentation, faster iteration, and real R&D outcomes for leaders shaping what comes next. Attendees will have a chance to win prize packs.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: SXSW Pitch-Sustainability, AgTech & Food
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Friday, March 13, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Sustainability, AgTech & Food Technologies that enhance agricultural efficiency, improve sustainability, and drive profitability in food production. This includes innovations in agriculture, horticulture, and aquaculture, as well as broader sustainability efforts in environmental management. Examples include food sustainability, food waste reduction, farm data, soil microbe data, crop protection, biofertilizers, biostimulants, supply chain transparency, agri-food testing, alternative proteins, animal health, aquaculture, farm management software, the blue economy, the green economy, hydroponics, vertical farming, Consumer Product Goods (CPG), and precision agriculture. Additionally, this category includes AI-driven farm automation, regenerative agriculture, controlled environments, and smart irrigation systems, along with sustainable practices such as waste management and recycling, water management and conservation, water security, carbon-neutral construction, air quality monitoring and pollution control, and climate change solutionns.Winner of the Sustainability, AgTech and Food Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Tradwives, Throuples, Trends: What Modern Love Means for Ads
description: We are obsessed with romantic love. But relationship dynamics in America are changing and other forms of love—from platonic to filial to digital—are taking priority in our lives. These loves aren't secondary, they shape our identities and steer our decisions. There's a reckoning that romantic love can't and shouldn't fulfill all your needs. In a culture where gender roles, dating trends, and intimacy are being renegotiated in real time, how should brands respond?
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: When AI Shops for You
description: As AI agents begin to discover, recommend, and even purchase on behalf of consumers, the traditional path to purchase is being upended. This session explores the rise of agentic commerce and how it’s reshaping the consumer journey, from search and consideration to checkout. We’ll examine the infrastructure enabling these agents, the implications for brand visibility and trust, and how marketers, platforms, and technologists must adapt to stay relevant when AI shops for us.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: 4D Volumetric Video & Gaussian Splats for XR Creators
description: Step into the future of content creation with this hands-on workshop exploring 4D Gaussian Splatting—the next evolution in volumetric video. Learn how real-time volumetric capture is transforming live streaming, XR experiences, and immersive storytelling. From filming with multi-camera rigs to compressing, rendering, and deploying in platforms like Unreal Engine, Unity, SuperSplat, and UVRSE, you'll discover how this cutting-edge tech brings people, performances, and places into fully spatialized digital worlds.

title: 7 Lies People Tell Today (& the Brain Science to Beat Them)
description: To separate yourself from AI slop and connect with customers, you need authentic, relevant messages.But customers say things about how they behave & what they want that just aren’t true. And their insistence compromises your marketing. Discover 7 current, common lies customers tell, plus the brain science backed ways to counteract them. Jammed with proven research and in-market examples, this session reveals exactly how to avoid messaging landmines at a time when what you say and how you say it makes all the difference. Don’t do what people claim they want. Do what science shows will work.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Art of Scaling Your Startup Without Venture Capital
description: I dive into what it really takes to build a high-growth startup without a dollar of venture capital, both in this session and in my new book, Bootstrapping Bronto. I introduce fresh frameworks and practical models for bootstrapping—brought to life through the story of starting Bronto Software, growing it year by year on our own terms, and ultimately selling it to NetSuite/Oracle for $200 million. The result is equal parts practical playbook and behind-the-scenes story.

title: Beyond Name Tags: An Insider's Guide to Connecting at SXSW
description: Most conference connections start with the same question: "so what do you do?" But the ones that feel effortless happen when you show up as who you are, not what you do. Two speakers with completely opposite approaches show you what actually works at SXSW. Adam figured out how to have strangers approach him all day at his first South by without starting a single conversation. Sally's quiet at first, but somehow ends up getting invited places by strangers within minutes of meeting them. You won't just hear about it … you'll do it. Whether you're outgoing or anxious, you'll leave knowing people you'll actually want to hang out with the rest of the week.

title: Big Ideas, Tiny Budgets: How Creativity Wins
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Grace Wells breaks down how she turned scrappy parody commercials into a full-blown creative career. She’ll walk through her process for producing viral-quality product videos using minimal equipment, a sharp eye, and a lot of imagination. Attendees will learn how creativity, not budget, drives reach—and how to use humor, storyboarding, and craft to make content that brands and audiences love.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Busting Myths: Why Virtual Production Isn’t What You Think
description: Across the industry there are several misconceptions about virtual production. It’s a VFX tool. It’s only good for car scenes or sci-fi. It’s too expensive for independent films. It’s a silver bullet that can fix all problems. In this session our panel will bust these myths and provide insight into how filmmakers can truly benefit from virtual production and how it’s changing the film and TV production landscape. And to put it to the test, the session will end with our panel breaking down a film treatment live to show how filmmakers can plan a shoot to get the most out of virtual production.

title: Climate Action Core Conversation
description: This interactive meetup flips the traditional panel format by turning attendees into speakers. In three fast-paced rounds, volunteers from the audience will take the stage to introduce themselves and answer two curated questions from the hosts. This peer-led format encourages cross-sector dialogue, surfaces unexpected insights, and empowers emerging voices in sustainability, circularity, regenerative systems, and climate action. The session ends with guided, field-specific networking designed to foster collaboration and diversify professional connections within the climate space.

title: The Companies Building and Automating Space's Superhighway
description: How do we keep space sustainable as it gets more crowded? Enter the Space Superhighway: a network of in-space robots that will keep spacecraft fueled, repaired, upgraded and built in orbit. Just like highways rely on gas stations, tow trucks and repair shops, space will need support systems to be accessible for future generations. This tech is real TODAY. Our panel convenes accomplished doers from startups working to extend the life of satellites, reduce space debris and build a future where space is safe, scalable and sustainable for exploration and economic development to benefit society.

title: Featured Session: 5 Non-Obvious Secrets of Human Connection (For Love & Profit)
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: What will it take to win the future? We’re suffocated by noise, minimized by algorithms, and living in fear of being replaced by technology. The cocoon of technology is making us lose touch with the messy, irreplaceable spark of real connection. In this provocative featured session, SXSW favorite Rohit Bhargava reveals five counterintuitive secrets from his most recent trend research about how we bond, trust, and remain indispensable in the modern world. The people who understand people always win. If you believe this, then this session is your key to becoming one of them.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: A Conversation with The All-American Rejects
description: For their first-ever SXSW appearance, The All-American Rejects will share how their sold-out House Party Tour reimagined live music by performing in backyards, skateparks and unique spaces for fans and artists. Once a staple of the major label system, the band is now fully independent—writing, recording, and releasing on their own terms. In this session, they’ll explore how artists can break into touring without gatekeepers and build community-driven experiences outside traditional venues.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: Shaping Brand Relevance for a New Generation
description: Join Disney for a candid, forward-looking conversation on staying culturally relevant and driving growth in a rapidly changing market. Drawing from real-world experience at the intersection of commerce, creativity and global trends, this session will tackle the essential question for any organization: "How can brands remain meaningful and resilient for future generations?"
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Forget Recruiting. Design Your Own Workforce.
description: What if your future leaders are already working inside your company - and they haven’t even graduated yet? Forward‑thinking organizations aren’t waiting for talent to emerge. They’re helping shape it.  Companies of all sizes are using work-integrated programs to build stronger talent pipelines, fuel innovation, and cultivate future-ready skill sets. In this candid conversation, you’ll learn why hands-on experience is quickly becoming one of the most valuable credentials and how organizations that invest early gain not just future-ready talent, but a strategic advantage.

title: From Vault to Vision: Reviving Film History with AI
description: If you ask someone their favorite version of The Great Gatsby, they might say Leonardo DiCaprio as few know there was a 1926 version that no longer exists. This session dives into how archives and AI are transforming how we preserve, discover, and give new life to forgotten film history. With insights from expert film archivists from leading production companies , we'll explore how AI-powered metadata tools are modernizing cultural preservation, making once-lost footage more accessible for creators and audiences alike.

title: The Geography of Prosperity: A New Map for Resilient Cities
description: We’re entering an era where resilience—not raw growth—determines success. This session unveils the Prosperity Index, a groundbreaking tool that ranks U.S. cities across five future-critical dimensions: population renewal, climate resilience, automation readiness, social cohesion, and governance foresight. In a time of climate disruption, demographic decline, and AI-driven change, discover which cities are rising, which are falling behind, and how data, design, and long-term thinking can future-proof communities and economies.

title: A Guide to the SXSW 2026 XR Experience
description: Step into the future with three XR and SXSW veterans as they pull back the curtain on the special 10th anniversary edition of the XR Experience, a SXSW highlight since 2016. Deep dive into cutting-edge immersive tech, storytelling, and AI innovations. Gain exclusive insights into this year’s standout projects and discover how XR and AI are reshaping industries like entertainment, healthcare, retail, and education. Whether you’re a first-timer or a seasoned attendee, learn how to make the most of your time at the exhibition and connect with creators and industry leaders.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Hello Again: Restoring Voices with AI
description: What if you lost the ability to speak forever and then found it again? This session offers a preview of an 11-part docuseries spotlighting people living with permanent voice loss and how, through AI technology, they speak again to their family, friends, and now the world. During the panel, you’ll witness a woman use her AI-restored voice live on stage and learn how voices can be recreated from home videos or even a single voicemail. You will walk away with a deeper understanding of how this work expands access, restores identity, and helps shape a more human-centered AI future.

title: Launching a New WNBA Team: The Story of the Toronto Tempo
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: As the WNBA prepares to tip off its expansion into Canada, this panel will take audiences behind the curtain of launching the Toronto Tempo (the first team outside the USA). With just months to go before their inaugural game in 2026, the Tempo’s CMO and CRO will share insights into the decisions that have defined the early stages of building a professional sports team. From defining a brand to selling founding sponsorships and shaping community partnerships, this conversation explores the intersection of sport, business, and culture at a defining moment for women’s sports.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Make it in Texas! Film, TV and Digital Media Meet Up
description: Love to make movies, shows or digital media content? Do you have a project to produce in Texas? Are you wanting to connect with the Texas production community and beyond? If so, the Texas Film Commission invites you to come chat about ways to make it happen. Let’s talk about locations, local production crews, vendors, grants, incentives, new technologies and the creative community in the Lone Star State. We want to share with you the many resources that Texas has to offer as a destination for your next project.

title: Pitch Camp! How To Win Creative Pitches by Being More Human
description: Let's be honest, pitching clients for new business is a slog.  Whether you're seeking new partners or locked in a 5-way competition, there is a way to cut through the madness and remain calm. This workshop will focus on human connection, now more important than ever in the age of AI. We will share hands-on tools for deepening client relationships, getting the right flow in your pitch, collaborating with your team, and standing out in a world of sameness. The facilitators have years of experience on both sides of the pitch table: at Frog, McKinsey, Philips, and Publicis Sapient.

title: Play to Build: Prosocial AI for Human Connection
description: What if AI helped us connect more deeply with each other, not just with a chatbot? This hands-on workshop introduces the 5 Principles for Prosocial AI, a new framework for designing tech that strengthens identity, agency, and real-world relationships. Then, we play The AI Effect, a fast-paced card game that challenges you to debate, design, and reflect: when does AI bring us closer, and when does it pull us apart? Come for the research-backed design; stay for the playful friction, ethical dilemmas, and radical imagination.

title: Sprints for Social Impact: Turn Time Pressure into Purpose
description: Discover how to turn purpose into action through rapid collaboration. In this interactive workshop, participants uncover their company’s unique “superpower” and use it to design a purpose-driven prototype addressing a real community need. Through guided exercises, folks will learn to align creativity with impact, build shared value between corporate and community goals, and craft a compelling pitch to bring their prototype to life. Experience how purpose-driven sprint work can empower employees through meaningful volunteerism and opportunities to create lasting social change.

title: SXSW Pitch-Smart Cities, Transportation, Manufacturing & Logistics
