description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Friday, March 13, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Smart Cities, Transportation, Manufacturing & LogisticsTechnologies that create and advance smart cities, enhance transportation systems, modernize manufacturing, transform logistics, and strengthen government infrastructure and civic operations. These innovations improve urban environments, mobility, industrial automation, global supply chains, and the travel and tourism industry. Applications include real estate development, infrastructure, utilities, public works, security, population data, government and civic services, digital service delivery, transparency, and urban planning and design. This category also encompasses advancements in transportation, such as autonomous vehicles, automotive improvements, ridesharing, multi-modal mobility, product delivery, and logistics optimization. In manufacturing, it highlights Industry 4.0 innovations like IoT-enabled production, 3D printing, robotics, metamaterials, predictive maintenance, digital twins, augmented construction, omnichannel management, and AI-driven industrial automation. Supply chain and logistics technologies focus on automation in warehouses, last-mile delivery, inventory management, and AI-powered demand forecasting.Winner of the Smart Cities, Transportation, Manufacturing & Logistics Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: U.S. vs. The World: the Future We’re (Currently) Losing
description: Is the United States losing its edge in global innovation? In this high-energy continuation of last year’s SXSW session, Congressman Gabe Amo and VC Matthew Weinberg delve further into how Trump-era research cuts, clean energy rollbacks, and talent restrictions have created lasting consequences for U.S. competitiveness and opened the door for China to surge ahead. Blending policy insight with on-the-ground investor perspective, this advanced fireside chat asks: How do we revamp a system with no Plan B, and what bold, bipartisan moves can win our future back before it’s too late?

title: What Marketers Get Wrong About Gen Z
description: Think you know Gen Z and how they behave? This session will invite you to think again. Drawing on ongoing global research among 250,000 Gen Zs annually, we’ll separate the fact from the fiction for many of the stereotypes and commonly accepted truths about this generation. From how Gen Z consume content, use screens and engage with social media, through to their attitudes and lifestyle behaviors, we’ll explore how marketers can look beneath the surface to understand what’s truly driving Gen Z - and which things make them unique.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Who Gets Credit When Anyone Can Train a Music AI?
description: Open-source models like MusicGen and LLaRK have made it possible for anyone to generate music trained on unlicensed datasets, with no metadata, no audit trail, and no path to compensation. This panel unpacks the growing attribution crisis: how metadata is breaking down, how local models make enforcement impossible, and how startups and regulators are working to rebuild trust and traceability. Can ethical licensing survive when the tools of mass creation are in everyone’s hands?

title: Beyond the Algorithm: Belonging in a Human+AI World
description: As AI becomes a collaborator at work, it’s reshaping how people find meaning, connect, and contribute. This panel explores how belonging evolves in Human+AI teams and the behaviors and mindsets leaders must model to inspire trust, motivation, and performance. Talent and leadership experts will discuss how to design emotionally intelligent cultures,  the human skills that matter most in an AI world, and how to ensure people feel valued for what only they can uniquely bring to an AI-augmented workplace.

title: The Clock Is Ticking for Space – Replacing the ISS
description: After 30 years of continuous operation, the International Space Station is going away, and fast. What comes next? Enter Axiom Space. Against all odds, this Houston startup flew four crewed commercial missions to the ISS, led record-breaking research, engaged more than 31 nations in the pursuit of space access and partnered with Prada, Oakley, Nokia, and NASA all in pursuit of building the world’s first commercial space station. The dream of living and working in space is no longer distant. Welcome to the space industrialization era—the time is now to transcend Earth.

title: Creators Are the New Gatekeepers: Building Opportunity in Entertainment
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Today’s creators aren’t just reacting to Hollywood, they’re shaping it. As audiences seek authenticity, creators have become the new gatekeepers of what’s seen, shared, and celebrated. From viral interviews to creator-led red carpets, this session dives into how creators can build influence in the entertainment world — crafting content that connects, collaborating with celebrities, studios, and brands, and turning partnerships into genuine growth. Hear from leading creators and industry insiders on how to build your voice, expand your reach, and become part of the next wave driving entertainment forward.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Fandom Runs The World: How Culture and Marketing Collide in 2026
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Join us on an exploration of how fandom now drives what becomes the next blockbuster, bestseller, or breakout moment. As audiences seek belonging and identity in an increasingly fragmented digital world, fan communities have emerged as the true tastemakers of culture. Spanning entertainment, media, and brands, this panel examines how participation and passion are reshaping how culture is created, discovered, and marketed.Reception to follow--stay to meet the speakers!
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: Enough with the Bullsh*T: BuzzFeed Founder Shares His Plan to Make the Internet Fun Again
description: After years of doomscrolling and algorithmic slop, BuzzFeed founder Jonah Peretti believes the internet is overdue for a reset, and for the first time he will share his plan to make the internet fun again. We’ll take you behind the scenes of BuzzFeed’s AI incubator where we are creating a new medium for content, self-expression, and social connection. We’ll start with big picture ideas you can apply to your business. Then you’ll see live demos of new products and attendees will get special access to the latest from our lab. You’ll see how AI-powered experiences can create shared cultural moments and help people explore the world together, creating a better alternative to filter bubbles and doomscrolling. We are transforming our business with a new approach to AI, and you can join us to think about how to transform your own work to maximize creativity, human connection, and growth.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: How Anyone Can Help Create Social Change: A Conversation with the authors of Somebody Should Do Something
description: People often feel powerless to create social change. Issues like climate change, misinformation, and the erosion of democracy are “wicked” problems. They are complex, multifaceted, global challenges with no simple solutions. How can anyone make a difference when it comes to problems like these? Drawing from philosophy, history, and contemporary social science, this talk offers tools for thinking in new ways about how individuals can help create transformative social change. Each of us can make a difference, but not in the ways we usually think.

title: Inside the Campaign: Deconstructing Successful Brand-Creator Partnerships on YouTube
description: How do high-impact collabs between YouTube creators and brands come together? Go behind the scenes with Adam W, a YouTube creator with more than 21M subscribers, and Ryan Deter, CEO of Influential, to unpack what goes into campaigns that land with audiences and ignite brand growth.Discover how partnerships come to life, the creative chemistry required for success, and the data-driven tactics that turn authentic storytelling into tangible business results.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Inspired by Research. Not Ruined by It.
description: “Testing” in Creative has a bad reputation, but that’s because the way most of it is framed and conducted is shit. It relies on likes and dislikes, dampening the soul of the work - making Creative vanilla. But not in our world. Here, winning Creative comes when brilliant brains are inspired by research; we elevate Creative by exploring unknown unknowns and using Creative to stimulate - and to power stronger ideas. Testing is no threat to creativity, it’s a catalyst. We’ll prove that research is invaluable through dialogue and perspectives from brand, agency, and research leaders – providing strategies to supercharge your work so it really lands in an age when your work has to hit harder than ever.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Leadership Unblocked: Break Through What's Holding You Back
description: Ever feel like you’re doing all the “right” things—yet still feel stuck? The problem isn’t effort or ambition. It’s the unseen beliefs shaping how you show up at work. In this eye-opening talk, Harvard Business Press author and host of the Coaching Real Leaders podcast, Muriel M. Wilkins, pulls back the curtain on what holds even the highest achievers back. Drawing from her bestselling book, Leadership Unblocked, she reveals seven beliefs that limit leadership potential—and offers a practical roadmap to break through them and lead with greater impact and ease.

title: Saving Monarchs With Machine Learning and Computer Vision
description: Monarch butterflies are experiencing severe declines, but AI-powered computer vision offers new hope for their conservation. This session explores how machine learning aids in identifying milkweed and invasive species, supporting precise conservation efforts across millions of acres. Learn how innovative image analysis is transforming vegetation management and habitat monitoring by combining technology and ecology to protect iconic pollinators, and why this approach is vital for maintaining biodiversity resilience.

title: SXSW Pitch-Smart Data, Security, and FinTech
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Friday, March 13, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Smart Data, Security, and FinTech Innovations that harness data and protect digital ecosystems while reimagining how people and institutions interact with money and information. This category includes startups focused on big data collection, simulation, analysis, and interpretation—turning raw information into actionable insights. These solutions prioritize effective data presentation and visualization, making complex content accessible through advanced analytics and user-centric design. Cybersecurity measures include threat detection, response, digital privacy, identity and access management, encryption, secure communications, infrastructure protection, and resilience against cyberattacks. This category also features cutting-edge financial technologies including fraud prevention, fraud protection, open banking, programmable money, cryptocurrencies, insurtech, embedded finance, payment systems, and alternative risk scoring. Solutions often support e-commerce platforms and help institutions maintain secure, scalable, and data-intelligent infrastructures. These innovations are critical in enabling trusted, connected digital economies across industries like finance and advertising.Winner of the Smart Data, Security and FinTech Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Vibe Shift: How New Media Upended Our Ideas and Aesthetics
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: What are the vibes? This presentation will unpack the idea of a "vibe" - an aesthetic appraisal of a situation - and how these vibes can "shift." These shifts are dictated by cultural and political undercurrents, by current events, but most importantly by developments in technology. In a rapidly changing landscape with more sophisticated generative AI and social media algorithms, the vibes are shifting almost too fast to keep up. New meme genres, political movements, and ideological upheavals are occurring - and this talk will explain how and why.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: What AI Agents Are Doing While You're Not Watching
description: Your AI assistant has the keys to everything – email, finances, sensitive systems – but operates in shadows you can't see, a problem that continues to grow as companies unleash agentic AI faster than they can build safeguards. This panel will explore how AI agents are changing the way we think about work and how we trust technology. We'll ask: What happens when an AI assistant, designed to help, accidentally (or intentionally) steps out of line? How do we even know what they're up to when they operate independently? And how can we stop AI agents from becoming the next insider threat?

title: Who Owns What: The State of Startups & Top Trends to Watch
description: Each year billions of dollars are invested in finding and building the next generation of great companies. So how do you increase your chances of being found and funded? Carta has conducted an analysis of data from tens of thousands of startups in its network since 2018, offering a bird’s eye view of the top trends in startup funding and job distribution. Discover whether employees are still getting meaningful equity, if founder ownership has improved or worsened over the last 5 years, and what you need to know to navigate these challenges as you build and pitch your company.

title: SXSW Pitch-Student Pitch
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Friday, March 13, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Student startups that are showcasing innovative technology with a focus on Enterprise and Future of Work; Entertainment, Media, Sports & Creator Content; Healthcare, AssistiveTech & BioTech; Innovative World Tech; Intelligent Systems, Robotics, & Multisensory Technology; Smart Cities, Transportation, Manufacturing & Logistics; Smart Data, Security, and FinTech; Student Startups; and Sustainability, AgTech & Food. A student from the startup management team will need to present at SXSW Pitch.Winner of the Student Pitch will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Featured Session: Amy Webb Launches 2026 Emerging Tech Trend Report
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Forget hype cycles. The real story of 2026 is bigger, stranger, and closer than you imagine. At SXSW, futurist Amy Webb, CEO of the Future Today Strategy Group and professor at NYU Stern, will take you on a mind-bending exploration of the forces reshaping business, culture, and society. With her signature wit and data-driven approach, Amy will decode the emerging technologies that demand your attention and reveal scenarios that challenge how we think about the future. If you’ve joined Amy’s packed sessions in past years, you know to expect startling insights, big shifts in perspective, and ideas you’ll be talking about long after the session ends. This year, though, there’s a twist you won’t see coming—one that could change the way we track, understand, and act on trends forever. The theme is Creative Destruction. While there's no official dress code for the session, we do recommend you wear black.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Brand Strategy Workshop for Emerging Tech Companies
description: This workshop is focused on the strategic brand positioning of your innovative company and cutting-edge technology. We will be firmly situated on the market side of product-market fit. We will start by investigating your target market(s) and then your competitive set. Then we will work together to articulate the qualities of your company and technology that are differentiated and valuable to your next investor or client. All with the goal of helping your company to stand out in an unprecedented time of innovation.

title: Breaking Cultural Codes That Keep Latino Leaders Silent
description: What if the biggest barrier to innovation isn’t lack of talent, but cultural dissonance silencing brilliant minds? After nearly 10 years of Latinas sharing their secrets with me, I’ve discovered how collective mindset conditioning clashes with individual-focused business culture. Latinas taught that visibility, ambition, and self-promotion are taboo unconsciously sabotage their potential. One executive never pitched her game-changing strategy - not from imposter syndrome, but because visibility felt like betraying her cultural values. This session reveals how to break these codes while honoring our culture.

title: Can Media Survive AI? The Fight for Public Trust
description: AI is reshaping how information is created, distributed, and trusted. But it’s not just a threat - it’s an opportunity to reimagine what media can be. We will explore how AI is transforming business models, content incentives, and consumer expectations. From the rise of creators to the reinvention of trust, we’ll unpack what’s breaking, what’s possible, and how to build what’s next.

title: Climate in Crisis, Health at Risk: Technology as a Lifeline
description: The climate crisis is now a public health emergency. Heatwaves, floods, and droughts are worsening chronic conditions and disrupting healthcare infrastructure. This panel brings together global experts in health, science, and technology to showcase real-world solutions that are strengthening health system resilience—from predictive models using climate and health data to AI tools and digital platforms enabling remote care in vulnerable regions. Technology can be the bridge between collapse and adaptation.

title: Couture Cognition: How Fashion Shapes Our Brains
description: What if fashion could rewire your brain? Neuroscientist Dr. Heather Collins discusses Neurostyle: How shoes and clothing activate memory, mood, and neural storytelling—boosting focus, emotional resonance, and curiosity. From color-triggered cognition to texture-based associations, fashion activates neural empathy through shared aesthetic choices. Discover how style shapes brain states and how professionals—from designers to technologists—can harness fashion’s cognitive power to craft more inclusive, immersive, and high-performing environments, from runways to boardrooms.

title: The Creator Growth Blueprint: How Small Audiences Become Real Businesses
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Newsletters are increasingly going beyond just the written word, expanding into podcasts, in-person events, digital products, and more. This session goes deep inside the explosion of the newsletter industry and offers a pulse on where things are headed next. That evolution is opening the door for creators to turn small, loyal audiences into real businesses. But while every breakout creator starts with a small but highly engaged audience, turning that early traction into predictable income is where most get stuck. In this candid, tactical conversation, beehiiv CEO and co-founder Tyler Denk joins Jasmine Enberg, co-founder of creator economy media company Scalable, to unpack what actually drives sustainable growth in the creator economy today. Together, they’ll explore:1. How early-stage creators identify and keep their highest-value audience.2. What separates creators who break out from those who stall.3. The first revenue streams that create real momentum and how newsletter models are evolving beyond writing (including Scalable).4. The systems and workflows to scale your newsletter business.5. The most common traps new creators fall into — and how to avoid them.Attendees will leave with a clear understanding of the state of the newsletter industry and how to turn small audiences into long-lasting businesses.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Epitome of Innovation: Long-Term Creator Partnerships
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: In the digital age, creator relationships based on trust and long-term investment are the ultimate innovation. These relationships not only empower creators as content engines but as culture-shaping collaborators who drive full-funnel results. Learn how real influence begins when creators start in the strategy room and long-term partnerships are not only established but fostered.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Why Saying Yes to the Unthinkable Works
description: We’re conditioned to play it safe—but what if the wild, unpolished ideas are actually the smartest bets? In this talk, you'll hear real stories of bold ideas that went from "no way" to "how did they do that?" It's a rallying cry for creators and leaders to stop self-editing, embrace bold thinking, and protect the unthinkable—because that’s where the magic (and real impact) happens.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: 'Heated Rivalry' Effect: How LGBTQ+ Content Drives Culture
description: More than a gay hit, HBO’s “Heated Rivalry” is a massive phenomenon at the center of an unprecedented wave of LGBTQ+ content now shaping culture (think: “Stranger Things,” “The Hunting Wives”). It reflects the larger truth that queer is now mainstream, and that’s exactly how marketers need to think about LGBTQ+ content and consumers to seize a huge opportunity. Execs from GLAAD, Do The WeRQ, PrideNOW, and Mark Allen & CO. unpack this moment and present new data for brands, as the time is now to pivot to queer storytelling.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How to Design a Successful Work Culture
description: Work culture gaps remain unbridgeable at many companies, yet failing to shrink them in today's multi-generational work world is like ignoring the oil light on your dashboard. You're not avoiding a breakdown—you're scheduling it for later. Join this remarkable line-up of today's top work culture experts to hear what keeps driving the divides—and how these designers are helping firms shift traditional career models, link people strategy to business value and use AI and new narratives to improve how people experience work, not as a demographic challenge, but as a design and strategy imperative.

title: Is Search Totally F**ked? What Do We Do Now?
description: We’re wired to search. With billions of search queries per day, marketers have long known where and how to find their audiences.Now with the rise of generative engines and AI, the search landscape is facing its biggest upheaval in decades. Traditional search, long the backbone of digital strategy, is being rapidly replaced by generative engine optimization (GEO), fragmented user journeys, and new discovery ecosystems. This session will highlight immediate threats to legacy approaches and inspire strategies for brands, marketers, and advertisers to reclaim visibility and influence.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Marketing Without a Map: Building in the Age of Chaos
description: Marketing playbooks are dead. In this hands-on session, Erik Huberman—founder of Hawke Media—dives into what actually drives brand growth when algorithms shift daily, consumer trust is eroding, and AI-generated content floods the feed. You’ll learn how to test faster, adapt smarter, and build a resilient marketing engine in an era of uncertainty. No fluff. Just a strategy that works when nothing else makes sense.

title: Missionary Misfits: Why Not Belonging Makes You Build Better
description: The best companies come from founders who don't quite fit in. Steve Vassallo (Foundation Capital, former IDEO designer) calls them "missionary misfits,” founders who weave different disciplines together instead of mining one vertical. He believes these cross-domain builders create the most transformative companies. Steve will sit down with Framer co-founder, Jorn van Dijk, whose misfit position helped him and his co-founder spot the dysfunction between design and development, and create a company now worth $2B. Learn why being an outsider might be the ultimate competitive advantage.

title: The New Lab Partner: AI and Future Scientific Discovery
description: AI is quickly becoming an active thinking partner, completely changing scientific discovery. That means, potentially faster cures, new drug uses, and insights to combat viruses. But it sparks key philosophical questions: Who owns AI-generated insight? How do we validate it? Are we more innovative or dependent? Join experts to explore AI's role in surfacing new questions, defining co-discovery, and navigating its risks/rewards for human health. A critical conversation on AI's radical impact on discovery.

title: Now is Japan's Time: Leading the Future with Deep Tech
description: Japan has a long history of developing fermentation technologies, which have served as the foundation for the growth of its biotechnology industry. Additionally, Japanese anime and games have garnered a large global fan base, generating excitement around new business opportunities utilizing these IPs. In this session, four speakers will share real-time insights from diverse perspectives on the forefront of Japan’s deep tech and IP businesses, as well as the emerging global ventures centered around them!

title: SXSW Pitch-Intelligent Systems, Robotics, & Multisensory Technology
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors. On Saturday, March 14, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Intelligent Systems, Robotics, & Multisensory Technology isTechnologies that enhance the way people connect, communicate, and share unique life experiences with those around them in a digital ecosystem. These technologies may be related to machine-simulated intelligence, consciousness, ease, speech, the Internet of Things and smart devices, or a machine's ability to perform, anticipate, and streamline tasks without human assistance. These startups seek to address a wide variety of issues and may include Web5 and decentralized applications, wearables, blockchain technology, multi-sensory incorporation, augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), Super Apps, generative AI, AI chips, autonomous systems, human-robot interaction, Robotic Process Automation, Natural Language Processing, and more.Winner of the Intelligent Systems, Robotics & MultisensoryTechnologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Tackling the “snakes” undermining digital rights
description: In the US and globally, civil society is facing existential challenges due to significant funding shortfalls resulting from cuts by the US and other traditional donors. For many nonprofits, this has meant imposing program cuts and layoffs, jeopardizing their ability to deliver on their missions sustainably.We plan to discuss the following challenges (or “snakes” according to Indigenous prophecy) undermining digital rights and how to overcome them: the shrinking of resources, the increasing power of Big Tech, data colonialism, the rise of war crimes, and energy-demanding technologies.

title: Vibe Coding: From Idea to MVP at Warp Speed
description: Forget decks and buzzwords—this is a build-first session. In this interactive workshop, founders will use Vibe Coding, a fast, intuition-driven method for turning ideas into real products. Using LEGO® to spark rapid collaboration and system-level thinking, participants will physically map app components before we Vibe Code them into working software. Along the way, we’ll explore what it actually takes to build an enterprise-ready app—covering usability, security, reliability, and scalability—so speed doesn’t come at the expense of quality. Expect hands-on collaboration, high energy, and an open-source prototype you can keep building after SXSW. No coding expertise required—just ideas, creativity, and collaboration.

title: Water is the New Sports Arena: The Future of Stadium Racing
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: SailGP is transforming water into the world’s most thrilling sports arena. Founded by Larry Ellison and Sir Russell Coutts, this high-speed, data-driven, climate-positive sailing league is built for the digital age. With 60mph races in iconic cities, real-time data, and immersive broadcasting, SailGP delivers scroll-stopping content for Gen Z. It’s not just a sport—it’s a purpose-driven movement redefining how fans experience live competition and putting them at the heart of the action.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Ambitious Things: How Big Ideas Become Real
description: Everyone has that one idea. The big, unruly, “what if?” thing. The idea that feels exciting... and terrifying. Ambitious Things is a creative-philosophical talk about how to turn ambitious ideas into ambitious realities. Drawing on decades of experience leaping into the unknown, writer, director, and Studio Farland founder Hal Kirkland shares the small steps that help us start, keep going, and stay inspired enough to finish, without waiting for permission, certainty, or perfect conditions. A talk for anyone cradling the weight of a wild idea and who need a boost to finally make it real.

title: Beyond Trends: Building Influence on AI-Saturated Algorithms
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: It’s estimated that 90% of content will be AI generated by 2026. But what does that actually mean for creators?  The impending boom of AI generated content will create an algorithm shift. In a world where AI tools can create content that can instantly copy any aesthetic, sound, or format, trends will no longer be the path to relevance, but a path to fatigue. This presentation shares actionable insights to embrace radical individuality and make sure that content creators of today are not the ones that get left behind tomorrow.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Build the Future: Entrepreneurship Within Reach
description: Entrepreneurship is one of the last viable paths to economic mobility in America—offering ownership, stability, and long-term wealth. But that path is narrowing fast. Early-stage funding to women and underrepresented founders has dropped nearly 50% since 2020 (Crunchbase, 2023), while systems like childcare, capital access, and mentorship remain fragile. This session brings together cross-sector leaders to ask: What must we build now to keep entrepreneurship within reach for the next generation?

title: Building Brands That Back Creators
description: As creators become the cultural vanguard, brand marketing must evolve. Author of the ICYMI newsletter Lia Haberman joins Mike McGarry, SVP of Marketing at Patreon, in a conversation on how to build campaigns that don’t just feature creators — they center them. Learn actionable insights from Patreon's marketing strategy which amplifies authenticity, drives relevance, and builds loyalty across both creator and fan communities. Through viral case studies and creator-led activations, this session will arm marketers, CMOs, and creatives with the tools to thrive in a world where creators are the brand.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: 10 Breakthrough Technologies of 2026
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Each year, MIT Technology Review reporters and editors assemble a list of the top breakthrough technologies that will change the world. We ask ourselves: What will really matter in the long run? And while we can’t see the future, we expect these technologies to affect our world in a big way, for decades to come. Niall Firth will share the publication’s list of ten breakthrough technologies with the SXSW audience, explaining each one and how it will impact the way we live and work.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Internet After Search
description: The economic model that has funded the Internet for three decades is breaking. AI systems now provide answers directly instead of sending users to websites, and AI agents are beginning to handle transactions without people ever visiting a site. Content creators are losing traffic and revenue, and nobody has figured out what replaces it.As CEO and co-founder of Cloudflare, Matthew Prince has a unique view of how the web is changing and what comes next, handling 20% of all Internet traffic. This session will examine the post-search Internet and the profound shift in power. We’ll dive into how AI agents are changing commerce, who truly controls information access, and the looming question of who gets paid for content. The choices we make now regarding compensation, ownership, and digital access will determine the web's future trajectory.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: How B2B Creators Are Driving the Next $100M Wave
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: While everyone is focused on channels like TikTok and Instagram, B2B creators are quietly building the next $100M wave in the creator economy. These "professional creators" aren’t chasing trends, they’re trusted experts turning LinkedIn, newsletters, and podcasts into engines for influence and revenue. This panel brings together leading voices from both sides of the ecosystem (Brand and Creator) to explore how creators are transforming B2B go-to-market strategies, reshaping brand trust, and unlocking new ways to drive growth in high-consideration industries.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How to Raise $$$ From Angel Groups: What We Really Look For
description: The objective of this session is to get more Early-Stage companies to apply for funding through the 200+ Angel Groups and Funds across the US. The strategy is to explain how the "Industry" actually works, from the perspective of people on the inside of one of the most active Angel groups in the US (Central Texas Angels) and a highly experienced Angel investor (investments in 110 companies through Angel Groups). This is a chance to demystify a process which a lot of entrepreneurs see as too difficult to be worth doing---and to help get the broad-based US innovation economy back in gear again.

title: Humanoid Robots Are Clocking In: Meet Who’s Putting Them To Work
description: You’ve seen the viral humanoid demos—now meet the people actually shipping them. From hospitals to warehouses, humanoid robots are already on shift. They ride elevators, open doors, and work alongside people in busy environments. But making that leap from prototype to real-world, reliable performance? It’s hard. This panel brings together renowned founders and researchers who’ve built and deployed some of the first real humanoids at scale. Join us for a behind-the-scenes conversation about what's working, what’s not, and what it really takes to get robots out of the lab and into the world.

title: Legal 101 for Startups & Creative Entrepreneurs
description: Starting a business is exciting, but skipping legal basics can lead to expensive mistakes. In this session, attorney and founder Morgan Ipanema breaks down the essential steps to legally protect and scale your business. From choosing the right structure to safeguarding your brand and client relationships, this session gives women-led startups, creatives, and freelancers the tools to grow confidently—without the legal overwhelm.

title: Made for the Reply: CapCut and the Speed of Culture
description: Social media isn't a distribution channel, it's a living culture you're either part of or watching from the outside. This session explores how creators use Gen AI to participate in cultural moments they would've previously missed not just faster, but in entirely new ways. Two creators, two approaches, one shared problem: not missing the moment. From building AI templates that let thousands join a trend, to turning around reactive content in minutes, this is about what it actually means to show up in culture at the speed it moves.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: The New Arena: How Sports, Fans & Culture Are Evolving
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Sports fandom is evolving from passive viewership to active, always-on participation. Fans, Athletes, and Creators are reshaping how culture is made, shared, and celebrated. This session explores how brands can authentically tap into the new dynamics of fandom, from game days to off-seasons. Learn how Athletes are the new storytellers, how fan communities are driving cultural moments, and how marketers can show up with relevance and purpose in this new era of sports and engagement.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Orchestrate or Obsolete: Leading the AI-Powered Near Future
description: The problem isn’t AI — it’s our leadership model. We’re still leading like humans are the only ones in the room… as the room quietly fills with AI agents. In the very near future, every decision will be co-authored by humans, AI agents, and live ecosystem signals — and the leaders who master this orchestration will rewrite how business works.Near Futurist Neil Redding shows how leadership must evolve in the Agentic Era, from command and control to the orchestration of human–AI symbiosis. You’ll leave with a clear mental model for leading in systems where humans and AI participate together, plus daily leadership rituals for thriving as business capabilities evolve in real time.

title: Precision & Passion: The Modern Art of Brand Storytelling
description: From skincare routines grounded in clinical science to fitness journeys fueled by community, modern brands live inside consumers’ daily rituals. In a world shaped by real-time insight and AI-enhanced personalization, these marketing leaders will explore how they transform precision into passion — turning data-backed credibility and performance metrics into emotionally resonant stories. This conversation examines how technology can sharpen relevance, while human-centered storytelling builds trust, inspires action, and creates lasting connection.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: SXSW Pitch-Entertainment, Media, Sports & Creator Content
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Saturday, March 14, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Entertainment, Media, Sports & Creator ContentTechnologies and applications that are re-inventing the experience of leisure, information, sports, and entertainment. Industries these technologies address may include gaming, music, film, television, video, arts, mixed-reality events, news and publishing, e-sports, sports analytics, streaming, computer-directed reporting, podcasting and audio content, social media, virtual production, user-generated content platforms, digital fashion and avatars, children’s technology, digital storytelling, and more.Winner of the Entertainment, Media, Sports & Creator Content Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Time Travel & Archives: Reclaiming Memory Through XR
description: The panel brings together artists using immersive tools — from AR and AI to dance and gaming — to explore how we can reimagine archives, reclaim erased histories, and create portals into the past and future. Each of us approaches this through different mediums, but with a shared urgency around memory, embodiment, and speculative technology. The idea is to explore how immersive practices can act as a kind of time travel: allowing us to move through historical absence and presence, remix narratives, and build alternate realities where marginalized voices take center stage.

title: Weird FTW: What Happens When Brands Zig While Others Zag
description: Big brands have big budgets, but scrappy brands win by embracing "weird"—something their larger counterparts can’t replicate. This session unites "CPG Avengers": founders, creative leaders, and brand stewards who've built fanatic communities from scratch. They're forging businesses with great scale and impact in record time. Hear how they're redefining the GTM playbook by inventing their own. We'll cover everything from cults to clubhouses to coffins. These experts shaped modern brand-building and will reveal unconventional strategies that continue to outmaneuver giants.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Where Climate Science Meets Industry Innovation
description: Can industry partnerships lead to scientific breakthroughs? Can climate science support business interests? Partnerships lead to win-win outcomes: business needs sparking scientific “Eureka!” moments and science innovation supporting business decisions. Join the non-profit University Corporation for Atmospheric Research for a talk at the nexus of science and industry: a research institution connecting climate science to real-world users, a startup using quantum sensors to detect oil and gas leaks, and an exploration company realizing efficiencies and reducing emissions with new technologies.

title: WILD COURAGE: Go After What You Want & Get It
description: What if the traits you need to succeed are the exact opposite of what you’ve been told? To get ahead, you need to be Weird, Selfish, Shameless, Obsessed, Nosy, Manipulative, Brutal, Reckless, and Bossy. And that takes courage. NYT bestselling author and former Google exec Jenny Wood offers a bold, tactical playbook to ditch fear—of failure, uncertainty, and judgment by others—and apply these nine eyebrow-raising traits in a sane and savvy way to go after what you want and get it, whether you’re trying to snag a stretch promotion, impress your new boss, or land a life-changing deal.

title: Winning at Work: Why it Pays to be a Status Seeker
description: Status - one of the biggest determinants of our quality of life and career success - is poorly understood and rarely discussed. Professor Alison Fragale, organizational psychologist and bestselling author of Likeable Badass: How Women Get the Success They Deserve, sheds light on the science of status and its relationship to power in the workplace: what it is, why it matters, and how we leverage it for individual and collective success.

title: Audible Dimensions: Expanding Story Through Spatial Audio
description: As storytelling expands into extended reality media, such as immersive films, VR, MR, AI glasses and location-based installations, spatial audio is emerging as one of the key narrative forces. With today’s advanced head-mounted display technology, audiences expect not just 3D visuals but equally rich immersive sound that conveys presence, space and movement.This panel explores the latest innovation in immersive sound technology and practical application from three different perspectives: software and hardware capabilities of hosting platforms, the spatial audio formats and content production workflows that bring these experiences to life.

title: Beta Testing a City: Startups, Sidewalks & Robotics
description: What if a city could be beta tested like an app? In South Downtown Atlanta, tech entrepreneurs, investors, and community leaders are transforming 16 acres of historic real estate into a living lab for innovation, equity, and urban reinvention. This panel brings together the visionaries behind the movement to explore how startups, real estate, and even robot dogs are converging to reimagine what cities—and civic life—can look like in the 21st century.

title: Building an Artist Identity & Sustainable Content Strategy
description: Every artist has a story, but not every artist knows how to tell it consistently. This session explores how musicians can define their brand identity and build a sustainable content strategy that supports long-term growth. We’ll break down practical methods for clarifying your creative voice, designing visual and emotional cohesion, and developing a posting rhythm that amplifies your music without burning you out. Perfect for emerging and mid-level artists looking to strengthen their connection with fans, media, and industry partners.

title: Decoding Consciousness, Psychedelics, and the Mind
description: After a lifetime studying consciousness, famed neuroscientist Dr. Christof Koch had a profound, "no self, no memories, no time, no space, just terror and ecstasy" experience after deliberately taking a potent psychedelic. He argues, if we claim to be students of consciousness, we must explore its most dramatic manifestations.  This story launches into the most urgent questions of our time: What is consciousness, really? As AI rapidly advances and we grow complex "mini-brains" in labs, understanding what it means to "feel" or "be" is no longer just philosophy—it's critical for our future.

title: Escaping Algorithmic Binds: Creators vs. Corporate Platforms
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Creators and users are locked in digital “serfdom” by corporate social media’s algorithms and business models. Rabble, Bridget Todd, and Rudy Fraser will tackle how to break free from the feudal overlords of tech, and explain why Musk buying Twitter aided the move to decentralized apps. They’ll dig into new financial models, open protocols, and new community apps that empower creators and users without enriching platform owners. Learn how to create strategies that sustain social communities, and why open, user-first social apps are gaining in popularity with creators and audiences alike.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Jamie Lee Curtis. If Not Now, When, if Not Me, Who? Pivoting and Manifesting!
description: A conversation on how I am now a boss at 67!
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: Why Work Feels So Unfair and How Hard Conversations Help
description: Let's be honest: many managers would rather eat glass than tell you why you really didn't get the promotion, assignment, raise, or bonus. But their avoidance is creating a bigger problem - when leaders dodge difficult conversations, they create a vacuum where perceptions of unfairness flourish. Workplace expert Amy Gallo reveals how the inability (or unwillingness) to have hard conversations is eroding trust in organizations. You'll learn what transparency really looks like, how to demand the feedback you deserve, and why meritocracy is impossible without managers who can handle the heat.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: The Fundraising Playbook: What Founders Need to Know to Raise, Build, and Win in 2026
description: In today’s market, the challenge isn’t finding opportunities, it’s finding signal in the noise. Join Nizar Tarhuni, EVP of Research & Market Intelligence at PitchBook, and Heath Butler, Partner at Mercury Fund, for a data-driven conversation on where capital is flowing in 2026, the signals investors care about most, how AI is reshaping sourcing and diligence, and what current dynamics mean for fundraising and valuation strategy. Founders will gain a clear view into how investors are making decisions—and how to position their companies to stand out and get funded.

title: GenMedia 101: Unlocking Creativity with AI & Google DeepMind
description: A hands-on session for artists, storytellers, and makers of all kinds. Build visuals, music, and media experiences - no code required. Every creative idea starts with a feeling: a scene in your mind, a sound in your head, or a story you keep coming back to. This workshop is for anyone who wants to bring those ideas to life, with a little help from generative AI. You’ll explore how tools like Veo (for video), Lyria (for music), and Nano Banana (for images) can help you shape what you imagine into something you can actually see, hear, and share. Laptop & headphones/earbuds recommended.

title: Going Off Script: Filmmaking Through an Indigenous Lens
description: Cherokee Film sits down with leading Native American directors and writers to discuss breaking into the industry, working in Hollywood, what's up next for independent film, and what they're binge watching right now.

title: How the Ocean Twilight Zone Regulates Our Climate
description: The mesopelagic zone, or Ocean Twilight Zone, where light begins to disappear, is home to creatures that are crucial to regulating our climate through the largest migration on the planet that locks away 2-6 gigatons of carbon every year. With improving technology and increasing demand for fish protein, industry is exploring ways to exploit these deep-sea animals for uses like fish oil and pet food. This session will showcase the amazing animals that live in the deep, their role in regulating our planet’s climate, and international efforts to keep this vital carbon pump functioning like it should.

title: I Bottled My Mother: The Mrs. Meyer's Story by Monica Nassif
description: Monica Nassif didn’t just build a brand—she bottled a legacy. I Bottled My Mother is the gritty, hilarious, heartfelt origin story behind Mrs. Meyer’s Clean Day, the cult-favorite cleaning line inspired by Monica’s real-life mom, Thelma Meyer: a whip-smart, no-nonsense Midwesterner who raised nine children with little more than elbow grease, common sense, and a garden full of vegetables. Part startup manual, part memoir, this is the candid story of how one woman turned domestic skills into corporate strategy, and a small soap company into a household name.

title: Keep Calm & Flo On: Keeping an Enduring Icon Fresh & Funny
description: How do you take a character from one TV spot to icon and keep her relevant, beloved & funny for 15+ years?Join the creative team and the woman behind the white apron: Stephanie Courtney, AKA Flo. Alongside Progressive Insurance (brand), Arnold (agency) and Station (production) , she’ll help unpack how one ad grew into a universe of characters, comedic styles and unforgettable moments. From Flo’s personas, to red carpet parodies and blockbuster genre spoofs, this campaign has done it all. Expect BTS stories, comedic insights and lessons on longevity in an industry that often chases the new.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Look What You Made Me Do: Revenge as Creative Catalyst
description: Beyoncé did it. Kendrick did it. Taylor did it. Now it’s your turn. This immersive workshop (with a killer soundtrack) turns rejection, rivalry, and resentment into creative fire. From The Count of Monte Cristo to Kill Bill, we’ll channel iconic revenge arcs, neuroscience insights, and pop power plays. In doing so, we'll reframe past slights as story fuel, sharpen your personal or professional brand, and turn petty into power moves. Part life strategy, part anti-hero training camp, this is your chance to alchemize grudge into growth. Who said motivation had to be pure?

title: A New Era of Brand-Creator Partnerships
description: Successful brand–creator relationships have moved beyond one-off sponsored posts. Now, brands are building long-term collaborations grounded in authenticity — not just treating creators like media buys. Join Lia Haberman as she discusses the future of this evolving landscape with brand leaders Carly Gomez, Josh Line and John Sullivan. They’ll discuss current trends like co-creation and employee brand ambassador programs as well as the future of where they think these relationships are heading.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Own Your Sh*t (and Your IP): The Creator CEO Playbook
description: Most creators don’t get screwed in dramatic blowups. They sign their rights away quietly in contracts that look “standard.” In fact, 6 out of 10 creators don’t realize they’ve given up ownership until it’s already gone. This isn’t a legal lecture. It’s a hard reset on how creators should think about IP, licensing, and leverage before agreeing to the deal. The Creator CEO Playbook shows how to spot ownership traps early and stop trading long-term assets for one-time checks.

title: Protecting Visual Storytelling: Tools for Radical Transparency in a Post-Truth Era
description: Synthetic media (AI-generated photo and video) increasingly undermines public trust in news imagery at a time when democratic resilience depends on accurate, verifiable information. This panel unpacks the adaptation of “radical” transparency as a tool for re-establishing public trust and cultivating audience literacy in the face of AI’s growing influence. Visual journalists and strategists will tackle how to establish image integrity in an AI-saturated world; why robust attribution and transparency is a key path forward in rebuilding public trust and is a news sustainability practice.

title: Securing Agency: Ownership in the Era of AI
description: As AI agents begin to act on our behalf—signing contracts, moving value, and managing data—we face a new crisis of authority: How does an autonomous system prove it truly represents you? Ian Rogers, Ledger CXO, explores the evolution of ownership and control in an AI-native world. This session moves beyond protecting assets to securing human intent. Discover why cryptographic signing and hardware-backed control are the essential layers for a responsible agent economy. Learn how to embrace the massive potential of AI without sacrificing autonomy or the human root of trust.

title: Smarter Sports: AI, Data, and the Future of Fandom
description: AI is reshaping every inch of the $680 billion sports industry – from optimizing team operations to improving athlete performance and fueling global fandoms. What does the AI era of sports engagement look like? And how can organizations embrace breakthrough advancements without compromising the deeply human experience that make sports so compelling? In this session, we’ll go behind the scenes of the cutting-edge data and technology operation powering some of the most iconic sporting events in the world, and hear first-hand from teams and players who are changing the game from the inside out.

title: Space Technology and Innovation Conversation
description: Have an idea that could launch the space industry into its next era? Join a vibrant gathering of space tech pioneers, passionate enthusiasts, and established space professionals. This is your platform to pitch innovative concepts, receive feedback on new and expanding ideas, and forge powerful connections. Come to share, learn, and collaborate with the brightest minds charting the future of space.

title: SXSW Pitch-Enterprise and Future of Work
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Saturday, March 14, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.Enterprise and Future of WorkTechnologies and platforms that empower organizations to operate more efficiently, securely, and intelligently in an increasingly connected world—while reimagining how, where, and why work gets done. This category includes Enterprise SaaS solutions that drive scalable business operations across departments including sales and Human Resources. It also covers Vertical SaaS tailored to the specialized needs of a variety of industries. Startups in this space leverage AI/ML for Enterprise to power decision support systems, workflow automation, predictive analytics, and intelligent process augmentation. Additional focus areas include project management, human capital systems, APIs, knowledge management, cloud computing, MLOps, and AI-driven customer service—tools that collectively shape the future of work, business intelligence, and enterprise growth at scale. These innovations often focus on simplifying processes through innovative workflows and intelligent design, enabling more efficient and personalized user experiences across enterprise functions. Solutions may also incorporate personalization across enterprise functions, as well as platforms that support both businesses and individuals in navigating modern productivity demands.Winner of the Enterprise and Future of Work Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: User Centric Design & Funding of Accessible Tech
description: This panel will focus on user centered design (UCD) as the crux of successful product development - with inclusion at the core. Founders with disabilities will discuss their experiences developing technology and services - focusing on how they undertook UCD, identified testers in their communities, and ultimately - funded those initiatives and brought them to market.

title: Why You're Culturally Irrelevant (...And How to Change That)
description: Data is essential—but it can also be a hiding place. When we over-rely on dashboards, chase the same trends as competitors, and optimize for algorithms, we play it safe, yet lose relevance. There's a better approach for cultural resonance: stop chasing culture and begin shaping it. Through frameworks, case studies, how-to's and data, we'll explore how organizations can move beyond reactionary trend hijacking, and instead author preferred futures alongside communities. The most culturally resonant brands aren't chasing, they're making. What future will you build?
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Will Your Job Still Exist in Five Years? Redesigning Work in the Age of AI
description: In this 30-minute keynote, Michelle Schneider explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping not just jobs, but the very architecture of work. Moving beyond the simplistic narrative of “automation versus humans,” she examines how tasks are being reorganized, how expertise is being redefined, and what it truly means to design work in the age of intelligent systems.Drawing on two decades leading high-performing teams at global technology companies and her current work at the frontier of AI transformation, Michelle offers a clear and provocative framework for understanding what comes next — and how leaders can prepare.Following the keynote, Michelle will be joined by Ian Beacraft and journalist Cris Pelajo for a core conversation, expanding the dialogue and exploring practical, human-centered implications for organizations, workers, and society.

title: Women in Film & Television Austin Hosts Connect & Collaborate
description: Women in Film & Television Austin hosts Connect & Collaborate, a meet-and-greet meant to be a fun pause in the day where creatives come together. It’s about conversation and community.

title: Your First 90 Days as a Creator from Experts at Meta, YouTube & Snapchat
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: In this rare cross platform conversation, moderated by acclaimed creator strategist Antonia Alakija, creator partnerships leaders from Meta, YouTube & Snapchat come together to break down what creators should focus on in their first 90 days of taking content seriously. We’ll explore how fast growing creators structure early momentum, the common pitfalls that derail progress & strategies for building an audience with intention. From platform-specific insights to proven growth frameworks, this session offers an honest tactical roadmap for turning creative potential into a lasting personal brand.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Creativity, Commerce & Chaos: Tech & Indie Filmmaking
description: As AI transforms how we create and consume media, quiet revolutions are happening behind the scenes: creators, producers, agents, and technologists are building the infrastructure to track, protect, and monetize creativity across its entire lifecycle. This panel brings together a first-time filmmaker, a veteran distributor, a top agent, and an AI platform CEO to explore how attribution, metadata, royalty tracking, and ethical packaging are becoming essential to creative business.

title: The Death of Passive Entertainment–A Future for Participants
description: Pop culture isn't just consumed—it's lived. A song or movie will spark IRL activity like themed workouts, sold-out dining experiences or fan meetups (e.g. Love Island events +4,950% in the past year). This shift from passive watching to active gathering reshapes culture and moves people–physically. Fandoms are inspiring creator-led experiences, turning local spaces into cultural venues overnight. In this panel Julia Hartz (Eventbrite), Dani Calogera (What/If), Kate Wolff (Lupine Creative) & David Salazar (Fast Company) explore how pop culture becomes a springboard for real connection.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Exotic Thinking: Outsmarting AI with Human Creativity
description: In the foresight world, staying brilliant in the Age of the Algorithm demands more than just tech fluency—it requires radical creativity. In this fireside chat, technofuturist Sarah DaVanzo and AI pioneer Miro Dimitrov explore how we can outthink AI using paradox, taboo, and cognitive disobedience. Drawing from Sarah’s forthcoming book Exotic Thinking, the session offers a provocative path to rewiring human ingenuity in a future shaped by machines.

title: Featured Session: A Conversation with Garry Tan
description: Hear from the President & CEO of Y Combinator. In addition to his Featured Session, Garry Tan will co-emcee the SXSW Pitch 2026 Award Ceremony.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: How Crypto is Building New Capital Markets for Everyone, not just Wall Street
description: For years, crypto was criticized as a solution searching for a problem. The on-chain creator economy failed to gain momentum, and the concept of ownership reflected less on community governance and more on self-custody. But something shifted. As crypto activity became increasingly financialized, the infrastructure built to support it quietly unlocked something bigger—new pathways for creators and everyday users to participate in capital markets once reserved for institutions. Join Pedro Miranda, Head of Consumer at the Solana Foundation, and Rodolfo Gonzalez from Foundation Capital as we examine what's changed in the on-chain economy and explore the emerging consumer crypto landscape that's democratizing access to financial opportunity.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Mythical Model: Next Gen Creator-Brand Partnerships
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Digital ad spend will hit $600 billion this year, with brands expected to shift more budget to creators. But how can creators themselves tap this massive opportunity? Rhett & Link, legendary creators and hosts of Good Mythical Morning, will discuss their innovative brand partnership model — built on 360 campaigns, programmatic ads, sponsorships, direct integrations, and the latest brand matching technology — and how other creators can unlock new revenue opportunities to create lasting careers.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: An Influential Conversation with Jordan "The Stallion"
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Join us for an engaging fireside chat featuring Influential Chief Communications Officer, Chris Detert, in conversation with one of today’s most recognizable digital personalities, Jordan “The Stallion” Howlett. Known for his infectious energy, unfiltered honesty, and viral storytelling across platforms like TikTok, Jordan has become a leading voice in the creator economy. From his signature food reviews to his candid takes on everyday life, he’s built a devoted community by staying true to himself—while navigating the business of brand deals, audience engagement, and online growth.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Is Reinforcement Learning the Real Future of AI?
description: While AGI, generative AI, and humanoid robots dominate the spotlight, reinforcement learning (RL) is quietly powering the next chapter of AI. Unlike other methods, RL enables systems to learn by doing – adapting through trial and error in dynamic environments. In this session, one of the world’s leading RL minds will explore why this approach is essential for building truly autonomous, intelligent systems, and how today’s breakthroughs are laying the groundwork for the next wave of transformative AI.

title: Keeping It Real: AI, Human Data, and the Future of Brand Connections
description: Brands are racing to adopt efficient, prompt-driven systems. But as we continue building a world optimized for machines, how do brands stay meaningful to people?Join Anastassia Predekha, Global Consumer & Marketplace Insights Manager for Nestlé and Billy Jones, CMO of Hootsuite powered by TalkwalkerAI, as they share their thoughts on navigating artificial intelligence in a human world.This isn’t just another debate about AI hype. It will be a real conversation about where AI helps brands understand people better—and where it quietly creates trust, culture, or reputation risks in ways leaders can’t ignore.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The New Cool: Choosing Moderation in a World of Excess
description: New generations are redefining consumption, choosing balance, moderation, meaning, and authenticity over excess. Using Georgetown University’s case study of AB InBev’s Global Smart Drinking Goals, this session explores how brands can leverage marketing expertise and innovations that meet consumers’ desire for more balanced choices, such as lower and no-alcohol beer, and influence to shape social norms and create shared value. The discussion highlights how purpose-driven strategies can drive behavior change, reinforce positive behaviors, promote moderation, and deliver sustainable growth.

title: OpenAI & Spurs: Building Human-Centered AI for Fans, Partners, and Communities
description: How do you scale AI without losing the human touch? In this session, leaders from the San Antonio Spurs and OpenAI share how they’re using AI to create more meaningful experiences for fans, partners, and communities. From personalized fan engagement and family-focused experiences to internal productivity and decision-making, the Spurs offer a real-world look at how purpose-built AI tools are being used on and off the court. Attendees will walk away with practical lessons on responsible AI adoption, concrete use cases from a global sports franchise, and a blueprint for putting people first while bringing cutting-edge technology to the courtside.

title: Reinvention Not Repetition: Shifting the Innovation Mindset
description: In a disruptive era, innovation needs to be agile, inclusive, and driven by purpose. It’s essential to move quickly and address issues by transitioning from endless MVP cycles to iterative approaches that integrate technology with human-centered design, ethics, and resilience. This panel discusses how founders, creators, and corporate innovators can leverage AI, automation, and digital collaboration to balance profit with purpose, support internal disruptors, and foster cultures where innovation delivers genuine value.

title: SXSW Pitch-Innovative World Tech
description: The SXSW Pitch provides a platform for early-stage technology startups to present their groundbreaking products and services to a panel of distinguished industry experts, influential media professionals, and leading venture capitalists and angel investors.On Saturday, March 14, five innovative companies will take the stage in Salon FGH at the JW Marriott, showcasing their ideas, innovations, and entrepreneurial vision before a live audience and judging panel. Come be inspired by the next generation of tech innovators shaping the future.This category is for any creative and innovative application, product, or service that does not fit in another SXSW Pitch category, as well as technologies that act as the infrastructure that new technologies are built on. Examples that might fit into this category include (but are not limited to) societal good, biodegradable materials, decentralized energy grids, neuromorphic computing, social media platforms, aerospace, nanotechnology, pet tech, quantum computing and computer chip technology, and others.Winner of the Innovative World Technologies will be announced at the Award Ceremony, Saturday, March 14th at 6:30pm.

title: Where Data Meets Creativity: Making Brave Brand Calls
description: In an era of dashboards, real-time metrics, and predictive analytics, it’s easy to mistake data for direction. But transformative brand moments don’t come from simply following what the numbers say; they come from leaders who interpret data through an informed and insightful lens — who see patterns others miss, ask better questions, and make bold decisions that single data points alone would never justify. This panel explores how modern CMOs use data not as a safety net, but as a springboard. Join us for a conversation with marketing visionaries — John Livanos, Krista Dalton, Eric Liedtke and Clayton Ruebensaal — who have leveraged data for creative advantage.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Augmented IQ: Scaling Human + AI Potential
description: Too many leaders today use AI to automate yesterday. Some use AI to do their thinking. But if we don't think critically and creatively, if we don’t define how we compete more effectively with AI, then we leave it to AI slop and cognitive Darwinism to shape our future. This is a moment for mastering AIQ: the Augmented Intelligence Quotient. The best leaders master IQ, EQ, and SQ. AIQ is the practice of becoming exponentially smarter with AI, to augment our thinking and output to achieve what wasn’t possible before AI. In this special session, Brian Solis, Futurist, author, Head of Global Innovation at ServiceNow will share a mindshift map to move from efficiency to augmented possibility, to automate what should be automated, and augment what only humans can imagine to reshape the future.

title: Big Audience, Bigger Feeling: How Brands Create Emotional Resonance
description: Big audiences don’t guarantee emotional connection. The brands breaking through today are those that intentionally design feelings such as belonging, joy, identity, and celebration that scale across millions of people. This panel brings together leaders from travel, fitness, and QSR to explore how emotional resonance is engineered through customization, community design, and cultural fluency. From large-scale experiences to everyday rituals, these executives will share how brands turn reach into relevance and why bigger audiences require even greater intentionality
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Collab Culture: Turning One-Off Brand Deals into Lasting Partnerships
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: In today’s content landscape, creators aren’t just making videos—they’re building universes. Long-form, narrative-driven programming is emerging as a way for creators to tell richer stories while incorporating brands in ways that feel natural and engaging. Creators aren’t just starring in the content—they’re producing the :15 and :30 ads that live across the platform. This session explores how creators can develop authentic brand partnerships that align with their vision, scale their careers, and transform one-off deals into long-term relationships built on trust, creativity, and shared values. Attendees will hear directly from top creators about why these deep partnerships are redefining how modern audiences connect with advertising that values authenticity and substance over surface-level promotion.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Conversation Culture: A Live AEO/AI Search Audit for Brands
description: Search is no longer transactional. It’s conversational, AI-driven, and increasingly shaped by how Answer Engines interpret brands. In this live workshop, Avenue Z and Profound will conduct real-time AEO Brand Audits to show how brands appear across ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and other AI-native experiences. You will learn how AI Search influences demand, reputation, and revenue, and leave with an actionable audit framework and tactics to understand, control, and scale how your brand shows up in the zero-click internet.

title: From NPC to Main Character: The Power of Belonging
description: Cultural conversations around “main character energy” and a loneliness epidemic have made some feel like NPCs in the game of life. But what drives someone to join a Twitch stream or wear a jersey to a bar? Belonging. Fandoms aren’t mindless obsessions — they’re identity-shaping communities that turn disconnected observers into players. This talk explores how brands can drive growth through belonging by inviting people in through storytelling, access, and shared imagination—turning passive audiences into passionate communities.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Future of Storytelling in the Age of AI
description: Why is everyone still reading the same article when no two readers are alike? This session challenges the future of storytelling in AI—where the promise isn’t cheaper content, but stories as personal as your own fingerprint.Led by MasterClass founder and CEO David Rogier, this conversation will challenge assumptions about creativity, technology, and culture—and invite the audience to imagine a future where AI helps us tell the most human stories of all: our own.

title: Innovators Today and the Future of Play
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: For generations, play has shaped how we imagine, connect, and create. Today it spans toys, gaming, anime, sports, and emerging tech, blending physical and digital worlds into shared experiences. As new technologies, platforms, and global fandoms accelerate, play is being redefined in real time. Play is no longer confined to a toy aisle, a console, or a screen; it now unfolds across worlds, driven by creativity, community, and connection. Join industry innovators exploring how breakthrough technologies, powerhouse partnerships, and mixed-world experiences are shapin g the future of play, creativity, and culture.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Prompt to Publish - AI Tools for the Modern Content Creator
description: Stop fighting the blank page and start building leverage. In this hands-on workshop, learn to build a personalized Content Operating System using Claude and Gemini. Move past robotic templates by building a Custom Brand Skill, validate ideas by roleplaying with your target audience, and discover how AI agents can act as your on-demand production crew. Walk away with a fully customized AI Co-Pilot that scales your content, analyzes your retention data, and eliminates creative burnout.

title: Resistance into Momentum: Leading Through Reluctant Change
description: Change isn’t optional—but engagement is. In this energizing talk, change strategist Jeff Skipper reveals how leaders can turn organizational resistance into momentum. With stories from Fortune 100 transformations and fast-growing companies, Jeff shows why most change fails (hint: it’s not the process) and how to co-create clarity, alignment, and commitment—even in reluctant teams. Walk away with tools to lead change with precision and humanity, and yes—maybe even a little grace.

title: Retail as a Stage: Merch, Culture & Artist Identity
description: Retail has become more than a revenue stream. It’s a cultural touchpoint and a powerful platform for artist marketing. This panel explores how brands and bands collaborate to extend an artist’s presence far beyond the stage through merch, in-store activations, exclusive drops, and co-branded campaigns. Hear how retail partnerships are shaping music fandom, fueling discovery, and embedding artists deeper into culture. We’ll break down how artists are leveraging retail to build lasting fan engagement.

title: SXSW Pitch Alumni: Where Are They Now?
description: Past winners of the SXSW Pitch competition are back to share how their lives and entrepreneurial journeys have changed since winning on the SXSW Pitch stage.
