Catch-up on their latest endeavors, hear directly from the winning founders, and learn how they leveraged the SXSW Pitch platform to take their startups to the next level.

title: We’re on the Cusp of Communicating with Whales
description: Chasing one of the most ambitious scientific discoveries of our time, a group of biologists, linguists, roboticists, & legal scholars have joined forces to understand what animals are saying to each other & devise a way to talk to them. Leveraging state-of-the-art technology, including a groundbreaking machine learning engine, Project CETI scientists have made huge progress decoding the communication of sperm whales. We will explore the impact of interspecies communication & what it means for humanity, the legal rights of animals, animal intelligence, and the future of conservation.

title: Why Most Companies Will Never Be AI Native
description: AI-native has become the goal, but for many organizations it’s unrealistic or unnecessary. This conversation will look honestly at what AI-native really means and why teams get stuck trying to achieve it. We’ll explore the technical, organizational, and human bottlenecks that hold companies back, and how strong leaders decide when to invest, when to adapt, and when to walk away. A grounded discussion about tradeoffs, judgment, and practical AI outcomes.

title: Wide Open: The Immersive Video Opportunity
description: I know, I know: you’ve seen it all before. In 2016-2020, many people thought VR would be *the thing*. It didn’t happen, and you’ve moved on. We didn’t. See: VR is cool, actually. Talk to any creative, any filmmaker: they want in. Why? BECAUSE IT’S AWESOME! Come to this talk and learn how the latest cameras and headsets *finally* make it easy to create mind-blowing experiences. I’ll show you a few projects that came out recently, share some audience numbers - and you’ll get why Immersive Videos are the most undervalued creative opportunity at the moment!

title: You Can’t Create Cultural Relevance Without Community
description: For marketers, being part of culture is no longer optional. As community-led discovery overtakes search monoculture, smart brands recognize that communities don’t want sponsors; they want neighbors. 65% of consumers say they’re more loyal to brands that foster meaningful connections. This panel explores the difference between relevance and rejection and demonstrates how showing up with humility, consistency, and value turns audiences into collaborators, unlocking cultural traction that drives lasting loyalty.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Access to Capital for the World's Underbanked Entrepreneurs
description: "Access to Capital for the World's Underbanked Entrepreneurs" will be a creative discussion centered on how to expand financial access for underbanked and underserved entrepreneurs around the world – helping to create local jobs and economic mobility for founders from Detroit to Nairobi to the Za'atari refugee camp and beyond. Two award-winning social entrepreneurs and human rights leaders will discuss their work on the ground in the U.S., Africa, and Asia to connect women, refugee, and low-income individuals to the capital they need to launch and grow life-changing micro-enterprises.

title: AI Killed Your Website Traffic (And What to do About it)
description: Marketing is experiencing its most significant disruption in 20 years. With 60% of searches resulting in no clicks and AI set to overtake traditional search by 2028, the old playbook is expired. From the creators of inbound marketing, join HubSpot CMO Kipp Bodnar and growth strategist Steph Smith for a special live recording of the podcast, “Marketing Against the Grain,” where they’ll unveil a new methodology to help businesses shift from controlling traffic to maximizing conversion, from providing answers to enabling actions, and from mass marketing to AI-personalization at scale.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The AI Panel: Or What Being an Apocaloptimist Looks Like
description: Meet the Academy Award® winning team behind the documentary THE AI DOC: OR HOW I BECAME AN APOCALOPTIMIST as they unpack the complexities around AI – the promise, the peril and what to do now. A vital exploration of the revolutionary technology and the people behind it — from the major figures fast-tracking its development to the ones warning us about its dangers — the film is at once a thrilling primer, a call to attention, and an intimate personal story about what it means to look toward an uncertain future. Panelists include producers Daniel Kwan, Ted Tremper, Diane Becker and moderated by Kristy Puchko, Entertainment Editor at Mashable.

title: Belonging is Built: How Brands Earn Their Place is Culture
description: Sponsorship no longer guarantees relevance. Today, brands must earn their place inside the communities they hope to reach. This panel explores how leaders across categories build cultural credibility by embedding themselves in real ecosystems from sports and creator communities to street-level rituals.  These executives will share how participation, proximity, and purpose-driven partnerships transform brands from observers into contributors and why belonging is more powerful than visibility.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Brains & Bots: Evolving the Future of Intelligence Together
description: Does offloading thinking to AI cause cognitive atrophy or free our brains to think about more complex problems? That’s the million-neuron question. This provocative panel explores “adaptive intelligence”, where human and machine learning loops converge. A neuroscientist, futurist, and AI visionary unpack how co-learning systems, neuromorphic design, and spatial computing can amplify creativity, decision-making, and human agency in a world where algorithms don’t just support us, they shape us.

title: The Cold Outreach Playbook for Tech Sales
description: Standing out in a crowded inbox or on a cold call is one of the toughest challenges in B2B sales. In this session, Glenn Kramon, Pulitzer Prize winning New York Times editor and Stanford Business School instructor, shares proven communication strategies that help sales professionals earn attention and credibility with decision-makers. You’ll learn how to open with impact, tailor messages to business priorities, remove unnecessary jargon, and strike the right balance of professionalism and authenticity to drive meaningful responses.

title: Digital Dopamine: The High-Performance Edge of Habit-Building
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: This panel will dissect the deliberate psychological and technological architecture that transforms fitness adherence into a captivating, long-term habit. We will explore how the calculated tension between AI optimization and the powerful, personal connection with human instructors and communities creates a highly effective system of lasting behavior change ultimately, increasing healthspan.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Eyewitness to Power: Citizen Journalism, Viral Video & Accountability
description: Armed with a phone: This panel explores how everyday people—armed only with cell phones—have become primary witnesses to state violence and how their footage reshapes public narrative and justice processes. #WhileBlack shows both the moral power and unpredictable personal cost of such witnessing. While the recent video of the Minneapolis ICE shooting underscores how recordings can surface in real time to both inform and inflame national debate, #WhileBlack, is also set in Minneapolis and it explores the impact of witnessing on the brave citizen journalists who are increasingly becoming harbingers of the truth.  Who gets to record, who gets to be believed, who gets protection, and how do social platforms’ monetize the impact of visceral, real-world events? #WhileBlack powerfully examines some of the most viral murders and beatings by police over generations through the lens of technology.Trauma in the digital age: Repeated viewing of viral moments of racial violence takes a deeply human toll, not only on the witnesses themselves but on the communities who see themselves reflected in those moments again and again. This panel investigates how does the constant replay of these moments affect mental health, civic engagement, and collective resilience? The Ethics of Watching: What does responsible viewing look like, and how do we educate audiences to engage ethically with real-world suffering?

title: Featured Session: Social Media Masterclass 2026 with Ex-YouTube & Instagram Insider
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Great content starts with great ideas. In this talk, you’ll learn how to find better ideas for your social posts from Jon Youshaei who worked inside Instagram and YouTube for 8 years. Jon used this process to grow his audience to 1M+ followers while being featured in TIME, Fast Company, and Inc magazine for “cracking the code on how to go viral.” No more running out of ideas. No more waiting for inspiration. Whether you’re trying to grow on Instagram, YouTube, LinkedIn, TikTok, or X, this talk outlines a repeatable process to grow your audience — and become a respected voice in your niche.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: From Pitch to Pilot: How Corporates Partner and Build
description: Startups are often pitched on “strategic partnerships” with large companies, but most never progress beyond a pilot. This panel features innovation leaders from a healthcare innovation and venture arm, a mobility and sustainability-focused enterprise, and a national restaurant brand redefining service through digital innovation. Together, they’ll unpack how corporates build new ventures, and what it takes for founders to gain traction and create lasting value with enterprise partners.

title: How to Manage AI Anxiety at Work
description: As AI transforms how we work and lead, it’s not just a technical revolution—it’s an emotional one. In this session, Harvard Business Review author Morra Aarons-Mele explores how current and emerging leaders can manage AI anxiety, build psychological safety for themselves and their teams, and keep humans at the center of innovation. Attendees will learn to navigate fear and resistance with empathy, reframe anxiety as fuel for growth, and lead with clarity, flexibility, and courage through change.

title: How to Not Screw Up Your Startup–Answering Legal Questions
description: Startup-savvy attorneys bring their real-world experience to SXSW in a fast-paced legal Q&A packed with insight, strategy, and storytelling. From navigating AI patents and trademark showdowns to managing sketchy investors and sudden privacy crises, they’ll tackle complex legal issues with humor and clarity. Attendees get tailored advice, practical tools, and a Startup Legal Survival Kit—no billable hours required. Think speed-dating, but for the legal hurdles every founder faces.

title: IndieMeme South Asian Film Meet Up
description: Indie Meme, Texas’s only South Asian film festival, welcomes South Asian and allied filmmakers, writers, producers, actors, and film enthusiasts to this informal meetup. This gathering is a space to share insights, best practices, and lived experiences across the film industry—locally, nationally, and globally—while engaging with questions of growth, representation, and an uncertain future. Whether your passion is new-wave, non-commercial Parallel Cinema, genre-blending Masala films, or the joyous kitsch of Bollywood, you’ll find a community of like-minded creatives, new resources, and, of course, Texas snacks.The meetup will feature brief introductions, guided prompts, and opportunities to build transnational partnerships in film and filmmaking. Filmmakers and enthusiasts, alike, welcomed. Together, we’ll build relationships and spark collaborations that last well beyond the SXSW season.

title: The Next Evolution of XR & Shared Realities in Entertainment
description: Iconic worlds are being reimagined across XR and shared-reality formats, giving audiences new ways to step inside the stories they love. Bringing together leaders from Meta, Universal Pictures, Nexus Studios and Cosm, we’ll explore how spatial storytelling is evolving as major IP moves into these new environments – and what’s ahead as these mediums step from the bleeding-edge into the mainstream. The panel will unpack what audiences are responding to, how scalable business models are emerging across platforms, and the new creative opportunities this opens up for worldbuilders.

title: Resilience Through Innovation: AI at NPR and KQED
description: The loss of federal funding and AI's reshaping of the media landscape present risks and opportunities for public media and public service journalism. This session features a transparent conversation between a local and national leader who are experimenting with GAI in ways that maintain trust with the audience, center the work and judgment of journalists, and augment storytelling and engagement. Join us for a lively discussion focused on transparency, ethics and how organizations can advocate for the sustainability, impact, and continued production of high-quality, human-centered journalism.

title: Unreasonable by Nature: The Psychology of Consumer Choice
description: As much as we'd like to think that splurgy new car and our Prime Day shopping carts are the fruits of reason, purchases big and small aren’t governed by logic. People purchase based on who they are, how they feel and what surrounds them at the time. Humans are 'Unreasonable by Nature,' and this panel will illustrate how to grow brands by aligning with the true psychological drivers of consumer decisions. Expert academics from leading American business schools, Ryan Hamilton, Alix Barasch and Keisha Cutright, will explore how rational marketing tactics often fail where unreasonable appeals win.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Who Owns Me? Legal War Over Identity in the Creator Economy
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Who owns your face, your voice, your following? In the creator economy, your identity is currency but also your biggest legal risk. This session exposes how influencers, founders, and professionals are losing control of their name, image, and likeness (NIL) in business disputes, brand deals, and even divorce. With deepfakes and Al cloning now part of the mix, you'll learn how to lockdown your identity before someone else monetizes it. If you are the brand, you better own it. Your face may be famous, but it's also vulnerable.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Wired for Change: How AI Is Supercharging Clean Energy
description: The transition to renewable energy systems presents unprecedented challenges for power system reliability and financial stability during the integration of clean energy sources. AI technology transforms the ability of utilities and investors and technologists to predict renewable energy output while maintaining grid stability and securing funding for advanced energy infrastructure. The panel unites experts from the technology and finance sectors with industry professionals and policymakers to examine AI's practical effects on future energy systems.

title: Art to Infrastructure: Creating a Company as an Artist
description: Join actor Billy Magnussen and the co-founder of his production company HappyBad Bungalow, alongside representatives from artist-led companies Madhouse Films and Not Your Daddy’s Films, for a candid conversation on building and sustaining a company as an artist.

title: The City That Plays: Digital Twins Reimagining LA & Beyond
description: Los Angeles is a vibrant playground for innovation where data, design, and community imagination converge. Powered by cutting-edge Earth-observing satellite systems, digital twins are transforming how LA plans for resilience, equity, and the joy of the games. Join us to explore groundbreaking projects—from wildfire recovery to creating green space networks—that leverage user-friendly immersive technology to connect AI, local stories, and spatial data. Discover how these immersive tools are shaping a smarter, more resilient city ready for the Olympics and the complex challenges ahead.

title: Culture Meets Commerce: Building the Next $1B Brand
description: Emma Grede, serial entrepreneur and multiple-time billion-dollar brand founder, joins Alison Furman, PwC US Consumer Markets Industry Partner, and Dallas Dolen, PwC US Technology, Media & Telecommunications Industry Partner, for an engaging panel discussion on how culture and commerce collide to create the next billion-dollar product category.The discussion will explore how cultural relevance, technology, and consumer behavior intersect to help shape emerging brands, and what today’s business leaders should understand to build, scale, and sustain the next generation of category-defining companies.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Data Equity Core Conversation
description: As data becomes the raw material of modern life, how do we ensure it's collected, shared, and used in ways that build, not break, trust? This structured meet-up invites technologists , policymakers, and community advocates to explore responsible data practices across industries. Together, we’ll reflect on the disappearance of public data, rising mistrust in institutions, and what it means to build for integrity, transparency, and equity. Participants will share challenges and insights, contribute to a collective best practices, and connect with others shaping the future of ethical data use.

title: The Founder’s Edge: How Psychedelics Unlock Creativity, Resilience, and Conscious Leadership
description: Startup founders face relentless pressure, emotional volatility, and decision fatigue. This session explores how intentional psychedelic experiences, combined with integration practices, can expand perspective, dissolve ego-driven blind spots, and strengthen leadership presence. Drawing from The Mind Shift Network’s work with executives and entrepreneurs, we’ll examine how psychedelics catalyze authentic innovation, emotional intelligence, and purpose-driven company culture.

title: From Function to Feeling: The Brand Is the Product
description: When features compete, feeling wins. In categories defined by parity and endless choice, functional differentiation is rarely enough. This panel explores how brand meaning, storytelling, and cultural connection transform products into emotional experiences that drive preference. Bringing together leaders across industries, the conversation examines how identity, memory, and participation influence purchase decisions.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: From Monk to Manager: Zen Lessons for Loving Your Work
description: By most accounts Jackson Kerchis has had an unusual career path: he launched a (failed) fintech startup, created the world's first major in happiness, lived 4 months as a Zen monk, spent 2 years in management, and then founded a consulting firm researching happiness at work. This session combines the art of Zen with insights from the social sciences to help you craft a new way of work. Even if you don't have your "dream job," you'll learn the ingredients for what makes for an engaging and satisfying work experience.

title: From Niche to Noticed: New Paths for Indie Films
description: You made a great film. Now what? With streamers scaling back, traditional buyers pulling out, and theatrical costs soaring, indie filmmakers are left navigating a broken and uncertain distribution pipeline. This session explores bold, alternative release strategies—grassroots outreach, community-driven campaigns, eventized theatrical runs, digital-first models, and more—that are helping meaningful, niche stories find their audiences. We’ll examine the macro industry shifts behind the disruption and spotlight the innovative approaches rewriting the independent film playbook.

title: The Hour That’ll Define 2026: Culture, Trends, & What’s Next
description: Most trend reports feel like homework. Not this one.This is an hour of zero effort, all takeaways.Chris Danton and Kirsten Ludwig have spent the year decoding what’s actually happening across culture, F&B, Silvers, Alphas, retail, tech, wellness, beauty, and travel for GOOD THINKING — the newsletter 16K+ executives at Target, LVMH, and Klaviyo wait for every week.Now they’re bringing you the distilled version:10 trends that matter most for 2026 — delivered with pace, perspective, and zero patience for the obvious.You’ll leave knowing exactly what to do on Monday — and how to think for the year ahead.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How Creators Teach: Turning Knowledge Into Income and Community
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: ​Today’s leading creators aren’t just entertaining; they’re teaching and sharing information. From wellness challenges and cohort-based coaching to digital courses and paid memberships, creator-led learning has become one of the most sustainable ways to grow audience, revenue, and build community.In this session, you’ll learn which learning formats from cohorts to in-person retreats are working now (and which have burned out). Panelists will break down how to design and monetize educational content that builds loyalty, whether you’re launching your first paid challenge, growing a coaching business, or integrating brand partners into your community. You’ll leave with a framework for launching your own “creator classroom”, one that deepens connection and drives consistent income.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How to Avoid Spreading Misinformation on Your Platform
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: It’s no secret that today’s media landscape is more fractured than ever. As trust in legacy media declines, people are consuming news in less traditional ways and, instead, often turn to content creators for the latest news, commentary, and connection. But how can they balance influence and responsibility in such a fast-paced digital ecosystem?Join industry-leading creators and strategists in conversation to explore how, in a digital world driven by clicks and engagement, you can communicate responsibly online. We’ll dive into how to build and leverage community, recognize and preempt false narratives, and turn complex realities into sharable content while engaging your audience authentically.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Immigrant Founders: Fueling America’s Future
description: Immigration is a contentious and tumultuous topic these days. But the contributions of global talent are clear: Immigrants make up only 15% of the U.S. population, but 40% of Nobel science laureates and 44% of tech unicorn founders. Meltem Ballan came from Turkey to the U.S. for her PhD, and co-founded a company that makes modular AI data centers. Jenny Yu came from China for her MBA, and now her startup helps phase out forever chemicals in the supply chain. Doug Rand has helped global talent navigate the immigration system as a senior government official, co-founder of Boundless, and now head of a philanthropic fund. While many recent immigration policies may create obstacles for global founders, there are still several viable pathways to obtain a visa and start a company in the U.S. Come learn how America can remain the top destination for great immigrant entrepreneurs.

title: It Writes Itself: AI Variety Show & Creative Workshop
description: Based on the “mind-expanding” AI variety show that sold out in NYC, LA, and CDMX, and fresh off of runs at Meta Connect, Disney, and Unilever, IT WRITES ITSELF is “an AI creativity workshop for people who hate workshops.” Part playground, part performance, it’s an interactive mash-up of AI experiments, games, stories and surprises tailored to the folks in the room, resulting in a fun, funny, weird, and occasionally existential workshop like none other. You’ll leave with your brain rewired, curiosity lit up, and a newfound way to explore AI that’s actually fun.

title: Jurassic Warnings: What Dinosaurs Knew About the End of the World
description: What can a 65-ton dinosaur teach us about the climate crisis and how we care for our health today? In this dynamic session, world-renowned paleontologist Dr. Kenneth Lacovara, who discovered Dreadnoughtus – one of the largest land animals to ever walk the Earth; and bestselling physician-scientist Dr. William Li, explore how the fossils hold urgent lessons for our future. From the extinction of giants like Dreadnoughtus to fossilized evidence of diseases like cancer, they reveal how Earth's deep past can help us understand modern threats to humans – and what we can do about them.

title: Lose Your Mind to AI? A Neuroscientist and an AI CEO Debate
description: AI can be your most powerful productivity tool… or a shortcut to cognitive decline. Neuroscientist Dr. Sahar Yousef and AI education company CEO Greg Shove debate the ultimate question: “AI feels like borrowing 30 IQ points, but will it ultimately make us dumber?” They will share new, unpublished UC Berkeley data showing that most “AI augmentation” is shallow cognitive offloading in disguise and outline a research-backed playbook for HOW you can leverage AI without losing what makes you most valuable: your mind.

title: Meet the Winners of the 2026 SXSW Pitch Competition
description: This session gives attendees the opportunity to hear more from the leaders behind the extremely talented startups that won each of the nine categories at SXSW Pitch 2026. Be inspired and amazed by these incredible entrepreneuers from aroud the world, as they showcase their winning ideas and shine a spotlight on the most innovative technologies of tomorrow.

title: More Pavlov, Less Badges: Gamification Isn't What You Think
description: Before there were prize wheels, badges, and leaderboards, there was a scientist and his dogs. Join gamification experts Adam Messing and Kinley Clifford to talk about the common misconceptions around gamification and how understanding behavioral and motivational science is the key to unlocking it. This interactive workshop will be jam-packed with fun and useful exercises and games.

title: Operating Systems of the 3rd Millennium: AI + XR + NEURO
description: Neural interfaces are entering a new phase where AI algorithms read brain signals in real-time and power adaptive feedback loops. This panel convenes OpenBCI and cross-disciplinary experts to show how AI turns raw brain signals into actionable insights, powers real-time closed-loop haptics and visuals, and scales from lab demos to consumer and research-grade neurotech. We’ll dissect technical hurdles, safety imperatives, and the roadmap to trustworthy neurotechnology.

title: Reality Hacked: Tech, Story, and the Future of Experience
description: What if the real world had hidden story portals waiting to be found? This panel explores an ongoing collaboration between Meow Wolf and Niantic that uses AR, geolocation, and immersive storytelling to transform physical places into layered, interactive experiences. From sidewalks to city parks, spaces become stages for narrative discovery, inviting people to move, play, and connect in new ways. Dennis Hwang (Niantic), Vince Kadlubek (Meow Wolf), and Scott Stein (CNET) join moderator Kati Murphy to share what happens when imagination meets infrastructure—and the streets start telling stories.

title: South by South America: The Rise of Southern Brazil Tech
description: Southern Brazil is emerging as a top innovation hub. This session explores how Rio Grande do Sul combines elite talent and AI infrastructure to attract global capital. Attendees will learn: (1) why investors are shifting to resilient emerging markets; (2) strategies for scaling tech in collaborative ecosystems; and (3) how to tap into a world-class engineering pipeline. Join us to discover the playbook of Brazil’s most innovative state and gain insights on navigating the next wave of South American tech growth.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Star‑Powered Startups: Hits, Misses & Messes
description: This panel builds on last year’s exploration of incubation models to spotlight how celebrities today are acting as startup founders, investors, and brand builders. We’ll unpack the good, bad and ugly details of celebrity‑backed ventures—from Skims to Goop—exploring brand fit, media power, product execution, partnership pitfalls, and long‑term viability. Attendees will gain actionable insights into how celebrity involvement amplifies opportunity… and risk.

title: Stop Apologizing to Your AI: How Anthropomorphism Deludes Us
description: Why do we talk to our cats, feel guilty throwing away childhood toys, or bond with our robot vacuum? Scientist and bestselling author Justin Gregg reveals how anthropomorphism, one of humanity's oldest psychological quirks, secretly influences nearly every decision we make. Drawing on cutting-edge research from his latest book Humanish, Justin explores our universal tendency to see human-like qualities in everything around us, and shows how understanding this bias can transform our relationships with animals, technology, and each other.

title: XR Filmmakers Meet Up
description: Come connect with fellow creators and curious minds exploring XR Film. Whether you’re already making immersive work or just excited by what’s possible, join us to mingle, share ideas, and spark conversations about the future of XR storytelling.

title: Branded Storytelling: Turning Culture into Business Impact
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Culture doesn’t just influence what people watch — it shapes what they do next. In this fireside chat, NBCUniversal and Taco Bell unpack how premium storytelling, live experiences, and cultural relevance are shaping marketing strategies and driving real business outcomes. Through real-world examples, they’ll explore how the brand is challenging traditional approaches, breaking category conventions, and showing up boldly and authentically to build brand love in moments that matter. Attendees will leave with practical insights for translating cultural engagement into measurable impact.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Behind the Scenes of the Fandom Economy
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Reality TV doesn’t just create stars — it creates superfans. Join NBCUniversal and one of their beloved stars for an insider look at how unscripted storytelling turns cast moments, catchphrases, and cultural momentum into full-blown fandoms. From Bravo’s most talked-about franchises to live reunions to Peacock’s social buzz, and IRL fan experiences, this session pulls back the curtain on how obsession is built — and why audiences keep coming back for more. Discover how talent-driven moments spark conversation, fuel community, and open the door for brands to authentically plug into fandom.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: A Conversation with Jack Conte
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Jack Conte is CEO and co-founder of Patreon, a media and community platform where creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive works and experiences. He founded Patreon in 2013 with Sam Yam (CTO), driven by the belief that creators should be paid for the value they provide to the world. Jack leads the team on their mission to build tools that give Patreon’s 300,000+ creators freedom, independence and ownership over their work and the communities they build. Since launching, Patreon has sent over $10 billion to creators.As a filmmaker and touring musician in the bands Pomplamoose and Scary Pockets, Jack has a deep understanding of and appreciation for creators. That passion is the driving force in the work Patreon is doing to usher in a new creative economy that puts the power back in creators’ hands.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: From Meme to Meaning: Making the Most of Accidental Virality
description: In today’s content-driven landscape, brands can go viral in ways they never intended. This panel explores the unpredictable nature of online virality through the lens of brands like Nuuly and Graza. Panelists will unpack what it takes to lead with creativity, clarity, and cultural fluency under pressure—and how chaotic moments can become opportunities for connection, growth, and relevance. Expect a rare behind-the-scenes look at the modern social media playbook these experts are writing in real time.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: Reclaiming our Humanity in the Age of AI
description: Too often, AI feels like something that’s done to us, not with us or for us. It will affect every aspect of our lives. But AI is neither poison nor panacea. It is and will be what we make it. Now is the moment to shape the future of AI for people. This panel will explore how we build the ecosystem and infrastructure to ensure that people will benefit the most from AI. Experts in AI, philanthropy, and advocacy, will offer a new way of thinking about AI that moves from Silicon Valley’s mantra of “move fast and break things” to an alternative vision that centers our humanity.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Growth You Can’t Spreadsheet: Why Culture Wins
description: In an era obsessed with performance metrics, the brands that win are the ones people actually trust. This panel brings together trusted leaders to explore how culture, storytelling, and emotional connection function as economic infrastructure shaping loyalty, pricing power, and long-term brand growth. From redefining value to reviving legacy narratives, these CMOs will examine how trust is built, how cultural relevance compounds over time, and why the brands that endure treat emotion as strategy not decoration.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Launchpad: Ready, Set, Communicate
description: Launching a brand? It’s not just what you build. It’s how you talk about it that determines take-off or stall. Strategic communications are your launch control pad. Know what to say and when to say it? Make the right connection and you’re flying. Join this interactive session to discover why comms is your brand’s biggest advantage at launch and beyond. Bring your challenges, get expert feedback and leave ready to make your brand soar.

title: Lights Out: Preserving Our Night Sky in the Modern Space Age
description: The increasing number and brightness of satellite constellations in Earth's orbit are significantly impacting ground-based and optical astronomy. So how do we balance humanity's expansion into space while protecting our study of the stars from Earth? Join the conversation on a sustainable future for space and hear from the experts on the front lines—astronomers, satellite companies, and space industry pioneers—as they discuss collaborative solutions to mitigate satellite brightness and preserve our night sky.

title: The New Gold Rush of AI Data Centers
description: As AI accelerates, demand for data centers has sparked a modern-day gold rush reshaping land use, energy, and regional economies. Drawing on her experience developing large-scale projects in high-growth markets, Anita Verma-Lallian explains why certain regions are emerging as AI infrastructure hubs while others face real constraints. She breaks down what drives site selection, from zoning and entitlements to power, water, and community impact, cutting through the hype to reveal where the true opportunities lie and how communities can realistically position themselves for long-term investment.

title: The New Growth Playbook: Activate Consulting’s 2026 Tech & Media Outlook
description: The global internet, media, and technology industries are entering a new era—one that will unlock more than $1.3 trillion in incremental revenue by 2029. But as silos disappear and competition intensifies, the rules of growth are being rewritten. In this session, Michael J. Wolf, Founder and CEO of Activate Consulting, presents the firm’s 2026 Tech & Media Outlook, revealing how leading companies must rethink strategy, product, and consumer engagement to stay ahead. Learn which trends will define the next phase of the digital economy—and why winning in 2026 will depend on owning attention across every moment of the consumer’s day.

title: Protecting Your Mental Wellness as a Creator with Elyse Myers
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Join creator and NYT bestselling author Elyse Myers for a candid conversation on authenticity, mental wellness, and brand continuity, moderated by acclaimed creator strategist Antonia Alakija. Elyse shares how she protects her voice, navigates imposter syndrome, and embraces her neurodivergence to sustain a recognizable, reliable brand. Drawing from her bestseller That’s a Great Question, I’d Love to Tell You, she brings humor and honesty, showing how embracing lived experiences helps creators stay grounded amid constant digital change.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Revolutionizing Astronomy with Next Generation Big Data
description: Massive datasets, like those in astronomy, are the lifeblood of AI innovation.  The panel, led by researchers from the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins, will discuss how AI is transforming astronomy to unlock new insights into the origins of our universe. They will present visualizations showing state-of-the-art astronomical datasets built on flagship observations and supercomputing simulations of the Universe. They will discuss how these data can be used to develop novel AI methods that accelerate discovery, expanding the frontiers of AI possibility inside and outside astronomy.

title: Tech Happens Here: Libraries as Innovation Partners
description: Libraries are books and so much more! We will spotlight how public and national libraries are launchpads for emerging tech, from AI to XR, while staying rooted in community trust and accessibility. Hear from library innovators and creative technologists co-creating inclusive spaces for play, learning, and critical tech discourse. We’ll explore libraries bridging the gap between innovation and equity, and how YOU can partner with them to build meaningful, community-driven projects. Expect a lively conversation that challenges assumptions, shares real-world collaborations, and sparks new ideas.

title: Too Many Interests? Good! Build Your Career Around Them.
description: What if your dream job doesn’t exist? So you create it! During this interactive session, award-winning designer and creative educator Julie Bonner shares how she built a joyful, sustainable career by making a living from her diverse interests. Through branding, community, and creative entrepreneurship, Julie shows how to create a clear professional story and turn multiple interests into real income.

title: Welcome to the Transformation Economy
description: As we shift deeper into today’s Experience Economy, people increasingly want to go beyond merely memorable to highly meaningful, deeply transporting, and even truly transformative experiences. B. Joseph Pine II, author of the 2026 book The Transformation Economy: Guiding Customers to Achieve Their Aspirations, shows why this is so and how brands can help customers flourish by guiding them to become who they want to become, whether that’s improving wellbeing, increasing prosperity, developing knowledge, or finding purpose.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: What’s the Catch? The Realities of Federal Contracting for Entrepreneurs
description: Federal contracts promise big opportunities—but they come with real trade-offs. In this candid session, Dr. Paul Campbell of Brown Ventures Group breaks down the realities of federal contracting for entrepreneurs. Learn how the process works, where founders get stuck, and how to evaluate whether federal opportunities truly align with your business goals—before you sign on the dotted line.

title: Agents at Work: Build AI That Ships with v0
description: Vibe coding isn't just for demos and landing pages anymore. In this hands-on workshop, the team behind v0 by Vercel will show you how to use natural language to build AI agents that actually transform how you work, inspired by agents already running in production at Vercel. You'll build your own and walk away with something real you can bring back to your team. No coding background needed. Just bring a problem worth solving, and we'll help you ship the solution.

title: Authenticity vs. Deepfake: Who Validates What's Real?
description: With deepfakes and synthetic media on the rise, the line between real and fake is razor-thin. A recent UK/US study found that most consumers can’t identify AI-generated fakes - only 0.1% of participants could accurately distinguish real from fake content. So, Unico’s Tech Advisor, Davi Reis explores how identity verification methods are now more critical than ever to fight fraud, detect synthetic media, protect users, speed transactions, while also building trust in the post-truth era. What if authenticity becomes the ultimate currency?

title: Beyond Branding: Advertising as Infrastructure for Change
description: Advertising is evolving—from persuasion to public service. Marco Venturelli explores how brands like AXA and McDonald's are turning campaigns into agents of change, from securing housing in contract fine print for domestic violence survivors to lighting dark city streets with golden arches. Showcasing powerful work from across the globe, he reveals how marketing budgets can solve real problems—not just build brands but build solutions.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Can Biotech Help Save Coral Reefs?
description: Coral reef ecosystems are in crisis due to climate change, pollution, and overfishing, and traditional restoration methods are limited in scale. This panel explores how biotechnology, materials science, and synthetic biology are converging to give reefs a fighting chance. From lab-grown super algae to bioactive reef coatings, scientists and startups are creating new tools to restore reefs faster, smarter, and more effectively. To scale impact, they are also developing sustainable business models and innovative funding partnerships.

title: Cannabis Prisoners in the Era of Legalization
description: As cannabis becomes legal across the country, tens of thousands of Americans remain incarcerated for the same plant. This panel brings together advocates and directly impacted leaders to examine the state of cannabis legalization, expose how cannabis prisoners are being left behind, and offer solutions. From clemency to re-entry, the panel will share strategies to ensure justice is at the center of cannabis reform, not just profit.

title: Corporate Dark Arts: The Real Rules of Marketing Power
description: This panel cracks open a vault of 60+ anonymous confessions from marketing leaders all over the world who've survived the C-suite by honing a new set of under-the-radar corporate superpowers. They evolved, not by fighting the forces of doubt, but by flipping them—using ambiguity as leverage, tension as traction, and absurdity as armor. What they faced first, every role faces now.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Creative Pitching: Nerve Bundle To Boardroom Wrecking Ball
description: Next time you’re nervous pitching to a client, ask yourself, “What’s my wrecking ball here?” It could be your tone, your brevity or your charisma. FACT: Most creative folk don’t get proper pitch/prezo training. They wing it. What if you could learn to tell/sell energising stories that sprinkle magic dust all over the client? Did you know this is an often-overlooked, age-old art form? Long-time SX advisor & keynote speaker Jon Burkhart has combined forces with 6x TEDx speaker Natalia Talkowska to create a program that will help you deliver & destroy in boardrooms & on stages the world over.

title: Featured Session: Launch, Land, Orbit: The Future of Space with Firefly
description: A fireside chat with Jason Kim, CEO, and Brigette Oakes, VP of Engineering, at Firefly Aerospace. In 2025, Firefly became the first commercial company to successfully land on the Moon, and now the company is working towards a repeat in 2026. Firefly’s next lunar is bigger and bolder, set to land on the far side of the Moon for the first time in American history. This company is simultaneously launching rockets to fill a void in the growing satellite market while also preparing to orbit a constellation of spacecraft in support of in-space maneuverability, space domain awareness, and more. Jason and Brigette will discuss what’s next for the company and where the space industry is headed next.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Harnessing AI to Serve Creators and Customers
description: At Amazon MGM Studios and Prime Video, AI is giving filmmakers, writers, and designers tools to move faster, think bigger, and take creative risks. We'll pull back the curtain on how AI is pushing the boundaries of what's achievable — and the story doesn't stop at production. Prime Video is harnessing AI to bring content to more people: localizing stories across languages and cultures, and enhancing the viewing experience so audiences connect more deeply with the content they love. This session will focus on what it means to build AI responsibly, with human creativity as the north star.

title: How AI is Driving the Future of Visual Work & Creativity
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Join Canva CCO Rob Giglio and Liz Bacelar, Global Head of AI & Advanced Analytics at Under Armour, for a fireside chat tackling the central debate around AI and creativity. Is AI making organizations more creative or less? As content demands skyrocket and AI tools proliferate across teams, Rob and Liz explore the real impact on workplace creativity, from how visual thinking is replacing text-heavy communication to whether democratized design tools are elevating ideas or creating a sea of sameness. Learn why the answer isn't binary, and discover the conditions that determine whether AI becomes a creativity amplifier or creativity killer in your organization.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: In Uncertainty We Trust: The New Rules of Leadership
description: When disruption hits, from AI to remote work, leaders face make-or-break moments. Control is out; trust is in. In this interactive workshop, you’ll discover the new rules for navigating change without losing your people. You’ll gain actionable tools and a proven playbook to steady teams, spark innovation, and keep your best people engaged—even when the future is uncertain. Real-world case studies and peer learning will help you transform uncertainty from a risk into your leadership superpower. Walk away ready to anchor your team, accelerate trust, and lead boldly through whatever comes next.

title: The Pickleball Payoff: Betting on America’s Top Sport
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Major League Pickleball and the PPA Tour are transforming pickleball into America’s next major pro sport. With 311% growth and investors like LeBron James and Tom Brady, the game is booming. This session features voices from across the sport, including the world’s #1 player, and MLP’s commissioner, to explore league building, athlete branding, sponsorship, and media. Attendees will get a full-picture snapshot of pro pickleball’s rapid evolution.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Taste Can't Be Prompted: Designing in the Age of AI
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Everything on the internet is starting to look the same. Feeds reward speed and recognizable patterns, pushing creative work toward generic. But the best designers aren't choosing between moving fast and having a point of view they've built systems that protect their taste even when platforms demand constant output. This session gets practical about the tension: how top designers and brands jump on cultural moments without losing their identity, and where AI fits not as a shortcut to sameness, but as a tool that multiplies your taste.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: When AI does the work, what do humans do next?
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: AI is becoming core infrastructure across every industry, but the biggest challenge is preparing people to use it with confidence, judgement, and trust. In this panel discussion, Justina Nixon‑Saintil, IBM Chief Impact Officer, and Amanda Butler, Sundial Media Group Chief Marketing Officer, share why AI readiness is key to performance and resilience. Drawing on their approach to responsible, human-led AI and its global work in education and workforce development, they'll discuss what it takes to build a workforce that uses AI effectively and ethically.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Who Owns Your AI? Probably Not You.
description: Most AI today is rented intelligence. Your ideas, workflows, and data are fed into centralized systems that learn from that input—and keep the value. In this session, webAI Founder and CEO David Stout explains how this approach is fundamentally flawed. As AI becomes the operating system of modern life, the real question isn't how powerful the models are. It's who owns the intelligence they produce. Stout outlines a different future where intelligence is personal, contextual, and yours, and what it could look like when AI runs on your devices, learns your expertise, and belongs to you entirely.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Who Predicts Better and Why? Humans, AI, and the Anatomy of a Forecast
description: AI models process vast signals; humans bring judgment and context. But who calls real-world outcomes better, and when? This session compares human and model forecasts on headline events, drawing on newsroom practice, product experience, and reasoning-centric AI research. We’ll map where models’ pattern breadth wins, where human context does, and why hybrids often work best. Instead of scoreboards alone, we’ll discuss how journalists and analysts should use uncertainty ranges, disclose methods and sources, and avoid feedback loops that can steer the story itself.

title: Why Work Sucks (And How We Can Make It Joyful Again).
description: Feeling burned out, underpaid, or like your work doesn’t matter? You’re not alone. Most companies mean well, but outdated systems give too much power to bad actors, leading to misaligned incentives and broken cultures. This session introduces the Work Evolution Framework, a six-part roadmap that helps you identify where things have gone off track and how to fix them. Walk away with a clear diagnostic tool and practical steps to build a work culture where everyone can be successful and happy.

title: You Are the Visionary: How to Stay Authentic While Using AI
description: If an artwork is to be ours, it must be a product of our own vision. AI assistants are readily available. But how do we make sure we never give away so much to a virtual assistant that nothing we create feels like it is authentically ours anymore? How can we keep the joy, the purpose, and the power of our making while engaging AI to support the project of our work coming into being? This workshop will offer a series of core principles, plus meditation and journaling practices, designed to help participants intuit which parts of their project to do themselves and which to outsource.

title: A.I. News That’s Fit to Print
description: In adopting artificial intelligence, news organizations are making starkly different choices about when and how to use the technology. Which is great, because we can compare and contrast each approach, to identify some early best practices. Zach Seward, who leads the A.I. Initiatives team at The New York Times, will present those findings across a wide range of compelling — and sometimes really terrible — uses of A.I. for journalism. He will also offer a look at how The Times is using A.I. to bolster the human expertise of its newsroom, while discovering new forms of reporting.

title: Animation Professionals Meet Up
description: The Animation Professionals Meet Up is a chance to mix with animation industry folks and make some sweet connections. This event is hosted by SXSW veterans Craig Staggs and Steph Swope of Minnow Mountain, animators behind Tower (PBS), Undone (Amazon), and Apollo 10 1/2 (Netflix). Animation pros at any level are invited to join us for the third year of this epic exchange of business cards and social media profiles. Leave your mark on our huge live-drawing canvas featuring the amazing cutting-edge technology of a Sharpie. These are your people!

title: Bending Reality: Scaling Film Productions with AI Workflows
description: Go behind the scenes of groundbreaking short films, The Bends and Pathways, developed under the Entertainment Technology Center, to explore how AI is reshaping the future of filmmaking. The Bends and Pathways both pushed the boundaries of traditional workflows using AI-integrated pipelines. In this panel, filmmakers and tech leads reveal how they reimagined VFX and production processes to deliver ambitious visuals without ballooning resources. This session offers an inside look at what’s possible when artificial intelligence becomes a central creative force in production.

title: The Brain Science of Getting and Keeping Attention
description: Attention is everything. It’s the gateway to memory, persuasion, and customer action. But in a hyper-saturated world, even the best content gets ignored. Cognitive neuroscientist Dr. Carmen Simon shares surprising findings from her studies of buyers’ brains, revealing what actually captures and sustains attention. This session busts myths ( attention spans aren’t shrinking!) and reveals four types of attention you must master to create effective campaigns. If you're building a brand, selling an idea, or designing a message, don’t just hope your audience is paying attention, design for it.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Brainwave Breakthroughs: The Next Healthcare Frontier
description: Tech is rewriting medicine’s playbook. Dive into AI engines, brain-computer interfaces, and VR therapies transforming healthcare. Learn which disorders they tackle and the trends poised to disrupt diagnostics, workflows, and recovery. These tools feed on rich cognitive data to personalize treatment in real time. Explore innovative applications that convert living rooms into mini clinics. Discoveries in brain science ripple beyond neurology, inspiring smarter solutions across healthcare.

title: Creative Destruction Unleashed by Artificial Intelligence
description: Artificial intelligence is a highly disruptive technology that will challenge existing institutions in all industries and domains globally. Traditional approaches to creating value will become less and less effective as this technology spreads. On the other side, this technology opens up new potential to create exponentially expanding value but, to harness this potential, we need to become very creative in imagining and implementing new approaches to managing organizations.

title: Featured Session: How to Design a Company That AI Can’t Outpace
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Yes. We’re failing at AI. But it’s not because the tools aren’t good enough. We’re failing because we’re designing for a system that’s already obsolete. The winners of the AI era won’t be the fastest tool adopters, but the ones who redesign how work is organized, value is created, and ecosystems are navigated.In this session,, Ian shows you how to think and build like an AI-native and re-architect organizations, sharing a practical map and mindset shift to thrive in a future where what’s valued changes in real time.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Future of News
description: A thriving news industry is essential—not only to democracy, but also as a powerful platform for marketers and advertisers. Yet concerns about brand safety have caused many to retreat from news content, often based on outdated fears and misinformation. In an era of rapid technological disruption—including the rise of AI and increasing cultural polarization— this panel unites marketers and media experts to challenge assumptions and champion news. We'll explore how advertisers can re-engage with journalism in ways that uphold brand values, build consumer trust, and deliver real business results.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: How the Pop-Tarts Bowl Became the Biggest Name in CFB
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: In the highly competitive and tradition-rich landscape of college athletics, few brand partnerships have achieved the level of cultural impact and consumer engagement as the Pop-Tarts Bowl. What began as a straightforward sponsorship evolved into a standout case study in modern sports marketing, illustrating how creativity, cultural relevance, and bold brand identity can redefine the value of a naming rights agreement.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Human+AI Alliance: Talent Leaders & AI Developers Collab
description: Breaking down the critical divide in AI implementation: the gap between those who design AI experiences, build AI systems, and develop humans to work with them. AI designers create experiences, developers build systems, talent leaders develop humans, UI/UX designers shape interactions—but they rarely collaborate. Result? AI implementations fail because they ignore human experience design and adaptation needs. We'll break down silos between these groups, creating the cross-functional alliance every organization needs but few achieve.

title: Liquid Brands & Vanishing Interfaces: New Rules of Experience
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: The website is dying. The mouse is next. In this bold fireside chat, Alex Hamilton and Suff Syed challenge everything we know about digital experience. From liquid brands that move across surfaces to voice interfaces that erase traditional UX, they’ll unpack what happens after design as we know it. What does it mean when a brand meets you, in your car, your watch, your fridge, your earbuds? What does design look like when it’s generated, optimised, and personalised in real time? Expect a thrilling look at the collapse of static web thinking.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: SXSW Episodic Pitch-A-Thon presented by SeriesFest
description: Back for its seventh year, the SXSW Episodic Pitch-A-Thon presented by SeriesFest gives filmmakers a chance to take their series ideas to the next level—while audiences get a front-row look at the art of the pitch! Independent filmmakers selected for SXSW Film + Television 2026 will present their projects directly to top executives from television, news media, and digital platforms—on stage, in front of a live audience, and a panel of industry experts.

title: The Systems We Build: Business Models for Meaningful XR
description: XR is booming… Or is it? How secure are business models in the interactive/immersive sector and how well do they support creators to have social impact? And how do you thrive - not just survive - as a creator, producer, developer of this type of work, right now? Acknowledging challenges and uncertainty but also highlighting opportunities, the panel reflect on their own journeys - including successes, challenges and startings-over - to explore alternative models and offer practical advice for creators and producers to make work that matters: including protecting your IP and your wellbeing.

title: Why Boring Brands Break the Internet
description: When the default expectation is dull, entertaining content is a growth hack. This panel gathers marketers from categories often considered ‘boring’ like sunscreen, hygiene, and language apps, who have proven that irreverence, humour, and showmanship drive disproportionate returns. Learn why the blandest brands can become the boldest entertainers.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: 25 Minutes of AI: The SXSW Speed-Briefing
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: The AI news cycle doesn’t just move fast—it moves at the speed of culture. In this high-intensity, 25-minute live briefing, Monks takes their signature monthly innovation series from the screen to the stage. This isn't a lecture; it’s a no-nonsense sprint through the breakthroughs, the hype, and the real-world applications actually worth your time.Designed for the SXSW audience, this session is structured into four rapid-fire acts:Monthly Wonders: A pulse check on the week’s biggest AI news and hidden gems surfacing in Austin.The 101 Deep Dive: We peel back the curtain on a complex concept—moving beyond the buzzwords to explain the tech shifting the industry.Monks in Action: A raw, behind-the-scenes walkthrough of an AI-powered case study, demonstrating how we turn agentic workflows into creative impact.The Homework: You leave with a tangible task—a specific tool to test or a framework to apply—ensuring you walk out smarter than you walked in.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Build a Future Ready Catalog for the Modern Music Ecosystem
description: Learn how to prepare your music for the modern licensing landscape across sync, creator campaigns, digital platforms, and emerging AI opportunities. This interactive workshop covers asset prep, rights setup, metadata strategy, pricing basics, and the steps artists and labels need to take to make their catalogs discoverable and revenue ready. You will leave with a clear roadmap and tools to support long term licensing success.

title: Building Hollywood Buzz Without the Hollywood Style Campaign
description: A number of specialty streaming platforms have been able to scale their audiences without needing to invest regularly in big launch stunts, massive advertising campaigns, and other means typical of the Hollywood machine. Hear from marketing heads at streaming services like Rakuten Viki, BritBox, and Hallmark+ about their secrets for leaning into fandom to build loyal audiences.

title: Decoding Nature: How AI is Learning to Program Biology
description: AI mastered Go, then human language—now it can write DNA. Glen Gowers, CEO of Basecamp Research, explains how his team trained AI on the world’s largest evolutionary dataset, spanning 1 million new species, to design a "beyond-CRISPR" tool for precise gene insertion—a long-standing challenge in genetic medicine. Microsoft’s Alicia Frame unpacks the AI advances enabling this scale, while UT Austin biophysicist Ilya Finkelstein explains the rigor required to turn AI-designed biology into safe, effective medicine.

title: Designing For Human Connection in Vertical Cities
description: What if buildings were designed not just around structure or code, but around human experience? As cities are constantly evolving, architect Eran Chen shares how architecture contributes to the built environment to benefit its community. Two of the guiding principles for his design philosophy, “form follows experience” and “porosity leads to prosperity,” are carried to ODA’s projects worldwide. The session will explore how an experience-centric approach can redefine design globally to create urban environments that challenge the boundaries of conventional structure and improve engagement.

title: Digital Health Engagement? Design for Intermittent Use
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Sustained user engagement is critical to the success of digital health interventions, particularly for chronic and long-term conditions that require patients to self-manage care over extended periods. But the prevailing design paradigm - adapted from consumer technology - often assumes continuous use as the marker of engagement. Real-world evidence demonstrates health behaviors are inherently intermittent. This presentation shows why & how “designing for intermittent use” is an essential strategy for improving user engagement and health outcomes.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: The Great Flip: Why Every Industry Is Running Backwards
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Technology isn’t just accelerating progress, it's changing the very process of progress itself: product testing can happen virtually before building, clinical trials can be simulated before major lab investment, distribution can dictate where production happens. This session will change how you see every workflow in your business. You'll walk away armed with a practical framework that will help you learn how to identify inversions in your own industry. The future belongs to leaders who think in reverse.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Internet of Value Meets the Internet of Intelligence
description: As AI and blockchain mature in parallel, we are witnessing the emergence of two powerful networks: the Internet of Intelligence, where autonomous AI systems reason and act, and the Internet of Value, where tokenized assets and stablecoins move frictionlessly across borders.This session explores what happens when these two networks converge — when AI agents can hold, send, and earn value — and how this convergence will reshape global commerce, financial systems, and enterprise operations. Drawing from her experience leading AI transformation at Circle, an internet infrastructure company pioneering stablecoins, Li Fan will share both the vision and operational realities of building compliant, intelligent, and scalable financial infrastructure for the AI era.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Film & TV Audience and Community Building Meet Up
description: After 8 years on the road as a pop-up screening outfit, Austin’s Hyperreal Film Club put down roots at their single-screen theater in East Austin. Now a year-and-a-half into the experiment, join the creators of the club to talk lessons learned (so far!), meet other indie filmmakers to talk about creative exhibition opportunities and strategies; and dream up new ways to use film programming and community building to create the film community you've always imagined.

title: Genius at Scale: How Great Leaders Drive Innovation
description: Amid relentless technological disruption, global uncertainty, and shifting work norms, most leaders know innovation is essential, but few know how to move from generating bold ideas to scaling them across complex organizations and ecosystems. Harvard Business School professor Linda A. Hill, one of the world’s leading experts on leadership and innovation, and Harvard researcher Emily Tedards argue that innovation isn’t a solo act, it’s a collective one.Drawing on their new book, Genius at Scale: How Great Leaders Drive Innovation, co-authored with Jason Wild, and nearly a decade of global research, Hill and Tedards introduce three critical leadership roles that turn ideas into impact: the Architect, who builds cultures and capabilities for co-creation; the Bridger, who fosters partnerships across organizational boundaries; and the Catalyst, who sparks movements that sustain innovation across ecosystems.Through case studies spanning technology, healthcare, and hospitality, Hill and Tedards show how leaders can harness digital tools, data, and cross-sector collaboration to transform experimentation into execution. Participants will leave with practical frameworks for creating environments where creativity thrives, collaboration flourishes, and innovation becomes everyone’s job.

title: The Great Recalibration: Centering Humanity in the Age of AI
description: AI is rapidly reshaping how we work, create, and connect—yet at the same time, people are craving the tactile, the real, and the human. As AI-generated content floods our world, a new question emerges: where do we fit?This panel explores the tension between acceleration and authenticity. How do we design AI that strengthens community instead of replacing it? How do we protect meaning, creativity, and trust in an era of misinformation and automation?Innovation leaders will examine the cultural shift already underway and share a vision for an AI-powered future where human values and relationships remain at the center.

title: How to Not Be Cringey On Camera: Media Insider Secrets
description: Hate being on camera? You're not alone! But nowadays everyone is expected to be confident and camera-ready—for social media, conferences, and even meetings. Award-winning media expert Kim Rittberg, now a public speaking and video coach, spent 15 years in media at Netflix and PEOPLE Magazine and has trained thousands to not just stay calm but shine under the lights. In this lively session, Kim shares insider secrets to help you win over anyone—on stage or on video. You’ll leave with tangible on-camera and speaking tips, more confidence, and a roadmap to becoming a thought leader.

title: Intelligence on Tap: Designing the Frontier Firm
description: Welcome to the era of intelligence on demand. Microsoft’s Work Trend Index introduces the Frontier Firm: built on abundant AI, where digital agents aren’t tools, they’re teammates. While some fear AI replacing jobs, the real risk is ignoring it. With 80% of employees maxed out and 82% of leaders betting on AI, the question isn’t if you’ll use digital labor, it’s how. Frontier Firms are rethinking org charts, hiring AI trainers, and managing human-agent ratios like headcount. This is your blueprint for building a business where AI scales work and where human judgment stays at the center.

title: Leading with Both Sides: The Business-Creative Hyphenate
description: More professionals than ever are combining business acumen with creative ambition, choosing careers that embrace both. This conversation explores what it takes to lead in corporate spaces and pursue creative passions with purpose. Hear from leaders who’ve scaled brands, launched movements, and redefined success—without “starting over.” We’ll share practical tactics for integrating creative identity into leadership and professional branding, and explore how corporate-to-creative leaders are reshaping success, innovation, and the future of work.

title: Lies My Creative Director Told Me: Myths of the Agency World
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Join some of the most senior creative leaders from four of the most successful digital design agencies in the world as they discuss the current existential moment for creativity and innovation in agencies. They will discuss the current state of creative agencies, including how AI, emerging technologies, and ever-evolving client expectations are fundamentally reshaping the industry, and how they are reshaping their teams and how work gets done. Discover what's irrevocably changing, what remains constant, and perhaps even unearth a few trade secrets along the way.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Making a Vision Pro Documentary: "D-Day: The Camera Soldier"
description: This session dives into the making of "D-Day: The Camera Soldier", a documentary built from the ground up for Vision Pro.From technical breakthroughs in video, 3D, and interaction design to the creative vision that brought it to life, hear directly from TIME, TARGO, and the team behind the project—tracing the journey from initial concept to distribution on Vision Pro.

title: The People's LAB: AI, Storytelling & the Future from Queens
description: What if the next great tech lab wasn’t in Silicon Valley—but in Queens? This panel explores how cultural institutions like the Museum of the Moving Image (MoMI) — the first museum in the U.S. dedicated to the moving image—can lead public innovation. MoMI LAB, a bold new initiative, puts artists and storytellers at the center of exploring how AI, XR, and emerging tools can shape more ethical, imaginative civic futures. In one of the world’s most diverse communities, artists, technologists, and funders are working to shape technology around public values—not private gain.

title: Plot Twist: Romance Authors Redefining Creator Success
description: Romance as a genre transformed book publishing, but in an industry known for gatekeeping, this growth happened via indie authors & entrepreneurs in the creator economy. By building direct relationships with readers on platforms like Kindle, Patreon, BookTok, and internal email newsletters, these creators have scaled faster, marketed smarter, and reshaped book discoverability. This panel explores how creators and marketers across industries can apply these strategies to grow fanbases, monetize directly, and turn niche communities into powerful engines of cultural influence and business success.

title: Powerful AI, Terrible UX:  Designing a Smarter UX
description: Enterprise AI is getting more powerful, but for many users it still feels slow, awkward, and hard to trust. The problem is rarely the model or the data. It is the experience. Too many tools rely on chat interfaces that add cognitive load, cause prompt fatigue, and fail in high pressure workflows. This session explores why enterprise AI UX keeps breaking down and how cognitive design can reduce friction, rebuild trust, and make AI fit how people actually work.

title: The Skin–Brain Connection: How the Microbiome Relates to Stress and Mood
description: Groundbreaking new research reveals for the first time that the skin microbiome – particularly the presence of Cutibacterium – is linked to lower stress and improved mood. In this solo session, Dr Samaras elucidates the link that was uncovered - via the skin–brain axis - and what it means for the future of beauty, mental health, and product innovation. Using advanced AI models, they decoded complex microbiome data to uncover patterns no human could detect. This is not just skincare – it’s a new frontier in holistic wellbeing. And imagine the unlocks when we add the power of quantum computing.

title: Sports Marketing & the World's Most-Watched Sporting Event
description: The World Cup is the most-watched sporting event & ultimate proving ground for brands ready to think beyond borders & traditional playbooks. Yet, we persist in approaching this once-every-four-years opportunity with tired strategies. It's not about slapping logos on jerseys; it's about cultural fluency at scale. The next cycle brings unprecedented viewing fragmentation, creator-driven content & fans expecting authentic engagement across 20+ markets simultaneously. ADWEEK's William Bradley and top CMOs explore how to revolutionize global sports marketing for the next World Cup.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Stop Playing Nice: Brand Safety Is Killing Your Business
description: In an AI-driven world, mediocre is invisible. With access to the same tools, budgets, and tech, only bold, resonant, and risky content cuts through. Safe marketing and SEO-optimized blogs won’t win visibility anymore—because AI doesn’t rank what’s average; it ranks what connects. Andrew Warden, CMO of Semrush, reveals why even top marketers are struggling to break through in generative search—and what it takes to truly earn attention when LLMs are the new gatekeepers. Learn how to ditch the illusion of “safe” and unlock real visibility in an era where the algorithm is no longer your ally.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Urbanists and Citymakers Core Conversation
description: Urbanism, SXSW, and Austin are an inseparable pairing!  In 2025 we kicked off the the urbanists meetup where dozens of architects, planners, and placemakers took a pulse of the most exciting urban ideas at SXSW. We also forged ties between SXSWers with local Austinites. The meetup features a round of self introduction, followed by some light, interactive networking activities. We will also discuss urbanism in Austin, and provide a platform for attendees to propose walks and outings to see new and historic projects that make up Austin’s urban fabric.

title: Visualising Feeling: Real-Time EEG Emotion Technology
description: What if you could see your brainwaves turned into real-time art that reveals how you feel? EVE (The EEG Visualizer of Emotions) is a live emotional visualization tool that transforms EEG signals into dynamic, responsive visuals. Blending neurotechnology, AI, and art, EVE opens new ways to explore emotional insight, user experience, and live experience. It can create interactive & personalized sensory designs, drive immersive wellbeing experiences, measure mood, emotion & real-time neural responses, map emotional learning & deepen meaning.

title: The Architecture of Intelligent Creative
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: A major barrier to intelligent optimization is the legacy divide between data, media, and creative teams. This session dismantles those silos, addressing the essential "plumbing" of modern marketing required to fuel experiences that feel human, authentic, and on-brand. We will explore the approach necessary to build your data foundations that fuel integrated and intelligent workflows, and transform insights into personalized creative outputs at scale. Join us to discover the strategic frameworks, partnerships, and tooling needed to ensure your content achieves real-time relevance.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: AI at the Speed of Life: How Technology is Reshaping Drug Discovery and Delivery
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming nearly every industry—but in healthcare, its impact is uniquely personal. The pharmaceutical industry stands at an inflection point: what once took a decade or more to move from scientific hypothesis to patient treatment is being fundamentally reimagined through AI, advanced computing, and automation. Yet in an industry where lives hang in the balance, speed must be matched with rigor, and innovation with responsibility.
