Join leaders from pharma and technology for a conversation about deploying AI at scale to accelerate breakthroughs while maintaining the highest standards of patient safety, governance, and compliance.

title: Beyond Silos: Aligning the C-Suite for Real-Time Execution
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: The biggest barrier to AI adoption isn't technology; it's organizational structure. Historically, Marketing chased the "Big Idea" while IT focused on security and stability. To win in the AI era, the C-Suite must de-silo. This session provides a blueprint for aligning the Chief Marketing Officer and the Chief Information Officer, turning technical constraints into creative enablers and ensuring the boardroom speaks a unified language.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Brands x Music: Bringing together Activations & Artists
description: Some brands don’t just show up - they create cultural moments. Today’s most innovative marketers are blending bold brand activations with the power of music and star power to build emotional resonance and spark global buzz. In this lively discussion, hear from marketing leaders at the world’s most forward-thinking brands as they explore how to craft unforgettable experiences that connect with consumers on a visceral level, through sound, story, and shared energy. Learn how music-fueled campaigns are driving loyalty, elevating brand perception, and shaping the future of experiential marketing.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Curious Critters and Human Health
description: Ozempic revolutionized diabetes treatment, inspired by Gila monster venom - proving nature holds answers to health challenges. This session explores how unique organisms like snails and cavefish can improve our quality of life. Panelists discuss the most exciting ways curious critters, genetic engineering and biomimicry transform human health and society through nature-inspired discoveries that could change our future.

title: Featured Session: Actionable Ikigai: Career Planning in the age of AI
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Most career advice stinks.Why? Because we can’t manage what we don’t measure, and most of us are flying blind.Join futurist and three-time SXSW fan favorite Mike Bechtel as he turns his foresight toolkit inward and demystifies the future of career planning in the age of AI with a flight-tested dashboard he’s used himself for the past 20 years.Over the course of this session, Mike will offer the audience tips, techniques, and even take-home-tech to help them determine their "reason-for-being“, plot a practical path to get there, and critically, know when to make course corrections.Tuned towards early to mid-stage professionals, with relevance to students and senior leaders seeking a recalibration, this fast, story-rich session blends personality with practicality, giving today’s audience the mindset and toolset to design more intentional futures.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Latticework of Mental Models For a Great Life!
description: Join renowned investor and philanthropist Mohnish Pabrai for a thought-provoking talk on the key mental models that have shaped major decisions in his life, from business and investing to philanthropy and personal growth. An avid follower of Warren Buffett and Charlie Munger, Pabrai has long embraced Munger’s call to develop a “latticework of mental models,” a multidisciplinary toolkit for thinking clearly and avoiding misjudgment. In this session, he will go beyond simply restating the classics. Instead, he will highlight the select models he has found most indispensable, the ones that have guided him in navigating uncertainty, spotting opportunities, and sidestepping avoidable errors. Pabrai will introduce new mental models of his own, distilled from decades of experience, hard lessons, and reflection. These additions help bring to light patterns of behavior and decision-making that he believes are critical for anyone seeking not just financial success, but a truly fulfilling life.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Fuel or Friction? Private Equity in the Age of AI
description: AI hype is at a fever pitch. Every headline promises a venture-backed moonshot ready to upend entire industries. But that is not how most AI will scale. Private equity is quietly shaping what real adoption looks like inside operating businesses. Andrew Brooks, CEO of Contextual.io, makes the case that capital structure is one of the most overlooked forces in AI’s future. VC rewards urgency and outsized bets. PE rewards operational discipline, durable growth, and EBITDA performance. Especially in markets where transformation takes time. This session explores what actually changes when PE enters the picture and why the next phase of AI may favor staying power and measurable impact over speed.- How capital incentives shape AI product decisions and timelines- Why private equity’s performance discipline can create more sustainable AI businesses than growth-at-all-costs VC models- How PE alignment enables company growth without the pressure to chase hype cycles

title: Indie Film, Hollywood Tech: Virtual Production on Any Budget
description: Independent filmmakers are told to write small. But LED volume technology is unlocking worlds of possibility outside the Hollywood system, proving virtual production isn't just for sci-fi blockbusters. From queer horror-comedies to intimate dramas to stylized period pieces, we'll look at real-world case studies that show how accessible virtual production can be. Learn how to think about LED volumes, Unreal Engine, and more from script to budget without technical overwhelm—and discover how to make those 'impossible' scripts possible.

title: Inside the International Scam Syndicate: How We Fight Back
description: Scamming is a multibillion-dollar, transnational business rivaling legitimate enterprises.  In actual offices, their workers labor leg to leg at long tables -- cubicles would actually be more humane. Scams are now a way for nation-states to steal the wealth of Americans, at speed and scale. These criminals are on a rampage, and those in power, in the US and abroad, aren't really focused on stopping it. Hear from a former prosecutor hell-bent on disrupting this prolific crime, an expert in deception, and a scam survivor and podcaster about how we can all fight back.

title: It All Begins With Birds – Insights, Joy, and Lessons from Avian Life
description: Explore the vital role birds play in our world. Not only do birds contribute to biodiversity and the stability of ecosystems, birdwatching carries psychological and emotional benefits – inspiring creativity, mindfulness, and a sense of wonder. By observing birds, we can learn about adaptation, social interaction, and survival strategies. Whether you are a seasoned birdwatcher or simply enjoy the cheerful songs outside your window, this session promises to deepen your appreciation for birds and their essential place in our lives, encouraging you to observe, protect, and celebrate our feathered friends.

title: Neurogaming: The Future of Interactive Entertainment
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: From button-mashing to brain-driven play, neurogaming is set to rewrite the rulebook. This panel hosted by OpenBCI gathers neurogamers and more to show how real-time EEG/EMG decoding replaces sticks and keys with pure intent, unlocking worlds that respond to mood, focus, and micro-gestures. We’ll spotlight a live neurogaming case study, unpack further real-world examples, and sketch the roadmap toward a brain-driven future of play.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Non-Obvious Podcast LIVE w/ Rohit Bhargava & Under Armour Founder Kevin Plank
description: In a world where it's tempting to chase relevance, what does it take to create a brand that feels true and stands for something? Join Non-Obvious Founder Rohit Bhargava for a live taping of the Non-Obvious Show podcast as he sits down with Under Armour Founder, Executive Chairman & CEO, Kevin Plank to uncover non-obvious secrets of entrepreneurship, why performance matters most and what it takes to get better at anything.

title: Perceptual Diversity: Art & Neuroscience in Design
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: How can we design for a world where no two people experience reality the same way? This panel explores the science and power of perceptual diversity, how differences in how we sense, process and interpret shape identity, connection, and emotion. With expert voices from neuroscience, immersive design, and the arts and through the new lens of neuroaesthetics: we’ll explore how expanding perception can create more inclusive, empathetic, and visionary futures across art, health, and technology.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: US Healthcare in Crisis: Who Cares? Who Wins?
description: A little over a year ago, Dr. Elisabeth Potter scrubbed out of an operating room to take a call from an insurance company and stepped into a national conversation about how our system is treating patients and healthcare workers. Time to scrub out again, and talk about the health of our $5.6 trillion system, the value of independent voices and the state of our healthcare workforce. She is joined on stage by Trina Spear, co-Founder and CEO of Figs, Inc. for a conversation, a pep talk, a call for assistance to creative innovators willing to take on a big, hairy challenge.

title: Waste is the New Gold: How Trash Will Fund the Next Economy
description: From wastewater to e-waste, the world is drowning in “garbage” that holds untapped value. But a new class of startups, scientists, and technologists is flipping the script by turning industrial runoff into rare earths, landfills into lithium banks, and toxins into trillion-dollar opportunities. This session will dive into how biology, chemistry, and data science are transforming global waste streams into the raw materials of the clean economy. Think of it as a startup gold rush just under our feet.

title: Your Brand Has Multiple Personalities. Is that a Problem?
description: Your brand acts completely different on TikTok vs LinkedIn vs Instagram - but is that authentic multi-dimensionality or dangerous personality disorder? We'll diagnose real brands live, dissect epic platform fails, and teach the difference between healthy code-switching and brand schizophrenia. Through interactive challenges, learn why Gen Z can spot inauthentic adaptation from miles away, which archetypes can successfully platform-hop, and how to maintain consistent values while speaking each platform's native language fluently.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: AI is Changing Consumer Behavior: How Brands Must Adapt
description: AI is quietly but profoundly changing how people think, act, and decide. From planning trips to seeking therapy, it’s quickly becoming the first stop for answers and everyday decision-making. It’s also reshaping cognition: one MIT study found that frequent ChatGPT users reported higher loneliness and reduced critical thinking over time. This session brings together behavioral experts, cognitive researchers and marketers to explore how AI is rewiring consumer behavior, and how brands can keep up.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Are We All Biohackers Now?
description: From personalized supplement regimens to wearables and AI health tracking, consumers are building their own health ecosystems. This session explores how self-directed health has gone mainstream, transforming how we approach wellness, performance, and prevention. But as products and tools become more accessible, people face an overwhelming and opaque marketplace full of bold claims and conflicting advice. What does it mean to be a “biohacker” today, and how can we take charge of our health whilst also navigating a system that still struggles with transparency?

title: CANCELED: AI & Culture: Empowering the African Diaspora
description: AI is transforming industries, yet cultural representation often lags behind. This session explores how AI can be used to preserve cultural heritage, amplify diverse voices, and create new business models across the African diaspora. Through real-world case studies in entertainment, fashion, and events, we’ll uncover strategies for building inclusive AI ecosystems that merge cultural identity, creativity, and innovation for global impact.

title: ENCORE: 7 Lies People Tell Today (& the Brain Science to Beat Them)
description: To separate yourself from AI slop and connect with customers, you need authentic, relevant messages.But customers say things about how they behave & what they want that just aren’t true. And their insistence compromises your marketing. Discover 7 current, common lies customers tell, plus the brain science backed ways to counteract them. Jammed with proven research and in-market examples, this session reveals exactly how to avoid messaging landmines at a time when what you say and how you say it makes all the difference. Don’t do what people claim they want. Do what science shows will work.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Craft Still Wins: The Irreplaceable Role of Human Instinct, Taste, and Emotion
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Have we lost sight that the creative work that truly impacts you on a human level, requires just that—the craft of a human? Greg Greenberg, award-winning Executive Creative Director at TBWA\Media Arts Lab, Apple’s global creative agency explores the irreplaceable role of human instinct, taste, and emotional intelligence in the creative process. Featuring real-world examples of handcrafted storytelling, he offers a rare perspective on how we can’t lose sight of what truly impacts people on a deep emotional level, human craft, even in a world infatuated by the speed, ease and impact of AI.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Floods to Fortitude: Infrastructure Resilience
description: Infrastructure is facing unprecedented challenges, from aging bridges to extreme weather events. In this session, leaders from the public sector, policy, engineering, and technology will discuss how to build better, more resilient infrastructure for an era of increasing volatility. The session will spotlight Kentucky’s response to recent devastating floods, showcasing technology's transformative role and featuring recorded remarks from Lt. Governor Coleman of Kentucky.

title: From “Sick Care” to “Health Care”: Redefining Expectations in Medicine
description: For too long, health care has focused on treating diseases after symptoms appear and they have already taken hold. That is what we call “sick care.” Lilly is working to change that by moving to true “health care” where diseases are found earlier, treated sooner and in some cases prevented altogether.​Lilly is already bringing this vision to life with breakthroughs like improving how we manage chronic conditions and extend healthy years, moving earlier in brain diseases, restoring senses with genetic medicines and harnessing the immune system in powerful new ways.

title: From Elevator to Spotlight: How to Pitch Anyone, Anywhere
description: Whether you're a startup founder, filmmaker, designer, or daydreamer—you've got something to pitch. But most people fail to get attention because they pitch information, not emotion. This workshop blends secrets with startup-tested frameworks to help you craft a pitch that actually lands. Learn the anatomy of an irresistible pitch, practice pitching, and get real-time feedback from a former Imagineer and creative executive who’s pitched everything from billion-dollar theme parks to five-minute film ideas. Expect live coaching, laughter, and lightning-fast lessons that stick.

title: The Future Design Language of Robots
description: As robots enter homes, hospitals, and public spaces, their design language — how they look, move, and behave — will shape how we trust and relate to them. This session explores emerging visual, interactional, and behavioral grammars of human-robot interaction. Must robots mimic humans or machines, or can new forms evolve? We’ll examine how design can expand the roles robots play and the relationships we build with them across cultures and contexts.

title: I’m Not Creative (And Other Lies We Tell Ourselves)
description: In a world where AI tools can generate logos, write copy, and compose music in seconds, what does creativity look like for the rest of us—especially those who don’t carry a “creative” title? This workshop is for the engineers, strategists, analysts, producers, and anyone else who’s ever said, “I’m not creative.” Through a series of provocative exercises and hands-on prompts, we’ll explore how creativity is evolving beyond traditional mediums—into product flows, systems thinking, code, and prompt design.

title: Low-Key Legends: Personal Branding for Introverts
description: This session with author of "Personal Branding for Introverts" and branding expert and ex-Forbes Sr. Contributor Goldie Chan focuses on empowering introverts to embrace their unique strengths to build a personal brand while struggling less. Attendees will learn how to harness quiet confidence for storytelling, develop strategies to connect with audiences without overwhelming social demands, and create a branding approach that aligns with their personality.

title: The Next Digital Wave Excludes Millions. Let’s Fix It.
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Our digital world risks leaving groups behind. This panel issues a call-to-arms for truly inclusive design in physical spaces, where technology amplifies human potential. With 20% of the world's population identifying as neurodistinct in some way, we'll unveil a blueprint for environments that care for all human needs. Explore how Google and Hypersolid are developing adaptable guidelines to unlock unique cognitive strengths - fostering engagement, productivity, and belonging. Join a revolution of the human mind where every mind shines.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: No Time for Safe Bets: Creative Impact Under Fire
description: When disaster strikes, there’s no time for creative review. Just hours after violence, fires, hurricanes, war, or other crises strike, frontline groups like World Central Kitchen and Watch Duty hit the ground to save lives, and their marketing and comms teams begin rallying attention and mobilizing donations to make it all possible. In this session, WCK’s Linda Roth, Chief Communications and Strategy Officer, and Liz Caselli-Mechael, Chief Communications Officer at Watch Duty, join Swayable CEO James Slezak to reveal how some of the world’s most important campaigns are using AI and cutting-edge measurement technology to develop authentic, purpose-driven content that works—quickly.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Not For Me: The Emotional Cost of Exclusionary Design
description: What does it feel like to be told — subtly or explicitly — that a product isn’t meant for you? From voice assistants that ignore accents, to websites that don’t account for disability, exclusionary design sends a powerful message: you don’t belong here/this product isn't for you. In this session, we’ll explore how design decisions shape belonging, dignity, and trust—and what happens when that’s broken. Drawing from real user stories, this talk will spark a conversation on what it takes to move beyond surface-level inclusion.

title: Perspectives on Cyber Security & Human Rights: Three Cases
description: Practitioners from industry (Google), academia (Columbia-SIPA) and civil society (Access Now) will show how cybersecurity impacts human rights. Drawing on research with vulnerable and marginalized communities, including human rights defenders, independent journalists, and the elderly, they will show how people in diverse circumstances attempt to navigate digital spaces safely and freely with few resources. They will offer lessons and debate best practices and norms in cyber capacity-building, corporate human rights due diligence, and technology-informed advocacy from an intersectional lens.

title: See Where the Money Goes: Rebuild Trust in Humanitarian Aid
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Trust is under pressure across industries, but in the humanitarian sector it has become existential. As public skepticism around waste, corruption, and opaque systems grows, support for aid can disappear fast—as seen in 2025 with sweeping cuts to U.S. foreign assistance driven by accountability concerns.This session explores how blockchain is reshaping humanitarian aid by enabling real-time, verifiable tracking of stablecoin-based funds from donor to recipient. We’ll examine what trust-by-design systems mean not just for aid, but for any sector grappling with accountability at scale.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Theory to Practice: Google’s Responsible Approach to AI
description: Every day, more of us interact with AI, prompting deeper questions about how these products are built. This workshop will allow participants to understand the intricacies of LLM training, providing a deeper grasp of how these models truly function. More critically, we'll dive into the essential strategies for mitigating risks and building safeguards into generative AI products. Participants will engage in activities to understand AI red teaming, using golden sets to calibrate AI responses to sensitive prompts, and discuss the potential and limits of various approaches.

title: Who Gets to Shape the Future of AI? Talent, Policy & Power
description: AI is reshaping every industry—but will America remain the world’s innovation engine? As visa rules tighten and geopolitical suspicion grows, top AI talent is being diverted elsewhere. This panel explores how U.S. immigration, research, and tech policy decisions are influencing the global AI race. Drawing on history and current data, we’ll ask: Can the U.S. still attract the world’s best minds—or will they build the future somewhere else?

title: Why SMB Cyber Attacks Could Cripple the U.S. Economy
description: Small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) account for 99.9% of U.S. businesses and nearly half of GDP, yet their cybersecurity capabilities lag dangerously behind. Nation-state actors and cybercriminals now target SMBs and the managed service providers (MSPs) that support them because one breach can cascade across thousands of businesses, posing a serious risk to national security and economic stability. As AI supercharges social engineering and ransomware at scale, this overlooked attack surface has become one of the most urgent vulnerabilities in America.

title: AI Infrastructure: Building Stargate & Beyond
description: The AI revolution has changed how we work, play, and more. It is also driving a major shift in the backend infrastructure that powers these developments.As AI capabilities accelerate, leading tech companies are moving beyond software and into the physical world of data centers, power, and infrastructure. Why? Because the future of intelligence now depends as much on electricity and silicon as it does on algorithms.In this session, OpenAI pulls back the curtain on what it really takes to scale AI today. The conversation explores how data center development has become mission-critical, how decisions about where to build are made, and what happens when AI growth collides with real-world limits such as energy, land, and supply chains.From energy partnerships to site selection, this conversation will reveal how data center development has shaped the landscape in nearby locations – such as OpenAI’s Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas – and perhaps someday far away in Earth’s orbit.

title: AI: Friend or Foe of DEIB?
description: Headlines warn that AI is hurting DEIB—propagating bias, reinforcing stereotypes, and risking the erasure of marginalized groups and their histories. Yet the same technology has the potential to uncover bias, scale representation, and raise the bar for inclusion at the brand, industry, academic, and societal levels.With a consumer-focused lens, panelists from MAC Cosmetics, the UN, NYU, and Responsible AI company, SeeMe Index, will explore both sides of the equation: where AI’s blind spots have failed us, where it’s driving progress, and what it takes to ensure technology reflects the world we want to see.

title: Architects of Emotion: Designing for Human Connection
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Today’s most iconic venues are more than just architectural marvels; they’re immersive, tech-powered environments designed to bring people together. Join leaders in architecture and brand experience to explore how large-scale stadiums and next-gen immersive destinations are crafted to amplify emotion, ignite human connection, and become part of the stories unfolding within them. These venues go beyond just aesthetics to create shared experiences that resonate long after the moment.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Eternal Icons: Tetris, Sonic & The Art of Timeless Play
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Some stories never fade—they evolve. This session explores how iconic franchises like Tetris and Sonic continue to shape culture and spark creativity. Maya Rogers, President & CEO of Tetris, and Shuji Utsumi, President & COO of SEGA, share how legacy IP adapts to new generations and drives innovation. Moderated by Greg Off, a 20-year game industry veteran and CEO of creative agency Off Base, this panel dives into the enduring power—and future—of beloved entertainment worlds.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Real-World Impact of AI: Opportunities, Risks and Intellectual Property Oh My!
description: AI is the most transformative technology of our generation. This session explores the promise and perils of AI, sharing concrete examples of how AI is being applied with impact by businesses, creators and others to drive operational efficiency, enhance customer experiences, increase revenues and augment creativity. The audience will also learn about the risks associated with AI including some novel intellectual property issues (IP) posed, and how to weigh opportunities with risks. The insights shared are from a speaker who has built AI solutions and been at the top levels of technology, law, business and government and boardroom, all of which perspectives are critical to the successful AI applications.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: From Neanderthal DNA to the Dark Genome: Diversity Explained
description: What sets modern humans apart from our cave-dwelling ancestors? The answer: genetic variations that may have hindered Neanderthals from advancing socially & technologically. That's according to Alysson Muotri, PhD, whose work creating brain organoids from stem cells — & studying them on Earth & in space — is shedding light on the factors that make present-day people unique — & especially susceptible to neurological conditions like Alzheimer's & autism. Plus: how the Dark Genome might shape personality, & why it all matters for the future of human aging, medicine, & the colonization of Mars.

title: The Future of Truth: How AI Reshapes Reality
description: Truth has never been straightforward. But now, AI offers to solve it—filter the noise, rank the sources, settle the debate. We’re told it’ll help us see clearly across politics, science, work, art, and justice. But what if the answers it gives us come with hidden terms? In this talk, Steven Rosenbaum unpacks the uneasy promise of algorithmic truth—and what’s lost when machines start shaping what counts as real. The Future of Truth: How AI Shapes Reality.

title: Generative Ghosts: AI Afterlives & the Future of Memory
description: As generative AI systems and AI agents increasingly model individuals, both living and dead, we find ourselves on the verge of a new kind of digital immortality. “Generative ghosts” are AI-based simulations that evoke or imitate human beings, created through personal data, online traces, or speculative design. But what are the social, ethical, and emotional consequences of engaging with these digital afterlives?This panel brings together thought leaders from academia and industry to explore the benefits and risks of AI-generated personas.

title: Hey Marketers: Get Out of the Feed and Into the Chat
description: The internet has gone quiet, at full volume. Public feeds are louder than ever, but the most meaningful conversations - and the real signals of what people actually care about - are now happening in private spaces. According to GIPHY's research, 90 percent of people share major life updates in group chats before they post them publicly. More than half of Gen Z says their chat knows them better than their family. And every day, 50 percent more GIFs are sent than voice memos. This talk goes inside the shift from public to private sharing, and what it means for brands, creators, and communicators.

title: How to Build AI-First Products: Models, Memory, Mastery
description: AI is reshaping how we build software products—from conversational UX to fast-evolving AI models that can upend product roadmaps and even entire products. In this session, a leading product exec and a Wharton AI expert share how to design products powered by large language models. You'll learn how AI is reshaping product roles, what it takes to build with ever-evolving models, and why great UX now means thinking in conversations, not clicks.

title: I Think of You Dying: Storytelling That Stings (and Stays)
description: Bland is forgettable—and in today's content-saturated world, forgettable is fatal. This session explores how emotionally evocative, creatively risky campaigns like Life360’s “I Think of You Dying”—which tapped into the quiet fear every parent holds—confront the gap between what people truly feel and what brands are willing to say. Hear from creative leaders who are leaning into discomfort to reflect human truths and challenge taboos. The conversation will explore boundaries, backlash, and how pushing limits can drive impact when rooted in authenticity and deep audience understanding.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Latine/x Voices in Film: Creatives Meet-Up!
description: Join Cine Las Americas to celebrate local Latine/x voices in film and the vibrant creative community thriving in Austin. This is a chance to connect with international filmmakers and engage with a platform dedicated to amplifying Latine/x stories. Cine Las Americas, alongside organizations like Malakita Productions and filmmaker Alejandro Pedemonte, showcase how local talent shapes the global film landscape.  Whether you're seeking collaboration, inspiration, or a pathway to share your vision, we invite you to connect and be a part of this vibrant growing community.

title: Pharma vs. Supplements: Breaking the False Health Dichotomy
description: The future of health isn't about choosing between pharmaceuticals and supplements – it's about understanding how both can work together. This panel explores the need for science-backed supplementation as food systems evolve and health challenges become more complex. We'll break down the false dichotomy between pharmaceuticals and natural supplements, highlighting how reactive medicine and proactive supplementation can coexist. Through expert insights, we'll bridge pharmaceutical literacy with supplement science, empowering consumers to make informed decisions about integrative nutrition.

title: Restorative by Design: Reimagining Wellbeing for Our Cities
description: What if true well-being comes not from self-optimization but from collective care? This session explores how we can design public spaces for rest, connection, and restoration. Industry experts share why parks, bathhouses, and shared spaces matter more than ever. From global traditions and new urban experiments, they make the case for new public institutions reviving communal well-being outside of commodified services or tech-driven wellness.

title: Separating science from science fiction in business content
description: One skill never goes out of demand: clear, memorable communication. No matter who you’re designing for, your words and visuals need to stick. But most of what we’re taught about content design is well-intended fiction From “just keep it simple” to “use more pictures,” we follow rules that feel right, but often backfire. There’s no shortage of advice on how to design content that sticks in memory, but how much of it is backed by brain science? This session explores how myths related to repetition, emotion, complexity, imagery and more influence all aspects of user design and experience.

title: UNCOMPETE: Choosing Collaboration Over Competition
description: The need to compete is deeply ingrained in every aspect of our lives and work. It’s often viewed as a positive: we are told it motivates us to do our best, to work hard, that it drives innovation and excellence. But, what if that’s a lie? In this session, Ruchika T. Malhotra, in conversation with author, speaker and HBR editor Amy Gallo, offers a new framework for flourishing at work and in life, by highlighting five key principles to mythbust what we've been taught about competition, rewrite the rules, and build cultures of collaboration, solidarity, and mutuality—that lift everyone up.

title: What Happens When Software Can Transact Autonomously?
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Autonomous AI agents are moving beyond recommendations to making decisions and executing transactions on our behalf—reshaping the shopping journey itself. As commerce becomes software-mediated, experiences are increasingly optimized for machines, not humans, changing discovery, choice, and brand interaction. While agent-led commerce reduces friction, it raises new questions around trust and accountability.This session explores how agentic commerce reshapes incentives for merchants, platforms, developers, and consumers, and introduces the technical frameworks enabling agent-led transactions.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: You, Reorged: The Future of Marketing Careers
description: AI is transforming marketing careers, not just the work itself. According to the 2025 CMO Survey, 17.2% of marketing efforts now involve AI and machine learning, with that share expected to reach 44.2% within three years. Traditional career paths are shifting, especially in fast-moving areas like advertising and branding, and hybrid roles like strategist plus prompt engineer are becoming the new norm. This panel dives into how to adapt, build in-demand skills, and navigate a career path that looks very different from even five years ago.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: KEYNOTE: Make Your Own Wave: Russ & Andreea Gleeson on Independence
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Chart topping artist/actor/author Russ sits down with TuneCore Chief Executive Officer Andreea Gleeson to discuss his path to success as an independent artist and the nontraditional fan engagement, release strategies, and alternative revenue streams that have paved the way for global stardom. From the foundation of his label DIEMON to his stint on the Columbia roster, his triumphant return to independence and the success of his latest Billboard chart topping album W!LD, Russ has built a career as a modern renaissance man on his own terms and serves as an inspiration for other aspiring artists.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: AI, Your Brain & the Battle for Cognitive Sovereignty
description: AI tools rewrite emails, generate ideas, even decide what we see and when. But will they make us lazier thinkers—or force us to think beyond? Will it make us think more clearly or manipulate our perspectives? Will it widen the cognitive gap between rich and poor? Are our neuro-rights protected by design—or will screen time just be optimized again? In this live debate, you’ll pick a side: is AI expanding or eroding our minds? Expect sharp provocations, real-time participation, and one rule: you can’t outsource your thinking here. Join the battle for cognitive sovereignty.

title: The AI-Powered Bionic Revolution Has Already Begun
description: As AI, robotics, and economies of scale converge, a new era of human enhancement is emerging. In this session, bionic limb pioneer Aadeel Akhtar reveals how shared breakthroughs between robots and humans are transforming assistive tech into accessible, intelligent extensions of the body. From real-time adaptive control to mass-producible bionic hands, this is a glimpse into a future where disability is no longer a limitation and AI is rewriting what the human body can do.

title: The Anime Advantage: Brand Strategy Meets Cultural Power
description: Anime is no longer a niche! It’s one of today’s most powerful tools for extending franchises, reimagining IP, and reaching global audiences. This panel, with leaders from Crunchyroll,  Dallas Mavericks, and Twitch, explores how talent, brands, and creators are using anime as both a narrative and business engine to fuel next-gen storytelling. For musicians, athletes, game studios, and fashion labels, anime offers a dynamic, fan-forward way to build immersive worlds, show up meaningfully in pop culture, and engage loyal audiences that drive long-term brand equity.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The Art of Attracting an Audience
description: With people exposed to up to 10,000 calls to action every day, it’s becoming increasingly difficult and expensive for organizations and personal brands to attract audiences. While many believe that more money leads to more awareness, and thus, more audience, research consistently shows otherwise. In fact, aggressive campaigns can backfire when people feel they’re being sold to. The magical key in effective campaigning is rooted in behavioral science, and during this session, people will learn how to create resonating campaigns from crafting communications all the way to advertising techniques.

title: Awakening to Light: Returning to Nature’s Rhythm
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: Light shapes biology. When spectrum and timing drift, circadian rhythms falter, degrading sleep, cognition, and long-term health. We move from hard science to possibility: technology that calibrates light to natural circadian cues, paired with daily practices - meditation, breath, mindful routines - to restore rhythm.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Behind the Scenes: Empowering Production with AI Tools
description: While generative AI dominates headlines, the true AI revolution in film and television is happening behind the scenes. From automated script breakdowns that assist with casting, budgeting, and identifying visual effects opportunities, to real-time compliance tools for flagging mature content themes, to simplified contract review for summarizing rights and talent obligations, artificial intelligence is transforming how film and television are planned, produced, and delivered. This panel unpacks how AI is streamlining production behind the scenes and raising new legal questions.

title: Break the Algorithm: Why Safe Marketing Is Dead
description: Playing it safe is the riskiest move a brand can make. In today’s culture, audiences crave authenticity, emotion, and tension - and the boldest campaigns are the ones that break through. Mariana O’Kelly, Executive Creative Director at Leo Chicago, explores how brands that embrace discomfort, controversy, and cultural tension are redefining connection in a crowded media landscape. From transforming missteps into cultural moments to using emotional storytelling that sparks conversation, this session reveals why bold thinking isn’t just creative, it’s essential for growth.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Designing Great Public Spaces to Power Economic Opportunity
description: Featuring the urban visionaries behind NYC's High Line and DC's 11th Street Bridge Park, this session will explore how we can transform America's abandoned spaces – from old rail lines to vacant bridges – into community-built urban oases. Drawing insights from their new book, "Inspiration from the Field," Scott and Kira will inspire park advocates, urban planners, and city-lovers alike to reimagine America’s post-industrial infrastructure as public assets that can connect rather than divide, while unlocking new economic opportunities and scalable investment into neighboring communities.

title: Dirty Data: The Hidden Climate Cost of Our Digital Lives
description: Data centers are the invisible engines behind AI, streaming, and cloud computing—but their carbon and resource footprints are growing fast. Decarbonizing these energy-hungry systems takes more than clean power; it requires a full-scale rethink of how we design, build, power, cool, and manage digital infrastructure. This panel brings together leaders in carbon capture, battery storage, green building, water management, and high-performance infrastructure to explore how we can decarbonize the digital world—from the foundations up.

title: Dissolve Lab: Clear Your Invisible Barrier Without Burnout
description: This immersive, hands-on workshop guides participants through The Dissolve: a nervous-system-safe method for moving through invisible barriers without force or burnout. You’ll name your own stuck point, map its emotional signature, and use tools like breathwork, the Feelings Wheel, and guided visualization to shift it. Walk away with a customized clarity map and a gentle, resonant next step—designed to move you forward with ease instead of push.

title: Featured Session: The Science of Scaling: Using Data to Decide When—and How Fast—to Scale Revenue
description: Are You Ready to Scale Sales? How Fast?These two questions are mission-critical to the success of any startup, product launch, or market expansion. Yet, too often we rely on gut feel—or let irrelevant signals like a recent fundraise or comparisons to past unicorns— to drive our decisions.The Science of Scaling offers a rigorous framework for founders, executives, and investors to calculate the answers using their company’s actual performance data—not wishful thinking.Drawing on insights from hundreds of startups over the past 25 years, Mark Roberge—Founding CRO at HubSpot, Senior Lecturer at Harvard Business School, and Co-Founder of Stage 2 Capital—reveals the five most common reasons revenue acceleration efforts fail.Whether you're a founder starting to scale, an investor guiding your portfolio, or a GM launching a new product, The Science of Scaling framework is your operating manual.Don’t guess. Don’t gamble. Scale scientifically.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Hands-On UX Design for Agentic AI
description: 2026 is the year AI goes fully agentic. UX needs to catch up—or risk sinking with the Figma Titanic. This hands-on workshop is your lifeboat, where we will sketch our way to survival through the stormy waters of agentic AI. Using pencils, sticky notes, and an LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.), we'll prototype autonomous agents using RAG, digital twins, and value matrices. Forget perfect pixels. This is low-fidelity, high-velocity UX for AI. The ship is sinking. Let’s design our way out.

title: Humans or AI? What Buyers Prefer, According to Neuroscience
description: AI is transforming business interactions, and one question looms large: do customers prefer human connection? Neuroscience offers a unique lens to answer this. This session shares surprising findings from recent studies tracking how buyers’ brains react in real time to AI agents vs. humans, including what makes AI persuasive, where humans outperform, and why AI often wins attention, but not memory. Drawing on brain scans, voice analysis, and eye tracking, this session reveals what’s actually happening inside your customer’s mind when they interact with AI tools and what to do about it.

title: A More Personal Computer: How AI is Transforming Desktops
description: In this session, MacPaw CTO Vira Tkachenko will explore how AI is redefining the concept of the “personal computer,” offering a more intuitive, private, and personalized experience. Drawing on her experience developing for the Apple ecosystem for 17+ years, she will share insights on building AI for macOS. Vira will trace the journey of Macs from their inception and highlight AI trends in software taking place across the industry. Attendees can learn how on-device intelligence enhances productivity, respects user privacy, and keeps the Mac a powerful tool for focused, meaningful work.

title: Quantifying the Unquantifiable: Changing How We Measure Pain
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Pain is currently measured on a qualitative scale first used 70 years ago, leaving many to feel like their experiences aren’t fully understood. With advances in technology, the healthcare industry is exploring how connected care systems, alongside emerging AI tools, can help change this model by quantifying the physiological changes a person undergoes when they are experiencing pain, potentially offering a new way to discuss and treat chronic pain. This session will review the realities of living with pain and how a deeper understanding of the way pain affects the body can improve treatment.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Seeing Is Believing: The Vision Behind Humanoid & Robotic AI
description: Humanoids and robots are moving from science fiction to reality, and vision is key to that evolution. Just as our visual cortex allows humans to perceive and adapt, machine vision gives robots the ability to understand, navigate, and interact with the world around them. This session explores how advances in depth sensing, AI compute, and cloud-scale learning are giving humanoids and robots sight, safety, and awareness to operate naturally among us—and what that means for the future of human-robot interaction.

title: Shot by Shot: From Music Videos to Feature Films
description: In this lively conversation between three filmmakers, you’ll hear how they’ve successfully leapt from directing music videos with famous artists to premiering acclaimed features at top global festivals. Emma Higgins’ debut, Sweetness, world-premiered at SXSW 2025; RT Thorne’s film, 40 Acres, had its US debut at SXSW 2025; and Floria Sigismondi’s film, The Runaways, has achieved cult status in pop culture. They will discuss the transition from the tight budgets of videos to the logistics of securing funding for their own stories and having the creative license to fulfill their visions.

title: TikTok vs. Real Doc? Doctor Approved Biohacking Tips
description:
Listen to live audio sessions from this venue, and get closed captions on your device.
