title: Will Journalism Survive AI - and Does it Matter?
description: Large language models scoop up news for training and live chatbots, but rarely pay a dime. Will they eventually put journalism out of business by self-generating “news” content or regurgitating data collected from gadgets and glasses? With trust and value in news plummeting, perhaps journalism’s role as a watchdog, forum for social exchange, and spark for civic engagement is already defunct. Or does original, ethical reporting offer something special? In this provocative session, experts in AI ethics, journalism and global trends share  divergent views on the future of journalism.

title: Work Reimagined: Building Attraction, Not Requirements
description: Stop fighting about office days and start designing workplace experiences that people actually want. This panel brings together LinkedIn's workforce research leader with real-time employee insights, the architect of Gensler's leading workplace experience platform, and Rivian's expert in customer community building. Discover why meaningful in-person connection becomes even more valuable as AI handles routine work, cutting edge examples of experimental workplaces, and proven strategies to create immediately measurable ROI from thoughtfully designed workplace experiences and communities.

title: Zig Zag: Having a Weird Career
description: What happens when your career makes zero sense on paper—but gets you exactly where you need to be? Two women share their very non-linear journeys: from the Air Force to Harvard to a gratitude startup, and from baking pies to founding a startup. In a world obsessed with “coherent” resumes, this conversation explores how detours, pivots, and reinventions are not just valid—but powerful. Expect laughs, real talk, and permission to break the rules.

title: Accelerating Discovery: The Role of Agentic AI in Science
description: The new generation of AI models and agentic AI systems are fundamentally transforming the pace of scientific discovery, offering new ways to solve humanity's most complex challenges. By supercharging productivity and augmenting human expertise, these tools help researchers push the boundaries of knowledge faster than ever before. From synthesizing vast scientific literature and generating novel hypotheses to creating new research plans and executing simulations, agentic AI acts as a powerful collaborator in the lab. We are entering a new era of collaborative science, one where AI works alongside researchers to turn the next iteration into the next great breakthrough.

title: Bespoke: How AI is Ushering in the Era of Precision Genetics
description: The pioneering base-editing method of Alexis Komor, PhD, was recently used to create a custom therapy for an infant with carbamoyl phosphate synthetase 1, a deadly rare disease. Her colleague, Gene Yeo, PhD, favors another new approach: RNA-targeting precision therapeutics, which he used to target ALS & aid a child with developmental epileptic encephalopathy. Both employ AI to discover the roots of polygenic diseases—from the ultra-common like diabetes & heart disease to the ultra-rare—& decode the Dark Genome. More on the promise the technology holds for the future of personalized treatment.

title: Building Reality: Generative AI for the Physical World
description: Generative AI has rapidly advanced in text, imagery, and 3D content, but its potential to shape the physical world remains largely untapped. This panel explores how AI can be used to generate designs for things that can be built, manufactured, or constructed, spanning products, machines, buildings, and infrastructure. Experts in computational design, AI research, and digital fabrication will discuss current challenges, technical hurdles, and the foundational work needed to unlock future impact and bring generative design into the realm of the physical.

title: Can Science Safeguard Earth’s Wildlife?
description: Habitat loss, climate change, and disease are threatening the ecological integrity of our planet. These threats are driving declines in wildlife populations, accelerated by the loss of genetic diversity. Biobanking, including the cryopreservation of living cells, coupled with advanced digital tools, provides an opportunity for a future where genetic diversity, species, and ecosystems can be protected and restored. This panel will discuss the urgency of protecting species—from microbes to megafauna—and how biobanking and digital strategies are helping give wildlife a lifeline.

title: Crafting Global Games with Local Insight and Cultural Fusion
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: Games aren’t just translated, they’re co-created with local communities from day one. This panel pulls back the curtain on how global hits come to life across markets without losing their soul. Hear behind-the-scenes stories on how early local insights fuel global growth, how culturally meaningful in-game experiences with players across regions drive deeper worldwide engagement, and how partnerships with regional hubs are building a global pipeline of creative talent. Nika will share a clear four-step framework to make culture and community central to game development, from concept to launch.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Creative AF: Women Creatives, Designers, & Content Hustlers
description: Whether you shoot it, write it, design it, sing it, or sell it - this space is for you.  Creative AF (aka Women Who Create Sh*t) is a high-energy, no-BS meetup for women-identifying creatives, founders, filmmakers, freelancers, content hustlers, and brand builders who are making sh*t happen. Come for fast-paced connection games, resource swaps, and real talk & stay for the energy and photo-ops you’ll actually want to post. No panels. No gatekeeping. Just powerful, talented women building cool things and backing each other up.

title: Designed to Feel: How Tech Brands Build Emotional Worlds
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Today’s most successful tech brands don’t just launch products - they build emotional universes. This session explores how brands are moving beyond screens to connect across sound, space, motion and mood. From sonic identities to tactile packaging and immersive design systems, we’ll unpack how multisensory storytelling creates emotional connections—the real competitive edge in the AI age. This isn’t about rejecting technology - it’s about designing through it to bring feeling and meaning back into experience. Expect provocations and a manifesto for the future of emotionally intelligent design.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Breaking Through Barriers to Innovation: Lessons from Space and Earth
description: New paradigm-shifting technologies seem to emerge every day, and the leaders that understand how to properly leverage the opportunities that come along with these new technologies can create huge competitive advantages for their organizations. Dava Newman, Ph.D., a futurist and the MIT Apollo Program Professor of Astronautics (as well as the former director of the MIT Media Lab and the first woman engineer to have served as a NASA deputy administrator) will reveal creative strategies for taking advantage of these opportunities. Learn how to make the most from technology breakthroughs in areas such as finding life elsewhere in the universe; climate+energy, and the convergence of artificial intelligence and generative biology. Dava’s mantra of “Love, Act, Discover, Innovate” urges leaders in business, government, and academia to courageously consider how innovation can truly change the way we engage with each other and our planet.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Sex in Outer Space & the Researchers Making It Happen (Soon)
description: Building on a 2023 SXSW presentation, this panel will be a light-hearted but highly educational conversation on the current science focusing on past, present, and future questions around human sexual activity in outer space environments - whether on private commercial space stations of the near future, or settlements on the surface of the Moon, Mars, and beyond. Panelists will include some of the world's leading recognized experts in this fascinating and highly specific research field, covering everything from space medicine and biological reproduction to psychosocial issues and crew dynamics.

title: American Dream 2.0: How Cities Will Design Our Future
description: America turns 250 next year, but the original American Dream is broken and outdated. If Singapore can build for efficiency and Copenhagen for equity, what would an American city of the future look like? Civic Innovator founder Andreas Addison invites attendees into a real-time design challenge to reimagine the American Dream for the next 250 years. Grounded in personal story, Brookings research, and proven civic wins from working in City Hall to serving on City Council, this talk introduces a 7-step Civic Innovation process to help cities design for impact with new systems that work for us.

title: Beyond Beautiful: A Data-Driven Framework for Design ROI
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Great design is often treated as an expense rather than an investment because we lack concrete methods to measure its business impact. Drawing from proven data valuation methodologies, we'll introduce a practical three-component framework that translates design improvements into measurable business outcomes. We will reference real-world case studies and demonstrate how design decisions directly influence Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), walking through an example showing how a typical e-commerce checkout redesign can generate exponential ROI.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Build Community Through Storytelling
description: The fastest way to create community isn’t networking.It’s shared humanity.In this highly interactive session, FABx founder Colleen Schell and guests guide you through a storytelling experience designed to take the room from strangers to a connected community. You’ll explore why story is more than entertainment: it’s a cultural technology that dissolves separation, builds trust quickly, and invites people to belong. Learn how to share your most potent story and walk away with a powerful tool for connection. This isn’t a panel. It’s an experiment in connection through sharing your story.

title: Come to Your Senses: Designing Analog Tools for a Digital World
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: How do you create analog products that resonate in an increasingly digital world? As interest in tactile, sensory experiences grows, designers are rethinking how physical objects engage us—through touch, sound, even scent. From the feel of materials to the rhythm of use, these sensory cues shape how we connect with the tools around us. In this fireside chat, leaders in analog design explore why slowing down matters, what it takes to craft products that invite presence, and how intentionally designed objects can offer a counterbalance to digital speed.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Defending Democracy: How Cities Rise Up When Feds Push Down
description: As federal authoritarianism threatens civil rights, immigration, and economic justice, cities are stepping up to defend democracy. Join District 2 Austin City Councilmember Vanessa Fuentes, at-Large Philadelphia City Councilmember Kendra Brooks, and Minneapolis City Council Member Jason Chavez as they share diverse, frontline perspectives in this conversation moderated by Francesca Menes, Managing Director of the Membership & Organizing Team at Local Progress. If democracy survives this moment, it will be because cities held the line.

title: From Policy to Practice: AI Implementation in Government
description: Artificial intelligence is no longer theoretical. Across the country, governments are moving from conversations about AI governance to real-world deployment — modernizing services, strengthening cybersecurity, improving legislative analysis, and rethinking how the public sector operates. But implementation is where the real work begins.

title: How to Be Seen: Building Your Personal Brand
description: Your reputation is your career capital. In this hands-on workshop, Alana O’Grady Lauk, a VP of Communications, and Katy Boos, a top thought leadership strategist, will guide you through why your personal brand matters, how to build it with confidence, and what visibility can unlock. You'll learn how to show up online and in real life in ways that feel authentic and sustainable. Expect real examples, actionable tools, and small group exercises designed to help you clarify your message, identify your platforms and target audiences, grow your presence, and speak with purpose.

title: John Paul DeJoria: Success Unshared is Failure
description: Entrepreneur and philanthropist John Paul DeJoria joins SXSW to discuss his soon-to-be-released memoir, Success Unshared is Failure – telling the story of his journey from twice experiencing homelessness to co-founding billion-dollar brands like John Paul Mitchell and Patrón Spirits. The book’s title is his personal motto and reflects his life’s mission to invest in people, animals, and the plant through his JP’s Peace Love and Happiness Foundation. DeJoria is celebrating the 15th anniversary of his foundation this year, and has long committed himself to supporting resources which uplift Austin residents, including food, housing opportunity, and support services; infrastructure projects like Patriots' Hall of Dripping Springs and the renovation of the historic Paramount Theater; as well as arts programming like the Austin and ConnectHER film festivals. This conversation will be moderated by Alan Graham, founder and CEO of Mobile Loaves & Fishes in Austin, a long-time partner of JP’s Peace Love & Happiness Foundation. Together, they will discuss some of the stories DeJoria shares in Success Unshared is Failure, including starting JPMS with just a $700 loan, knocking on doors as an encyclopedia salesman, riding motorcycles with the Hells Angels, navigating international conflicts, and so much more.

title: Medicated for Life? Antidepressants & Conflicting Science
description: Are antidepressants really that hard to stop? Do the benefits outweigh the risks? Scientists can’t seem to agree on a definitive answer. And yet, Dr. Mark Horowitz has some 20,000 emails in his inbox from Americans who feel trapped on their meds. With insights from his clinical research at University College London, and his own harrowing journey tapering off antidepressants, Dr. Horowitz, the world leading expert in the science of safely stopping psychiatric meds, exposes the uncomfortable truths behind one of today’s most controversial topics in mental health.

title: The RAI of Every Day Things - from Principles to UX Practice
description: Responsible AI isn’t a buzzword—it’s a daily practice that drives the appropriate level of trust, mitigates risk, and powers better business outcomes. But what does it look like in product and design? It’s a practical, human-centered approach to designing AI systems that are safe, equitable, and effective. In this session, RAI leaders from Microsoft, SAP, Adobe, and JPMorgan Chase share real tools, UX strategies, and lessons from the trenches. Gain an understanding of RAI - and walk away with actionable best practices to apply now, whether you're leading solo or scaling across an enterprise.

title: The Shortcut to Better Video for Overloaded Teams
description: Most teams need video but don’t have time to wade through tutorials or waste money on the wrong tools. This interactive, hands-on workshop shows you how to create high-credibility video using what you already have. Bring your phone (and any gear you like). We’ll cover light, sound, composition, and story, plus which tools are worth it and where AI helps. We’ll also review editing platforms (hint: one popular option grants broad rights to your content once uploaded—even if you never publish it).

title: Trust, Tech & Disruption: Brand Love When Stakes are High
description: In a world where travel is unpredictable, housing is uncertain, and consumers are pulling back, how do brands that serve high-consideration decisions earn trust and love? This panel features leaders from Vrbo, Southwest Airlines, and Realtor.com — three brands navigating economic volatility, shifting expectations, and technological change while staying emotionally resonant. We'll explore how tech platforms build human connection, why emotional loyalty matters more than ever, and how creative strategies can evolve to meet the moment.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Unbland Your Climate Brand
description: Before climate tech can change the world, it needs to attract customers. So why do so many climate companies look like they’re trying to blend into the background?  This highly visual show-and-tell session is about the power of expressive design to drive growth. Join industry leaders who’ve built category-defining brands as they dive into climate design visual clichés and discuss standout brands using design to drive growth. They’ll also reveal the design choices in their work that made customers pay attention and what they learned building brands people want.

title: Unlocking the Power of Community in the Post-Social Era
description: The marketing landscape is in chaos. As attention spans contract, audiences retreat to private digital spaces and ad costs rise, traditional playbooks are becoming obsolete. Sara Wilson identified this shift in 2020, coining “digital campfires” in Harvard Business Review to describe Gen Z’s move toward intimate communities. In this talk, she shares her community-powered marketing framework—shaped by years at Instagram and advising Fortune 500s—showing how prioritizing relevance over reach by tapping into organic communities drives attention, loyalty, and ROI.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: What AI Can’t Replace: People Skills in a Changing World
description: AI is changing how we work. But the people skills that help teams thrive, like communication, time management, and adaptability, are here to stay and more crucial than ever (92% of companies say soft skills matter as much or more than hard skills). Learn what makes human skills like feedback, prioritization, and resilience durable in an AI-powered workplace as well as practical ways to grow these essential capabilities into scalable habits, starting with small behaviors that create a big impact.

title: Building the Alien: Morphology, Mutation & Future Life
description: What if we didn’t just search for alien life—but built it? Drawing from Proxima Kósmos, a research project exploring how life and intelligence might emerge under radically different conditions, this session examines the intersection of scientific experimentation and design. Claire Webb and designer Shane Griffin present Xenoterra—a modeled world inspired by evolutionary robotics and AI—that reimagines how we define life itself.

title: Design in Tech Report 2026: UX to AX
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Designers make screens/flows for stakeholders to review, for users to test, and for many teams to adjust until everything matches the brand. But as AI acts on human intent directly, we’ll be designing for agents. Not users. Grounded in research, this talk explores the shift to Agentic Experience (AX), moving from crafting interfaces to orchestrating outcomes with zero visual affordances. Early-career designers will snap to this new reality, late-stage folks get to skip it, but mid-career Product people have the most to lose and need to redesign themselves for the AX era.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Featured Session: Human-Centered Storytelling: Create Connection & Community
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: The most successful brands and leaders tell stories that move people deeply, spur connection and create community. Cheryl Miller Houser provides a powerful storytelling framework that achieves this by tapping into universally shared human emotions to foster understanding and belonging. Participants leave with actionable tactics they can apply across all platforms to build strong brands, shape culture, ignite loyalty, drive growth and mobilize positive change.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Female Voices Shaping the Next Era of Preventive Health
description: Personalized health and longevity are no longer niche topics—they’re becoming cultural priorities that will define the future of medicine, wellness, and human potential. This panel brings together leading female voices in integrative medicine, nutrition, longevity, and lifestyle optimization to explore how cutting-edge science and holistic approaches are reshaping the way we think about aging and long-term vitality. From cellular function to optimizing hormones, nutrition, brain performance, and exercise routines, these experts will share what’s next in the rapidly evolving field of longevity—and why women are uniquely positioned to lead this transformation.

title: The Future of Health Care is at Home
description: AI’s real power won’t be unlocked inside the hospital walls. Its transformative potential lies in the continuous, context-rich, patient-generated data that never sets foot in a hospital—data from daily life, daily behaviors, and daily decisions. When care moves into the home—when conditions are monitored passively, when early warning signs are detected days before symptoms surface, and when interventions can be nudged in real time—AI stops being a convenience tool. It becomes a true clinical force multiplier.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How to Be Seen, Heard, and Remembered on LinkedIn
description: LinkedIn is where your reputation takes shape in public. In this session, Don F. McLean reads from The In Crowd for LinkedIn Mastery (#1 in its category on Amazon in its first week!) and shares how professionals can build a presence that opens doors. Attendees will learn how to communicate clearly, show up with consistency, and turn visibility into meaningful opportunity. This talk challenges outdated habits and introduces a sharper, more strategic way to use LinkedIn for growth.

title: How to Build a B2B brand with Swagger: Lessons from B2C
description: In this talk, the Chief Brand Officer of ServiceNow offers three rules that B2B marketers can use to build a great brand, based on his B2C experiences. He will share tactics he is using to connect his brand with audiences and show up in unconventional places – Tribeca Film Fest, Aston Martin Aramco F1 cars, ads starring acclaimed actor Idris Elba – and how you can apply the lessons he’s learned to your own company, regardless of your budget. Discover why sounding like a human, personalizing your pitch, and using AI to fuel (not replace) your creativity are the keys to winning the brand wars.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Making a Mind Live from SXSW: When AI Agents Leave the Lab
description: Everyone's talking about AI agents, but what actually happens when companies deploy them to do real work? This live recording of the "Making a Mind" podcast explores the gap between how we think agentic AI should work and how it actually behaves in the wild. Cognitive scientist and podcast host Dr. Danielle Perszyk sits down with Michael Giannangeli, head of product, agentic AI for Amazon Nova, plus industry voices from Amanda Doerr, vice president of core shopping at Amazon and more who've recently launched agents into production. Together, they'll dissect real implementation stories—the successes, the surprises, and the moments where human expectations collided with AI reality. We'll explore the fundamental questions emerging as AI agents become collaborators: Where do humans want control versus autonomy? How do we build trust with systems whose reasoning we can't see? And how does cognitive science research translate into product decisions—from tool-calling architecture to the design choices that determine when agents act independently versus ask for help? This isn't a demo. It's an honest conversation about what we're learning as agentic AI moves from theory to practice—and what it reveals about both artificial and human intelligence.

title: Say Vagina: Platform Censorship, Algorithms, and the Cost to Women's Health
description: Across the country, platforms like Meta and the NYC MTA are blocking ads and posts that mention vaginal health, fertility or menopause— censoring essential medical terms and allowing misinformation to spread. In this panel, brand leaders and marketers will explore how censorship is shaping the way women’s health is discussed— or not discussed— in public spaces, and how companies are navigating censorship challenges. It will examine why naming matters and how bold, stigma-breaking messaging can educate, empower, and shift the narrative.

title: Serious Fun: How to Bring Game Mechanics into Product Design
description: Serious products don't have to be seriously boring. In fact, a little play might be the power-up your product needs. This talk explores how proven game mechanics like feedback loops, progression systems, and "juice" can supercharge your product's design, making it intuitive and addictive (in a good way!). Drawing from indie game dev, client work with major brands, and 40+ game jams, I'll show you how thinking like a game designer creates playful, emotionally resonant experiences. Learn to make your products more rewarding, alive, and user-loved. When in doubt, add more "juice"!

title: Smells Like Brand: The Future of Scent Branding
description: Every brand has a scent, whether designed or not. Now, brand leaders can control it. This panel explores the frontier of scent branding and the new AI-powered technology that enables it. With the development of olfactory intelligence (OI), brands can harness our most powerful sense, capturing consumers’ memories and influencing their associations. Hear from Generation by Osmo’s Founder and Master Perfumer on how OI has made fragrance accessible for brands and creators of all sizes, and from brand leaders harnessing its power to develop signature scents, including the Museum of Pop Culture.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: ACCESS IS POWER: Who Gets to Participate, and Why It Matters
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: From arenas and sports to workplaces and boardrooms, access determines who gets seen, heard, and valued. Accessibility is cultural power, not just compliance. This challenges the audience to rethink how all spaces – both physical and digital – can include, or exclude, entire communities and identify cultural gatekeepers. It also highlights the dangers of exclusivity and the risks and hard work ahead.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: AI & Human Thriving: The Droids We’re Looking For
description: Senate hearings. Teen suicides. Parents demanding new laws. Whatever happened to the cheerful sci-fi future our movies and TV promised us? We’re a long way from AI like Data and C-3PO, who help us be us without pretending to be us. AI doesn’t have to be this bleak. But the deeper question isn’t better guardrails or tougher regulation. It’s what we ask AI to optimize for: human thriving, or human imitation?Anastasia Uglova, founder of Better Half, argues AI needs something the industry hasn’t built yet: relational intelligence — the capacity to drive trust, agency, and real connection.

title: Beyond the 9-to-5: Rewrite Your Success with a Portfolio Career
description: Explore how the traditional career path (maybe even yours soon) can be replaced by multi-stream careers that combine financial independence with impact. Join us to learn how you can build a career that offers true flexibility - of place, time, and choice - by thinking like a remote entrepreneur, even if you aren’t one yet. Designing a lean, sustainable portfolio with minimal overhead can help you turn your true passions and causes into scalable income streams, one venture at a time.

title: Brands Built for Fandom Report
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Fandom has become one of the last true engines of belonging, meaning, and participation as shared institutions fade and identity moves online. This report, from SOON Future Studies and SoHo Experiential, explores how fandom evolved from subculture to cultural infrastructure and why it now carries the emotional weight of legacy systems. We outline what this shift means for brands and how fandom has become not just what people love but where they choose to live.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Built for Change: The Human OS for Enterprise Agility
description: Leaders face a perfect storm: AI evolving faster than organizational capability while economic volatility and talent burnout intensify. Decision windows are shortening, and strategy alone no longer suffices—organizations must be intentionally built for change. Project Management Institute (PMI) convenes senior leaders across talent, technology, and transformation to examine the human operating system—leadership mindset, organizational structure, and governance strategies—that empower enterprises to detect disruptions promptly, make decisive decisions, and deliver consistent value.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Everything Is a Touchpoint: Designing Cohesive Brand Moments
description: Every interaction tells your brand’s story. As marketing guru Seth Godin reminds us, “Every interaction, in any form, is branding.” This workshop explores how every facet of your brand, from your website to a digital campaign or even an internal memo, creates a lasting impression. Join us as we break down universal principles of elevated design, brand consistency, and intuitive user experience, drawing on real-world examples. This session blends inspiration with hands-on practice, equipping you with actionable methods for strengthening brand cohesion across every touchpoint you influence.

title: F*ck Bauhaus: A Global Meetup to Recode Creative Work Meet Up
description: This is for designers, strategists, technologists, and creative misfits from around the world who refuse to let automation flatten our craft. We’ve designed a dynamic format: a built to spark real connection, fast idea exchange, and collective learning from every person in the room. Whether you want to reshape your creative code, challenge the algorithm, or just find your global crew this is your table.

title: Featured Session: A Conversation with Nothing's CEO and Co-Founder Carl Pei
description: Join Carl Pei for a wide-ranging conversation about where the tech industry is heading in 2026 - and why Nothing is on a mission to make tech for the next generation. In this session, Carl will reflect on how the consumer tech industry became closed and predictable, and why the next era will require a different mindset. He will share his perspective on some of the biggest shifts facing the industry, including the rise of AI-native hardware, and how AI is becoming a core part of the product experience. The conversation will also touch on Nothing’s own next chapter - from its continued push into audio to the brand’s growing physical footprint with new store openings planned across New York City, Tokyo, London, and India this year. Join Carl for a conversation about rebuilding belief in consumer technology, not by constant product launches, but thoughtful design, cultural relevance and products that people genuinely want to live with.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: How CMOs are Rewriting the Rules of Connection
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Modern CMOs have cracked the code: AI doesn't kill authenticity—it supercharges it. In this session, several CMOs skip the hype to share what actually works. They've turned algorithms into empathy and data into deeper human connections. No fluff, no buzzwords—just the real wins, messy learnings, and breakthrough moments that separate leaders from followers. These aren't your typical "AI will save us all" stories. They're practical insights on building marketing that genuinely serves people, not just metrics.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: The Innovation Engine You’ve Never Heard of: Your Neighbor
description: Traditional corporate innovation keeps ideas locked away until launch, but GE Appliances innovation arm, FirstBuild, has flipped the script—inviting passionate enthusiasts to co-create products from day one. The result is a 93% failure rate that just so happens to drive million-dollar successes like the Opal Nugget Ice Maker, which raised $2.7 million in crowdfunding after the community helped shape its development. This session will explore how major corporations can harness the power of co-creation, where rough transparency builds more trust than polished presentations, and where your best product ideas might come from a Reddit user with a 3D printer. Learn how FirstBuild proves that building in public, failing fast, and letting communities vote with their wallets creates better products than closed-door development—and why the future of innovation looks less like a pipeline and more like a playground.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: The Future of Development: Anthropic x Bolt.new A Case Study
description: Bolt.new went from $0 to $40M ARR in five months after launching on Claude 3.5 Sonnet — making it the fastest-growing customer in Anthropic's history. In this fireside chat, Bolt.new's CEO and CTO join Anthropic's Head of Applied AI to explore how AI is reshaping software development. They'll discuss the rise of "vibe coding," whether learning to code still matters, how the developer role is evolving from implementer to architect, and what's next as enterprise adoption accelerates. Expect candid takes on AI hype, scaling laws, technical debt, and what transformative AI actually looks like in production.

title: Future Relic: Failures, Disasters, Detours, and How I Made a Career as an Artist
description: “Inspiration is for amateurs. I’m a professional artist: I wake up every day and go to work.”This is the mindset that transformed Daniel Arsham from a student scraping by in his Brooklyn studio into one of the most sought-after artists of his generation. In Future Relic, he breaks through the mystique of the art world and shows what it really takes to make it in any creative endeavor.  He'll discuss the art of the grind: how to run a creative business and why surrounding yourself with ambitious, successful people is non-negotiable.

title: How AI Systems Will Make Brands More Human
description: As technology has evolved—from mass media to digital to social—brands have developed new abilities to interact with humans. The real transformation that brands undergo with AI won't be just about efficiency, but rather about showing up in the world in new ways that are adaptive, contextually aware, and authentic. Through recent case studies from Google, we'll explore how brands are embedding human creativity into AI systems to enable entirely new forms of human-brand interaction—and why this shift demands a fundamentally different approach to creativity.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: How Sleep Sounds & AI Detect Sickness Days Early
description: What if your breathing patterns could warn you that you are getting sick days before you have noticeable respiratory illness symptoms? The answer lies in sleep audio analysis powered by AI. This session explores how new advances can help us detect patterns far before our bodies notice. Sound is passive, honest, and scalable, and with AI trained on 3B nights of data, it can turn every bedroom into a node in an early-warning health network. AI has the power to turn sleep audio into a new public health infrastructure: one that listens, learns, and helps us act before symptoms even appear.

title: The Measurement Gap: Why Clicks No Longer Matter (And What Does)
description: Your marketing dashboard is likely missing half the story. Impressions, clicks, and keyword rankings were built for a world where Google sent traffic to websites. When 60% of searches end in AI-generated answers without clicks, those metrics don't fully connect to revenue. AI traffic from platforms like ChatGPT and Perplexity is already converting at 4-5x the rate of traditional search for some brands — but most marketing teams can't see it, let alone measure it.This session introduces the measurement framework for AI-first marketing: answer share (how often you appear in AI responses), citation rate (how often you're referenced as a source), and AI-driven conversions (revenue from users who never clicked a traditional link). We'll discuss how to design attribution experiments that prove impact, connect your brand’s AI visibility to actual business outcomes, and build an executive dashboard that ties AI performance to revenue.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: The NDA Economy: How Corporations Use Silence as Infrastructure
description: The weaponization of NDAs is a core structural feature of the economy that shapes corporate culture. Using cases from My NDA (World Premiering at SXSW 2026), panelists will expose how NDAs are used to protect brands; enable discrimination, harassment, and corruption to go unchecked; and destroy the careers of whistleblowers who risk everything to speak out. Panelists will also discuss what an ethical NDA looks like and the simple legislative fixes that can solve this problem.

title: Print’s Edge in a Culture of Immediacy
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom A
description: As digital immediacy accelerates creative output and reshapes our ideas of design quality, questions of intention and integrity are more urgent than ever. This session examines how print media—with its deliberate design and intentional editorial standards—offers a model for clearer communication and higher-quality work. We’ll explore how analog formats encourage depth, accountability, and thoughtful creativity in an era obsessed with speed.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Safety and Security in the Age of AI Autonomy
description: We’re entering an era where AI doesn’t just answer questions—it takes action. From calendar scheduling to full-stack coding, agentic systems are gaining autonomy, unlocking powerful capabilities and introducing novel risks. How do we secure systems that can act on our behalf? Who’s accountable when they fail? In this presentation, Dhamani and Engler, co-authors of the bestselling book Introduction to Generative AI, explore the future of safety, security, and human oversight in a world where AI systems don’t just assist us—they act for us.

title: Smarter Together: The Cognitive Toolkit for Better AI
description: As AI reshapes how we think, feel, and decide, this workshop explores how marketers, researchers, and creators can design more human-centered AI systems. Blending neuroscience, behavioral science, and real-world case studies, we’ll unpack the psychology behind trust, emotion, and collaboration in AI. Participants will leave with a framework for building AI that enhances—not replaces—human insight, helping teams navigate this new era with curiosity, empathy, and practical wisdom.

title: Turns Out People Buy From Brands They Actually Like
description: Join AG1’s CMO and its performance marketing agency, Tiger Tracks, as they discuss the interplay between brand and performance marketing. You’ve optimized the ads. You’ve split-tested the landing pages. You’ve chased the ROAS. So why does it keep getting harder? Because performance without brand is a treadmill – faster every quarter, going nowhere new. The brands winning right now aren’t the ones buying the most clicks. They are the ones people actually believe in. Come to find out how to become one of them.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Beyond Checkpoints: Immunotherapy’s Next Act
description: Checkpoint inhibitors opened the door; now a new wave of immunotherapy discoveries is poised to turn more cancers into curable diseases. MD Anderson’s Nobel Laureate James Allison – inventor of the first immune checkpoint inhibitor – and immunotherapy pioneer Padmanee Sharma sit down with 21-year, stage-IV cancer survivor Sharon Belvin and veteran TIME journalist Alice Park to chart where the science goes next and what still blocks its path. Attendees will learn how immunotherapy advances are changing the cancer landscape, including technologies to watch and a candid look at obstacles ahead.

title: Building the Next Generation of Trustworthy AI: From Generic Cloud Models to Custom, Local Intelligence
description: AI adoption is growing, but cloud-based one-size-fits-all models are hitting limits — high costs, privacy concerns, environmental damage, & latency issues. This session will explore why the next generation of AI will be specialized, expert, & local. Panelists will unpack how custom models, trained on proprietary data and deployed closer to the user, can deliver improved performance, privacy, & scalability with a more sustainable infrastructure. They'll share how this approach is reshaping businesses and privacy expectations — and what it takes to make it widespread.

title: Designing Attendee Experiences at the World’s Largest Events
description: The standard for live events has changed forever. Today’s attendees demand more than just a passive experience; they expect immersive, engaging and technologically advanced experiences. This session explores how the world’s most iconic events are not just meeting but exceeding these evolving expectations. We will dissect how cutting edge technology is transforming in-person experiences and creating unprecedented levels of attendee engagement and connection.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Designing for Fandom: Where Brand Meets Belonging
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom C
description: Fandoms aren’t audiences; they’re communities. And communities thrive on meaning, identity, and emotion. This panel explores how brands use design to speak the language of fans and build deep cultural connections. From anime to gaming to pop culture collabs, motion mnemonics, and type systems inspired by subculture, we’ll unpack how visual identity becomes a tool for trust, transformation, and belonging. When done right, design doesn’t just sell, it unites.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: DISABILITY: The Ultimate Innovation Driver
location: The Thompson Hotel Austin / Red River Ballroom
description: Some of the most important breakthroughs in design, technology, and culture came from need, not abundance. Tapping into this disparity is key. Magnifying the business case for innovation in all spaces reframes disability as a creative advantage that we should all actively seek insight from.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: David Pogue on Apple’s First 50 Years
description: As Apple enters its second half-century, author David Pogue (“Apple: The First 50 Years”) explores the throughlines of Apple’s astonishing success: simplicity, beauty, elegant design, making the whole widget, focus on very few products, and a deep commitment to the customer experience. He’ll be joined by Phil Schiller to share stories and insights from their decades of launching iconic products.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Featured Session: From Pilot to Payoff: 7 Pattern-Matched Traits of AI Systems That Actually Work
location: Hilton Austin Downtown / Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
description: Most companies are stuck in “pilot purgatory” with AI. What separates the experiments that stall from the projects that scale? Drawing on research across 450+ companies, Sandy Carter reveals the five common traits that turn AI pilots into ROI-driven successes. Walk away with a roadmap you can apply immediately—whether you’re a startup founder or a Fortune 100 executive.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge

title: Game On: The Future of Sports and Gaming Collide
location: Austin Marriott Downtown / Waller Ballroom D
description: The next wave of sports fandom won’t be built in stadiums; it’ll be built in-game. From the NBA’s digital court activations to Fortnite’s immersive events and EA Sports’ evolving cultural platform, the lines between sport and play are blurring fast. This panel explores how gaming is reshaping how fans engage with sport, not just watching, but emulating, remixing, and co-creating it. The future of global sport is interactive, creator-powered, and playable.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Global Experience: What AI Means for Worldwide Communication
description: For the past several years, the global communication industry has quietly lived through the generative AI transformation that many sectors are just now encountering. Now the implications are much bigger than translation. As brands scale across markets, AI is reshaping not just content production but global customer experience. The real challenge is no longer output. It’s coherence. It’s cultural alignment. It’s training data. It’s brand perception at scale. This session explores what generative AI means for global communication in a world where digital may be borderless, but culture is not.
badge: Film & TV Badge, Innovation Badge, Music Badge, Platinum Badge
locationAddress: 111 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Money, Sex & Religion - How Design Turns Taboo to Touchpoint
description: Sex, money, and religion are no longer taboo—they’re trending. This panel explores how behavioral UX, gamification, and human-centered design can destigmatize what used to be off-limits. From intimacy apps to investing platforms, discover how intentional design is helping users navigate vulnerable moments with confidence. Hear from sex educator Shan Boodram, Alinea’s Gen Z fintech founder Anam Lakhani, TechCrunch's Dominic Davis, and Play Ventures’ Phylicia Koh—one of few female investors in gaming VC—on how intentional UX is turning discomfort into discovery.

title: The New Bleachers: World Cup Fandom in the Age of Snapchat & Roblox
description: The future sports fan isn’t discovered in a stadium or in front of a TV, they’re formed in group chats, AR lenses, virtual hangouts, and video games. This panel explores how U.S. Soccer and other forward-thinking sports properties and brands are leveraging platforms like Snapchat and Roblox (home of hit game FIFA Super Soccer) to meet young audiences where they already are.With the 2026 FIFA World Cup set to be the largest tournament in history, the race to build the next generation of fans is already underway. Leaders from U.S. Soccer, Snapchat and Gamefam will unpack how virtual experiences translate into measurable fandom, what metrics actually matter when engaging Gen Z and Gen Alpha, and how sports brands are redesigning the traditional fan funnel for a generation that expects authentic, always-on experiences.
locationAddress: 304 E Cesar Chavez St., Austin, TX 78701

title: Technologies of Care: Ritual, Play, & the Future of Wellness
description: This interactive talk will explore how artist-led, science-backed immersive experiences can foster emotional expression and wellbeing. Award-winning artists behind Explore Deep and Soul Paint, Sarah Ticho (Hatsumi, XR Health Alliance) and Niki Smit (Monobanda) share tools for co-creating rituals of care through creative health design. Participants will explore emotional mapping, embodiment, and cross-sector collaboration to imagine new pathways for impact across health, art, and education.
